title: Bloom Heart
content:
**Tier 1 Leader**
_At the heart of the Bloom, something pulses. It is not a creature — or if it is, it bears no resemblance to any cataloged Etherotaxia. A mass of dense, knotted Creep roughly the size of a wagon, shot through with veins of pale bioluminescence. The tendrils radiate from it. The Madanikuputukas swarm around it protectively. It grows visibly, moment by moment, and with each pulse the Forestdown border lurches another foot outward. The Tenders have never seen anything like this. No one has._
**Motives & Tactics**: Expand Forestdown, defend the Bloom, summon protectors
***
Difficulty: 12
Thresholds: 7/12
HP: 6
Stress: 3
Attack: +0
Weapon: Creep Pulse: Close | 1d8+2 Magical
***
**Features**
**Rooted - Passive**: The Bloom Heart cannot move. It is anchored to the ground. However, it does not need to be spotlighted to use its Reaction features.
**Bloom Expansion - Passive**: At the end of each GM turn, the area of Creep expands. Any PC who was previously outside the Creep's area and is now within it must make an Agility Reaction Roll (11) or mark a Stress from sudden contact with the corruption.
**Call the Swarm - Active**: Spend a Fear. A new [[Madanikuputukas (Swarm)]] emerges from the Bloom Heart and is immediately spotlighted.
**Creep Surge - Reaction**: When the Bloom Heart takes damage, it releases a wave of Creep energy. All creatures within Close range must make an Agility Reaction Roll (12) or take 1d4 magical damage.
**Vulnerability - Passive**: The Bloom Heart takes double damage from fire. If it is destroyed, all Bloom Tendrils in the scene lose their Regenerate feature and the Bloom's expansion halts immediately.

title: Bloom Tendrils
content:
**Tier 1 Horde**
_Thick, ropy tendrils of living Creep that erupt from the ground during a Bloom event. They are not creatures in any conventional sense — they are the forest itself, extending its reach with terrifying speed. Each tendril is as thick as a human arm, dark and fibrous, and moves with a blind grasping urgency. Where they take root, the ground darkens and new undergrowth begins to spread within minutes. Cutting them only slows the advance; the stumps regenerate unless the root mass is destroyed or burned._
**Motives & Tactics**: Spread, root, entangle
***
Difficulty: 10
Thresholds: 4/8
HP: 5
Stress: 1
Attack: -1
Weapon: Lash: Melee | 1d6+1 Physical
***
**Features**
**Horde**: When the Bloom Tendrils have marked half or more of their HP, their Lash deals 2 damage instead.
**Entangle - Reaction**: When a PC moves within Melee range of the Bloom Tendrils, the tendrils attempt to grapple them. The PC must make an Agility Reaction Roll (11) or become Restrained. A Restrained PC cannot move and has Disadvantage on attack rolls until they break free with a Strength Roll (11) on their turn.
**Regenerate - Passive**: At the start of each GM turn, if the Bloom Tendrils have not taken fire damage since the last GM turn, clear 1 HP from their track. Fire damage prevents this regeneration for one full round.

title: Captured Etherotaxic Entity
content:
**Tier 1 Solo**
_A towering, semi-corporeal figure that shifts between forms — now a column of pale light, now a shape that almost resembles something familiar, now a tangle of limbs that reach toward you with what might be curiosity or might be hunger. Unlike the smaller Etherotaxia bound to the Creep, this entity radiates an intelligence that is unmistakable. Its movements are deliberate. It watches. It waits. When it reaches toward you, it is not attacking. It is trying to communicate. The Etherements do not know this, or do not care._
**Motives & Tactics**: Communicate, escape captivity, understand
***
Difficulty: 12
Thresholds: 10/16
HP: 8
Stress: 4
Attack: +2
Weapon: Desperate Lash: Close | 1d8+2 Magical
***
Experience: Whispers of Ichida +3
***
**Features**
**Semi-Corporeal**: This entity takes half damage from physical attacks.
**Relentless (2)**: This adversary can be spotlighted up to 2 times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.
**Resonance - Passive**: PCs within Close range hear fragments of thought — not words, but impressions of emotion, memory, and intent. A PC who succeeds on a Presence or Instinct Roll (12) understands that the entity is frightened and wants to leave, not fight.
**Plaintive Reach - Active**: The entity extends a limb toward a PC. This is not an attack. The PC must make a Presence Reaction Roll (11). On a failure, they flinch and the entity recoils, marking a Stress. On a success, the PC receives a vivid flash of memory — the entity being captured, dragged into the Keep, confined. This grants the PC Advantage on all subsequent Presence rolls to communicate with the entity.
**Cornered Fury - Reaction**: When the entity has marked half or more of its HP, its attacks deal an additional 1d6 magical damage and it no longer attempts to communicate. If a PC has previously succeeded on Resonance or Plaintive Reach, they can spend their turn making a Presence Roll (13) to calm the entity and end Cornered Fury.
**Release - Active**: If the PCs open the chamber door or otherwise create an escape route while the entity is not in Cornered Fury, it departs peacefully. All PCs gain a Hope. The entity pauses at the threshold and projects a final impression — gratitude, and a warning about something deeper in the forest.

title: Creep-Bound Etherotaxic Entity 1
content:
**Tier 1 Standard**
_Any of the numerous body plans of the etherotaxic entities that live in Forestdown. They follow variable body plans and motivations, but like all etherotaxia their motives can be unique to the individual._
**Motives & Tactics**: alter, advance, establish new territory
***
Difficulty: 12
Thresholds: 5/9
HP: 3
Stress: 3
Attack: +1
Weapon: Touch | 3d4 Magical
***
Experience: Whispers of Ichida +2
***
**Features**
**Semi-Corporeal**: Etherotaxic characters take half damage from physical attacks.
**Flickering Displace**: Spend a Fear to move this creature up to Far distance instantly. 
**Creep**: All targets within melee range must succeed against an Agility reaction roll or mark a Stress. Any character that marks two Stress this way gains the _Creep-Touched_ condition. A _Creep-Touched_ creature has disadvantage on all action rolls until the condition is cleared narratively.

title: Creep-Bound Etherotaxic Entity 2
content:
**Tier 2 Standard**
_Any of the numerous body plans of the etherotaxic entities that live in Forestdown. They follow variable body plans and motivations, but like all etherotaxia their motives can be unique to the individual._
**Motives & Tactics**: alter, advance, establish new territory
***
Difficulty: 15
Thresholds: 8/15
HP: 4
Stress: 4
Attack: +2
Weapon: Touch | 6d4 Magical
***
Experience: Whispers of Ichida +3
***
**Features**
**Semi-Corporeal**: Etherotaxic characters take half damage from physical attacks.
**Flickering Displace**: Spend a Fear to move this creature up to Far distance instantly. 
**Creep**: All targets within melee range must succeed against an Agility reaction roll or mark a Stress. Any character that marks two Stress this way gains the _Creep-Touched_ condition. A _Creep-Touched_ creature has disadvantage on all action rolls until the condition is cleared narratively.
**Beckon**: A target within far range must make a Presence Trait Roll (14) or gain the _Beckoned_ condition. A _Beckoned_ target moves immediately towards the adversary which inflicted it with the condition at full speed.

title: Creep-Touched Deaconfish
content:
**Tier 1 Standard**
_A semi-aquatic, lunged beast of burden corrupted by the Creep. Where healthy Deaconfish are docile and nimble despite their bulk, these specimens have grown erratic — their numerous muscled pseudopods twitch independently, their large humped backs are split with dark growths, and they lash out at anything that enters the water near them. Some still bear the remnants of harnesses from when they served the people of Gatehouse._
**Motives & Tactics**: Panic, lash out, flee to deeper water
***
Difficulty: 11
Thresholds: 5/9
HP: 4
Stress: 2
Attack: +1
Weapon: Pseudopod Lash: Close | 1d6+2 Physical
***
**Features**
**Flailing Pseudopods - Passive**: Any creature that enters Melee range of the Creep-Touched Deaconfish for the first time on their turn takes 1d4 physical damage from its uncontrolled thrashing.
**Panicked Dive - Reaction**: When the Deaconfish takes damage that marks its Major threshold, it dives beneath the surface and moves up to Far range. It resurfaces at the start of its next spotlight.
**Remnant Harness**: A PC who succeeds on an Instinct Roll (12) notices the old harness. A subsequent Finesse Roll (13) allows the PC to seize the harness and calm the creature, removing it from combat. A calmed Deaconfish can be used as a mount for the remainder of the scene.

title: Frightened Villagers
content:
**Tier 2 Social**
_Panicked villagers desperately trying to escape, but too scared to sensibly follow directions_
**Motives & Tactics**: Escape, survive, save possessions
***
Difficulty: 13
Thresholds: 6/13
HP: 3
Stress: 4
Attack: -1
Weapon: Trample: Melee | d8+2 Physical
***
**Features**
**Escalating Fear**: Whenever you gain Fear during this encounter, the Frightened Villagers deal an additional d8 damage with their Trample.
**Unruly Mob**: If the Frightened Villagers move through Melee range of a PC, the PC automatically takes damage from their Trample weapon.
**Unreasoning**: If the PCs fail a social interaction to calm the mob, they must mark a Stress. When the mob has caused 3 stress to be marked this way, they must **Flee**.
**Flee**: The Frightened Villagers moves as a group away from the most visible threat at maximum speed.

title: Glowfry School
content:
**Tier 1 Horde**
_A dense, churning school of small luminous fish that swarm just beneath the surface of Ergantine Lake. Each fish is no larger than a hand, but the Creep has fused their schooling instinct into something more aggressive — they move as one, their collective glow shifting from pale blue to angry red when agitated. Their tiny teeth, individually harmless, can strip flesh from bone in moments when the school descends._
**Motives & Tactics**: Swarm, feed, overwhelm
***
Difficulty: 10
Thresholds: 4/9
HP: 6
Stress: 1
Attack: -1
Weapon: Swarming Bite: Melee | 1d8+1 Physical
***
**Features**
**Horde**: When the Glowfry School has marked half or more of their HP, their Swarming Bite deals 3 damage instead.
**Blood Frenzy - Reaction**: When a creature within Close range takes physical damage from any source, the Glowfry School moves to Melee range of that creature and makes a free Swarming Bite attack.
**Luminous Warning - Passive**: The school's glow shifts color to indicate its state. PCs gain +1 to Instinct rolls made to detect hidden creatures in the water while the Glowfry School is visible and calm (not yet in combat).

title: Kuputuka
content:
**Tier 2 Solo**
_A massive, low slung, elongated eight-legged creature. It is semi-corporeal, and considerably more intelligent than its progenitors._
**Motives & Tactics**: Defend, escape, spread creep
***
Difficulty: 15
Thresholds: 13/25
HP: 9
Stress: 3
Attack: +4
Weapon: Crush: Melee | 2d20+3 Magical
***
**Features**
**Semi-Corporeal**: The Kuputuka halves incoming physical damage.
**Force Transform**: Spend a Fear to force a target that is _Creep-Touched_ to develop an abnormal transformation. The target takes 2d6+3 magical damage.
**Disassociation Aura**: Creatures within Close Range must succeed at an Instinct Roll (13) in order to target this creature with an attack.
**Compounding Dread**: When the Kuputuka successfully attacks a player character, gain a Fear.

title: Lanmaoa
content:
**Tier 1 Standard**
_A race of forest dwellers. Pint sized. Known to crawl under doors and through small cracks. Little is known of these, and their appearance is often linked to the early stages of delusions in those affected by the [[Creep]]. This may be due to the intense psychotropic effect on those who spend any amount of time in their presence._
**Motives & Tactics**: Move, Evade, Steal
***
Difficulty: 14
Thresholds: 4/8
HP: 2
Stress: 4
Attack: -1
Weapon: Bite: Melee | 1d4-1 Physical
***
**Features**
**Confusion - Passive**: Any target within Close range of a Lanamoa must make a Presence Roll (14) or become _Confused_. A _Confused_ creature gains Disadvantage on Presence, Instinct and Knowledge trait rolls.
**Hallucination**: Spend a Fear to create a shared hallucination for all targets within Close range of the Lanamoa. A character can dismiss this effect by succeeding in a Presence, Instinct or Knowledge roll against the Lanamoa's difficulty.
**Thieve**: Once per scene, a Lanamoa can steal an item from a _Confused_ target, or a target which is under the effect of a Hallucination.

title: Madanikuputukas (Swarm)
content:
**Tier 1 Horde**
_A swarm of silvery, semi-corporeal creatures arranged in a dihedrally symmetrical pattern. Each of its eight legs and body sections appear identical to one another. It's movements come in quick, unexpected bursts, and where it lingers too long, creep seemingly follows._
**Motives & Tactics**: Investigate, commune, aggregate
***
Difficulty: 10
Thresholds: 5/10
HP: 6
Stress: 2
Attack: -2
Weapon: Pinch: Melee | 1d4 Magical
***
**Features**
**Conglomerate**: When 2 swarms of Madanikuptukas meet, they join and replenish. Clear all HP and Stress. Double their attack (e.g. 1d4 Magical => 2d4 Magical). If a third Madanikuputukas swarm joins in this way, replace the swarm with [[Kuputuka]].
**Creep**: All targets within melee range must succeed against an Agility reaction roll or mark a Stress. Any character that marks two Stress this way gains the _Creep-Touched_ condition. A _Creep-Touched_ creature has disadvantage on all action rolls until the condition is cleared narratively.

title: Quaddadura
content:
**Tier 1 Bruiser**
_A massive, furred, thick-skinned semi-aquatic creature ranging from ten to thirty feet long. Large spade-like teeth surround four prominent hook-shaped eye teeth. Normally a deep-water feeder on lungfish, prolonged Creep exposure has driven some Quaddadura into the shallows where they are far more aggressive than their uncorrupted kin. The fur along their backs has taken on a dark, matted quality, and their eyes glow faintly in low light._
**Motives & Tactics**: Feed, defend territory, surface aggression
***
Difficulty: 11
Thresholds: 7/13
HP: 7
Stress: 2
Attack: +0
Weapon: Bite: Melee | 1d10+2 Physical
		Ram: Close | 1d8+1 Physical
***
**Features**
**Slow**: When you spotlight this adversary and they don't have a token on their stat block, they can't act yet. Place a token on their stat block. When they have a token, clear it and they can act.
**Thrash**: On a successful Bite attack, the Quaddadura shakes its target violently. The target is moved to Melee range if not already and is knocked prone.
**Territorial Bellow - Reaction**: When a PC moves within Close range of the Quaddadura for the first time, it lets out a deafening bellow. The PC must make a Presence Reaction Roll (11) or become Frightened, gaining Disadvantage on attacks against the Quaddadura until the end of their next turn.

title: Skelkandi
content:
**Tier 1 Solo**
_A salamander-like creature of immense proportion, nearly forty yards long, its cavernous mouth wide enough to swallow a Teetle Cart whole. Decades or centuries of exposure to the Creep have left its skin mottled with patches of dark, fibrous growth and its eyes clouded with a milky iridescence. It bears intelligence — it attacked Gatehouse once, timing its assault for when the garrison was depleted. That attack collapsed the northernmost tower of Eskikale Keep._
**Motives & Tactics**: Territorial dominance, patient ambush, destroy threats to its domain
***
Difficulty: 12
Thresholds: 10/16
HP: 9
Stress: 3
Attack: +2
Weapon: Lunge: Close | 1d10+3 Physical
		Tail Sweep: Close | 1d8+1 Physical
***
Experience: Deep Lake Predator +2
***
**Features**
**Submerged Approach - Passive**: The Skelkandi begins any encounter submerged. It cannot be targeted by attacks until it surfaces. It surfaces when it attacks or when a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll (14) to spot the disturbance in the water.
**Capsize**: On a successful Lunge attack against a target on a watercraft, the Skelkandi can attempt to capsize the vessel. All passengers must make an Agility Reaction Roll (12) or be thrown into the water, becoming Vulnerable until their next turn.
**Drag Under**: Spend a Fear. The Skelkandi seizes a target within Melee range and pulls them beneath the surface. The target must make a Strength Roll (13) at the start of each of their turns or take 1d6+2 physical damage and remain submerged. Other PCs must enter the water to assist.
**Creep-Scarred Hide - Passive**: The fibrous Creep growth on the Skelkandi's skin hardens against blows. The first time each scene the Skelkandi takes damage that would mark its Severe threshold, reduce the damage by 4.

title: Trinkanus
content:
**Tier 1 Solo**
_A large, flat-headed cave creature. Possesses two long, thin, whip-like forelimbs segmented at its front. It has a broad, saw-like mandible and several dull crusty eyes. It is of an order of creatures that survive by "ranching," they mind and control populations of another population of creatures carefully, picking off only those that it needs to sustain itself, otherwise providing crude subsistence means for those creatures to survive._
**Motives & Tactics**: Eat, Farm, Survive
***
Difficulty: 12
Thresholds: 10/16
HP: 9
Stress: 3
Attack: +2
Weapon: Pincer: Close | 1d6+1 Physical
		Bite: Melee | 2d8 Physical
***
**Features**
**Grab**: On a successful attack with a Pincer, the Trinkanus can move its target up to Close range.
**Rancher - Passive**: The Trinkanus will not attack creatures it sees as part of its "herd." A creature moved by _Grab_ will not attack unless it's moved.
**Consume**: On a successful attack with a Bite, the Trinkanus can attempt to swallow the target. The target must make a Strength Roll (11) or be swallowed whole. A target who has been swallowed whole cannot act until they are free from the stomach of the Trinkanus.

title: Allonaut
content:
A hard-shelled aquatic race of creatures with wildly varied physiology between members. The Allonauts are intelligent and fiercely loyal, often forming lifelong bonds with [[Ayonik]] partners.

Allonauts are a little known species outside the [[The Land of Tidwell/World Details/Nations & States/The Waste/Varjalune Republic/Varjalune Republic]], as they rarely venture far from their natural habitats. Rarely, an Allonaut will make a name for themselves, but given the vast diversity of species that rise and fall within Tidwell most assume it them to be one of the brief flashes of sentience that emerge amongst all kinds of creatures on the continent.

# Ancestry

**Hard Carapace:** Gain a +2 bonus to your armor score

**Fiercely Loyal**: Mark a Stress to cause an attack against another player to be rolled with Disadvantage

title: Ayonik
content:
A group of stout land dwellers with heavy, oiled coats and large glassy eyes. Loquacious and considered extremely good neighbors, the Ayonik are rarely found away from the [[Allonaut]]s they live in harmony with on the shores of the [[Erelbaad Lake]] in [[Taliga]].

Ayonik bear great resemblance to fat otters, and their cherubic faces and round black eyes are often co-opted by merchants on labels and signage as a short-hand for trustworthiness. However, as anyone who has directly dealt with an enraged Ayonik knows, they are fully capable of extreme violence in defense of their close compatriots and ideals.

# Ancestry

**Adorable**: Spend 2 Hope to gain Advantage on a Presence roll

**They Sleep Holding Hands!**: When using Prepare as a downtime action with one or more members of your party, you each gain 1 additional Hope.

title: Crenshaw
content:
A small ratlike species of characters. Hairless. Large grey eyes and oversized hands. Live for exceptionally long times. Bear the stereotypes that come with longevity and their stunted physiques. This manifests in cultural attitudes towards the Crenshaw as ranging between patronizing tolerance to fetishization of their cultures and traditions.

As a people, they are insular and familial-oriented. Crenshaw often create large, intensely hierarchical societies within larger communities that operate on their own rules whose logic most outsiders consider impenetrable.

# Ancestry

**Experienced:** Mark a Stress to create a temporary Experience. This has the same bonus as your highest bonus experience. This lasts until the end of the Scene.

**Cagey:** Once per long rest, you can mark a Stress instead of taking Minor Damage.

title: Ghotling
content:
Medium-sized species that bears a distinctive hunch to their postures and inordinately long arms. Their pallid grey skin and warty features often cause them to be viewed as nuisances or bad luck, but they are an exceedingly cunning people able to ingratiate themselves to prominent figures within systems of power. 

Their skills as spies, managers and engineers have them often found in places of high employ, though they can just as often be found in criminal gangs or as the heads of vast networks of confidence artists.

# Ancestry

**Long Arms**: Treat any weapon, ability, spell or other feature that has a Melee range as though it has a Very Close range instead.

**Shrewd**: Spend a Hope to gain Advantage on an Instinct trait roll.

title: Goran
content:
One of the native species of Tidwell. Apparently composed of nothing but rocks held together with a fleshy moss-like substance. Little is known about the society or culture of these people, as they are intensely cloistered, but occasionally a foundling will be discovered abandoned on trips to [Rhodia](Rhodia.md). 

When raised in society with other species, Gorans are capable of speech but in a labored and slow manner as their anatomy is obviously unsuited for it normally.

# Ancestry

**Hardened**: Gain +1 bonus to your Armor score and an additional Hit Point at character creation.

**Adaptable Physiology**: You can cause yourself to disassemble into a pile of large rocks. You can use this in combat to make an attack on every target within melee range, treating it as a Strength weapon that deals d8 physical damage using your Proficiency.

title: Hokaw
content:
Birdlike humanoids, crested heads, long arms and short legs, beady eyes that are usually black. Hokaw inhabit an inordinate amount of positions in leadership throughout Tidwell. Their commanding voices and ease of communication naturally lends the Hokaw an air of authority.

Their culture is one that glorifies suicidal altruism. Hokaw culture is replete with stories of heroes (sometimes wantonly) sacrificing themselves for the better of all people. As such, Hokaw routinely find themselves as religious leaders and soldiers.

# Ancestry

**Eagle-Eyed**: You have Advantage on Instinct trait rolls related to sight.

**Commanding Voice**: Once per session, you can mark a Stress to reroll a Presence trait roll with Advantage.

title: Human (Tidwell)
content:
Your bog-standard human, though humans in [Tidwell](Tidwell.md) are rare and are usually regarded as a particularly devious race of people.

# Ancestry

**High Stamina**: Gain an additional Stress Slot at character creation.

**Adaptability**: When you fail a roll that utilized one of your experiences, you can mark a Stress to reroll.

title: Isdaan
content:
A race of fish-like characters that live a semi-aquatic life. Primarily from the [Medilaac](The%20Land%20of%20Tidwell/World%20Details/Nations%20&%20States/Carnithia/Medilaac/Medilaac.md) region, they can be found in almost every city in Tidwell due to their ability to broadly adapt their custom to the societies around them. 

Isdaan are often agrarian when in their own communities. They settle on the banks of streams, rivers, lakes and the sea where they build kelp farms and their nests for spawning. Intensely family-oriented.

Bipedal, with long flat feet and webbed toes, as well as a set of exposed gills at the edge of their jaws, giving them an almost bearded appearance. Thin bodies, often top-heavy, causing them to have a hunched appearance on land. Very graceful and fluid in the water, however.

# Ancestry

**Amphibious**: You can breathe and move naturally underwater.

**Socially Practiced**: When rolling to influence a Character, roll an additional d6. If you roll a 6, add a +5 modifier to your roll and then roll again.

title: Lothrian
content:
Lothrians are one of the rarer of the common species of Tidwell, and are often regarded with some awe on accordance of that fact. Most people within Tidwell will go their entire lives without ever encountering one.

Lothrians are small, jewel-eyed relatives of mollusks, and bear an adaptable body template. A typical Lothrian stands less than a meter at its tallest, and due to their flexible body plan often resemble the societies in which they choose to inhabit.

Lothrians can come both in pygmy and mammoth, with the latter rivaling a [[Human]]'s stature, and the former coming in at no larger than a bread box.

# Ancestry

**Camouflage**: Gain Advantage on rolls to Hide.

**Lucky**: When you spend a Hope, roll a six-sided die. On a 6, you gain a Hope and re-roll this die.

title: Riolan
content:
A species of characters bearing a deep violet skin, and a partial hard carapace of segmented black keratin covering their heads, necks and bellies.

They have six sets of thin armlike appendages with small grasping claws at their ends, and another six sets of legs that are short and stiff, leaving their bellies to rub on the ground while they move.

They wear ornate costumes that mostly cover their upper limbs and backs, often consisting of the hides of predators found throughout Tidwell.

Riolans exchange a base cultural and personal understanding of each other through exchange of cud, which is performed at nearly every meeting between two or more Riolans. This cud contains a microbe load that serves as conveyance of the larger opinions and thoughts of the Riolan species.

Cave Riolans tend to be larger and paler than their City Riolan cousins.

# Ancestry

**Cud-munication**: When interacting with another Riolan within Close range, you may make a Presence roll (12). On a success, you may convey specific information that will propagate to all other characters within Riolan ancestry over a period of a few days.

**Communal**: When you take a downtime action to Tend to Wounds or Tend to All Wounds, you can choose an additional target.

title: Spittletusk
content:
A scaled creature with long necks and slender, needle like tusks that jut from their lower jaws. Large, ridged eyebrows, and thin, delicate limbs. Extremely dexterous and flexible.

This species originally referred to themselves as Shiandrans, but have proudly adopted the pejorative term Spittletusk given to them by less generous societies when they were a new species on Tidwell. Their societies value cunning and depth of consideration more than most, and venerate a nameless trickster god whose mark is two crossed tusks.

Intensely strategic, Spittletusk find great success historically in societies structured around trade, where they are known as shrewd and calculating negotiators.

# Ancestry

**Dexterous**: When you make an Agility roll, you can mark a Stress to gain Advantage on that roll.

**Venomous**: When you succeed on an attack against a target within Melee range, you can spend a Hope to inject a paralytic nerve agent via your tusks, causing the target to become _Restrained_ until the end of the Scene. _Restrained_ characters cannot move unless they succeed a Strength roll (13) or mark a Stress.

title: Æther Engineer
content:
|        Domains        | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Study]] & [[Weird]] |        9         |          7          |
### Class Items
_An arcane device of your own construction_ or _a set of annotated technical schematics_
### Hope Feature
_Overclock_: Spend 3 Hope. Both settings of the Apparatus activate simultaneously this scene. The combined effect is determined by your GM based on the nature of your two settings.
### Class Feature
**The Apparatus**
You carry a unique device of your own design with two settings. Define both settings with your GM at the start of play. One should be Study-oriented (analytical, mechanical, detection-based) and one should be Weird-oriented (supernatural, impossible, energy-based). Once per scene, activate one setting. Both settings use your Spellcast Roll when they require active rolls. If destroyed, the Apparatus can be repaired during a long rest.

### Subclasses
As an Æther Engineer, choose between the following subclasses.

**The Mechanist**
_Play the Mechanist if you want the Apparatus to be a precision instrument: reconfigurable, diagnostic, and consistently reliable under pressure._

**The Channeler**
_Play the Channeler if you want to push the Apparatus past its safe limits and see what it can do when the weird starts talking back._
### Background Questions
_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What inspired the original design of the Apparatus? Was it your idea entirely?
- What has the Apparatus done that you didn't intend, and what did you learn from it?
- Who wants the Apparatus, and what have you done to ensure they can't have it?
### Connections
_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You helped me repair the Apparatus after it was damaged. What had gone wrong?
- I used the Apparatus in a way you disapproved of. What did I do?
- You asked me to explain how the Apparatus works. Did my answer satisfy you?
# Subclasses
## **The Mechanist**
_Play the Mechanist if you want the Apparatus to be a precision instrument: reconfigurable, diagnostic, and consistently reliable under pressure._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Modular Design_: Once per rest, reconfigure one of the Apparatus's two settings. The new setting must remain within the established category (Study-style or Weird-style). The reconfiguration takes a few minutes.

_Diagnostic Mode_: Once per rest, run the Apparatus in a non-active diagnostic mode rather than using a setting. Ask the GM one factual question about the current environment, adversary, or situation. The answer must be truthful.

### Specialization Feature

**Field Modification**: Once per scene, when you use the Apparatus and succeed, add one minor additional effect to the output that you hadn't planned. Work with your GM to keep it consistent with the active setting and reasonably within the device's established capabilities.

### Mastery Feature

**Perfect Calibration**: Once per session, the Apparatus functions flawlessly. Every roll you make using it this session gains +2, and any Critical Success also applies the effect of the second setting for free.
## **The Channeler**
_Play the Channeler if you want to push the Apparatus past its safe limits and see what it can do when the weird starts talking back._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Push the Limits_: Once per rest, when you use a setting on the Apparatus, push it beyond safe parameters. The effect is enhanced: doubled damage, doubled range, or extended duration, as determined by your GM. After the scene ends, mark 2 Stress as the device requires stabilization.

_Supernatural Feedback_: When supernatural or magical phenomena are nearby, the Apparatus vibrates faintly. You always know if there is active supernatural energy within Close range, without a roll.

### Specialization Feature

**Resonance**: Once per scene, when using the Weird setting of the Apparatus, choose to interact resonantly with any other supernatural effect present in the scene. Make a Spellcast Roll (14). On a success, your effect is amplified (+d6 damage or extended duration). On a failure, the other supernatural effect is neutralized instead.

### Mastery Feature

**Critical Discharge**: Once per session, discharge the full capacity of the Apparatus in a single release. This deals 3d10 magic damage using your Proficiency to all targets within Far range, friend and foe alike. If not used for damage, it creates a massive supernatural phenomenon you and your GM design together. After use, the Apparatus requires a full long rest to recharge.

## Mechanical Notes

Define your Apparatus settings clearly and specifically in your session zero. Each setting should do one thing well, not everything passably. The class is built around the interaction between two focused, distinct powers; vague settings produce vague play. The Mechanist path rewards planning and using the device as reliable infrastructure; the Channeler path rewards aggression and willingness to pay costs for outsized effects. The Hope Feature is your designated big moment, so plan for it and save Hope accordingly.

%% armor rec: med %%

title: Banner Saboteur
content:
|           Domains           | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Trickery]] & [[Valiance]] |        9         |          7          |

### Class Items

_A symbol of your clandestine cause_ or _a stack of pamphlets about your cause_

### Hope Feature

_The Meaning Behind It_: Spend 3 Hope. Until the end of the scene, every action you take in service of your cause provides all three benefits from Cover and Cause simultaneously.

### Class Feature

**Cover and Cause**
Once per scene, name the cause you are serving in this moment. When you take any action directly advancing that cause (through deception, sabotage, or open heroism), choose one benefit:

- Gain Advantage on the action's roll.
- An ally within Far range who can see your action gains +2 to their next roll this scene.
- Gain 1 Hope.

### Subclasses

As a Banner Saboteur, choose between the following subclasses.

**The Shadow Patriot**
_Play the Shadow Patriot if you want to fight for something real while working through means no one will ever be able to confirm were yours._

**The Vanguard Deceiver**
_Play the Vanguard Deceiver if you want your courage to be the truth and your deceptions to be the method, with your allies feeling the difference._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What is the cause you serve, and when did you decide it was worth lying for?
- What is the line you won't cross even in service of it?
- Who on the other side knows your real identity, and what prevents them from using it against you?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You know what I actually serve, and you've kept it quiet. Why?
- I lied to you on behalf of the cause. Have you forgiven me?
- You've been asked to vouch for me as cover. Did it cost you anything?

# Subclasses

## **The Shadow Patriot**

_Play the Shadow Patriot if you want to fight for something real while working through means no one will ever be able to confirm were yours._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_The Handler_: Once per rest while operating under a false identity in service of your cause, gain Advantage on all Presence rolls to maintain that identity.

_Operational Security_: When you use Cover and Cause, the action you take cannot be traced back to your real identity unless you choose to let it be.

### Specialization Feature

**Turn the Asset**: Once per scene, identify a GMC currently working for an adversary [[Faction]]. Make a Presence roll (14). On a success, you reveal information that convinces them to take one action against that faction this session. They believe the decision was entirely their own.

### Mastery Feature

**Deep Cover**: Once per session, spend a long rest establishing a complete false identity in a new location or with a new faction. Future rolls to maintain this identity are made with Advantage. The identity persists between sessions until actively dismantled by you or via discovery.

## **The Vanguard Deceiver**

_Play the Vanguard Deceiver if you want your courage to be the truth and your deceptions to be the method, with your allies feeling the difference._

### Spellcast Trait

Strength

### Foundation Features

_Rally by Deception_: When you use Cover and Cause to benefit an ally, they also gain a +1 bonus to their Evasion until the end of the scene.

_Righteous Misdirection_: Once per rest, when an adversary targets an ally instead of you, you can intervene as a reaction and redirect their attention using a taunt or misdirecting action. Make a Presence roll (12). On a success, the interaction redirects to you. Can be used socially or in combat.

### Specialization Feature

**The Cause Leads**: Once per scene, when you directly protect an ally in combat or through social misdirection, gain 1 Hope. If you do so while under a false identity, gain 2 Hope instead.

### Mastery Feature

**Stand and Be Counted**: Once per session, reveal your true identity in a dramatic moment. All adversaries who believed your cover must mark 2 Stress at the revelation. All allies within Far range gain Advantage on their next roll. After this, your cover for this session is burned, but you gain +2 to all rolls for the rest of the session.

## Mechanical Notes

Cover and Cause requires you to name your cause at the scene level. Don't assume it carries over automatically. The clearer you are about what you're serving in this moment, the more easily the GM can confirm when an action qualifies for the benefit. The Shadow Patriot path is for players who want to win through accumulated social manipulation and information control; the Vanguard Deceiver path is for players who want to protect allies through misdirection and tactical self-sacrifice. Both paths benefit from knowing which faction or cause you're advancing before the scene begins.

title: Cantor-Marshal
content:
|         Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Charm]] & [[Faithful]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A vestment, badge, or symbol of an office you hold or claim_ or _a personal affectation that reads as authoritative to anyone who sees it_

### Hope Feature

_The Voice Carries_: Spend 3 Hope. Until the end of the scene, your Sacred Address activates automatically whenever you make a successful Presence roll. The scene use is not consumed.

### Class Feature

**Sacred Address**
Once per scene, when you speak to a group of at least two characters, your words carry both social weight and divine resonance. Choose one effect:

- All characters who hear you gain a +1 bonus to Attitude toward you or toward a cause you name, lasting until the end of the scene.
- All allies within Far range who can hear you clear 1 Stress.
- One adversary who hears you gains the _Shamed_ condition until their next action. _Shamed_ characters gain a -2 penalty to their Difficulty.

### Subclasses

As a Cantor-Marshal, choose between the following subclasses.

**The Congregation's Marshal**
_Play the Congregation's Marshal if you want to lead through loyalty, building the network of people who will move when you speak._

**The Touched Preacher**
_Play the Touched Preacher if you want your words to carry something more than persuasion, something your party will feel when you speak._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- Who first told you that people listened to you differently than they listened to others? Were they right?
- Do you believe what you say, or have you said it long enough that the question doesn't matter anymore?
- What movement or community formed around your words, and how do you feel about what it became?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You've heard me speak when I didn't know anyone was listening. What did I say?
- I convinced you of something once that you later questioned. What was it?
- You've previously met someone who reminded you of me, and it unsettled you. Who were they?

# Subclasses

## **The Congregation's Marshal**

_Play the Congregation's Marshal if you want to lead through loyalty, building the network of people who will move when you speak._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Rally the Faithful_: Once per rest, when you use Sacred Address, you may double its effective range. If you do, the chosen effect (excluding _Shame_) applies to all characters who can hear you, ally or not.

_Commanding Bearing_: Whenever you succeed on a Presence roll with Hope, you may immediately designate one character present who gains a +2 bonus to Attitude toward you until the next scene.

### Specialization Feature

**Standard-Bearer**: Once per scene, when an ally within Far range fails a roll, you can call out to them. They reroll with a +2 bonus added to the result. They must use the new result.

### Mastery Feature

**The March**: Once per session, when you use Sacred Address, choose one goal you describe aloud. Every GMC who heard you will take one meaningful action toward that goal before the end of the session, provided it doesn't require them to act entirely against their nature.

## **The Touched Preacher**

_Play the Touched Preacher if you want your words to carry something more than persuasion, something your party will feel when you speak._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Laying On_: Once per rest, as part of your class feature or as an independent action, touch a willing character. They clear 2 Hit Points or 2 Stress (your choice).

_Marked Flock_: Once per rest, when you use Sacred Address, choose up to two allies within Very Close range. Those allies gain a +1 bonus to all rolls until the end of the scene.

### Specialization Feature

**Channel the Belief**: Once per scene, when an ally within Close range would mark their last Hit Point, spend 2 Hope. They mark a Stress instead, and you clear a Stress.

### Mastery Feature

**Consecrated Ground**: Once per session, designate the current location as sacred. Until the end of the session, allies within Very Close range of that location cannot be affected by fear-based effects as long as they remain within.

## Mechanical Notes

You are strongest in crowd scenes and multi-character social encounters. Position yourself centrally and speak first. Sacred Address before a social roll sets up your whole group for success. You have real power in combat too, but look for moments where the social and tactical overlap (demoralizing an adversary while rallying allies). The Marshal path builds influence that compounds over a session; the Preacher path focuses on keeping your party in fighting shape moment to moment.

title: Confidence Artist
content:
|           Domains           | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Thievery]] & [[Trickery]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A collapsible cane with a hook at the end_ or _an old companion's belonging on a string_

### Hope Feature

_Three Moves Ahead_: Spend 3 Hope. This scene, your pre-arranged condition provides a +2 bonus to all relevant rolls instead of just one.

### Class Feature

**The Setup**
At the start of any scene with a social element or criminal objective, you may establish a pre-arranged condition: something that is, strictly speaking, already true about the situation that only you know. Establish this in one sentence with your GM (e.g., "I planted a document here last night," "I already know the guard rotation," "someone in this room is expecting a payment and thinks it's coming from me"). The established condition gives you a +2 bonus to one relevant roll this scene.

### Subclasses

As a Confidence Artist, choose between the following subclasses.

**The Long Con**
_Play the Long Con if you want every job to be a chapter in something larger by establishing a fiction that outlasts the scene and has a payoff worth waiting for._

**The Quick Mark**
_Play the Quick Mark if you want clean, fast, decisive work and to be well away before anyone starts asking questions._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What was the longest job you've ever run, and was it worth it?
- Who have you taken from who, once you found out more, you wished you hadn't?
- What do you consider the difference between a good job and a bad one?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You've been part of one of my setups without knowing it until it was done. What happened?
- I trusted you with a piece of the setup you were supposed to keep quiet. Did you?
- You've asked me what I actually get out of this, and I told you a lie. What did I say?

# Subclasses

## **The Long Con**

_Play the Long Con if you want every job to be a chapter in something larger, a fiction that outlasts the scene and has a payoff worth waiting for._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Layer by Layer_: Once per session, when you establish a pre-arranged condition, also establish a secondary condition that deepens it. Both conditions can each provide a +2 bonus, but only one applies per roll.

_The Face_: While maintaining a false identity this session, gain Advantage on all Presence rolls made while in character as that identity.

### Specialization Feature

**The Reveal**: Once per session, reveal that a setup you've been running has been considerably deeper than it appeared. Choose one outcome: a character who has been working against you realizes they were manipulated and must mark 3 Stress, or a character previously neutral to you becomes fully cooperative for the rest of the session.

### Mastery Feature

**The Masterwork**: Once per session, if you have sustained a deception for the entire session without it being fully exposed, it becomes permanent. The false identity, the planted evidence, the long game: the GM incorporates it as true until dedicated adversary effort dismantles it.

## **The Quick Mark**

_Play the Quick Mark if you want clean, fast, decisive work and to be well away before anyone starts asking questions._

### Spellcast Trait

Finesse

### Foundation Features

_Immediate Opportunity_: Once per scene, use the pre-arranged condition from The Setup to enable an immediate Thievery action without a roll. The situation is already in your favor. Describe how the setup guarantees basic success. Complex or heavily contested actions may still require rolls at the GM's discretion.
_Cover Story_: After a Thievery action, once per scene, immediately construct a brief false narrative about what just happened. The first character to question you believes the story without a roll.

### Specialization Feature

**In and Out**: Once per scene, after successfully completing a Thievery action, move up to Far range without a roll and gain the _Hidden_ condition.

### Mastery Feature

**The Perfect Crime**: Once per session, declare in advance that a Thievery action this scene cannot fail. No roll is required; you have already planned for everything. Work with your GM on what "successful" means at this level. The target must have been established via **The Setup** in your pre-arranged condition earlier in the session.

## Mechanical Notes

The Setup requires active engagement with your GM at the start of every relevant scene, not retroactively. The more specific and concrete your established condition, the more powerful the bonus. Vague conditions produce vague advantages. The Long Con path rewards patience and multi-session investment; the Quick Mark path rewards clean decisive action and knowing when to stop. In either case, establish the pre-arranged condition as early as possible, as it shapes the whole scene that follows.

title: Creep Enthusiast
content:
|        Domains         | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :--------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Oddity]] & [[Study]] |        9         |          7          |

### Class Items

_A field journal of unexplained encounters_ or _a measuring instrument that measures unmeasurable things_

### Hope Feature

_Cross-Referenced_: Spend 3 Hope. Until the end of the scene, Filed Under Impossible triggers automatically whenever you encounter a new manifestation of the Creep or Etherotaxia, without requiring a roll or action.

### Class Feature

**Filed Under Impossible**
Whenever you encounter a manifestation of the Creep, a haunt, an Etherotaxia specimen, or another phenomenon that defies natural or institutional explanation, make a Knowledge roll to categorize it. On a success, choose one effect:

- **Predict**: Ask the GM one question about what the anomaly will do next. The answer must be truthful.
- **Contextualize**: You've seen something like this before. You and all allies add +2 to the next roll made against or involving this phenomenon.
- **Demystify**: Describe it to nearby characters. Anyone within Close range who heard your explanation becomes immune to non-physical conditions caused by the target.

### Subclasses

As a Creep Enthusiast, choose between the following subclasses.

**Anomalist**
_Play the Anomalist if you want to turn every manifestation of the Creep or Etherotaxia into a weapon or a tool, and remain clear-headed where others falter._

**Exception Theorist**
_Play the Exception Theorist if you want to build a working model of Forestdown's corruption by building a dossier to help explain its mysteries._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What was the first Creep manifestation or Etherotaxia encounter you documented? What did you eventually conclude?
- There is a phenomenon from Forestdown in your journal that you still haven't classified correctly. What is it, and what makes it resist categorization?
- Are you affiliated with the Hygiane Order, or did you come to this work independently? What drew you to studying the corruption?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You once watched me take notes on a haunt or Creep effect during a moment of genuine crisis. Were you impressed or disturbed?
- I explained a Forestdown phenomenon to you in a way that actually made it manageable. What was it, and did it help?
- You've asked me to stop treating dangerous things from Forestdown as data points. I haven't stopped. Why do you keep asking?

# Subclasses

## **Anomalist**

_Play the Anomalist if you want to turn every manifestation of the Creep or Etherotaxia into a weapon or a tool, and remain clear-headed where others falter._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Exploit the Exception_: Once per rest, when a manifestation of the Creep or Etherotaxia is present, spend 1 Hope to redirect its properties in your favor. The GM describes the phenomenon's current effect; you turn it toward a benefit you describe. Gain Advantage on your next roll and trigger the chosen effect.

_Acclimatized_: You are immune to fear and Stress from encountering the Creep, Etherotaxia, haunts, and other corruptions of Forestdown. Allies within Very Close range gain a +1 bonus to rolls to resist the same effects.

### Specialization Feature

**Weaponized Paradox**: Once per scene, invoke the properties of the Creep or Etherotaxia against an adversary connected to or affected by the corruption. Describe the effect and make a Spellcast Roll. On a success, the target marks 2 Stress and gains Disadvantage on their next roll. On a failure, you mark 1 Stress instead.

### Mastery Feature

**Classified**: Once per session, invoke a documented Creep manifestation or Etherotaxia entry from your field catalogue. Choose any such phenomenon you have encountered this campaign or this session. It manifests fully and roughly under your direction. Work with your GM; make it significant and strange.

## **Exception Theorist**

_Play the Exception Theorist if you want to build a working model of Forestdown's corruption by building a dossier to help explain its mysteries._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Build the Model_: When you successfully use Filed Under Impossible, you may forgo the immediate effect. If you do, your next Knowledge roll is made with Advantage and gains +2, representing your accumulated cross-references.

_Predictive Analysis_: When you succeed on a Knowledge roll to analyze something, ask the GM one additional question about that thing's nature or history. The answer must be truthful.

### Specialization Feature

**Working Hypothesis**: Once per scene, declare a theory about how a Creep manifestation or Etherotaxia specimen operates. The GM tells you if you are right, partly right, or wrong. If right, all party rolls to deal with that phenomenon this scene gain Advantage. If partly right, clear 1 Stress and gain a Hope. If wrong, mark 1 Stress.

### Mastery Feature

**The Grand Index**: Once per session, reference your accumulated documentation on the Creep and Etherotaxia of Forestdown. Ask the GM up to three true/false questions about the current scene, encounter, or adversary. The answers must be factual. Use them however you see fit.

## Mechanical Notes

Your class feature rewards active engagement with the Creep, Etherotaxia encounters, haunts, and other Forestdown phenomena your GM presents. The more you lean into noticing and questioning specific manifestations in the fiction, the more Demystify, Predict, and Contextualize can do. The Anomalist path turns what would terrify others directly into offense or cover; the Exception Theorist turns documentation into reliable information advantages — and Build the Model lets you convert a Filed Under Impossible success into a boosted Knowledge roll when you need it most. Both paths get stronger the deeper into Forestdown the situation goes. Lean into the corruption.

title: Devout
content:
|           Domains           | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Artistry]] & [[Faithful]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A sigil of your god_ or _a set of tools for your chosen art_

### Hope Feature

_Perseverance_: Spend 3 Hope to clear 2 Stress on yourself or an ally within Close range.

### Class Feature

**Inspiration**
Once per scene, when you or an ally within Far range makes a roll, you can spend 1 Hope to inspire them. Before the roll, choose one benefit:

- Gain a +2 bonus to the result.
- On a success, clear 1 Stress.
- On a critical success, clear 1 Hit Point.

### Subclasses

As a Devout, choose between the following subclasses.

**Impactful Artist**
_Play the Impactful Artist if you want to influence entire societies by appealing to their entire populace._

**True Believer**
_Play the True Believer if you want to wield your faith to garner yourself immense political power via ruthless cunning._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- Who or what is your deity, and why? What has led you to follow this particular ideology?
- How did you first realize you were able to influence others' thoughts and opinions?
- What do you do in order to show your devotion to your beliefs?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- Do my beliefs bring you comfort or make you uncomfortable? How does that affect our relationship?
- Were you familiar with my chosen artform before we were introduced? What was your opinion of it?
- You've asked me to do a favor for you if you were to not survive, but you're worried I won't follow through. What favor is it and why are you worried?

# Subclasses

## **Impactful Artist**

_Play the Impactful Artist if you want to influence entire societies by appealing to their entire populace._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Paean_: Once per long rest, you can deliver a performance of your most famous work. All allies within Far range can use your Presence score as their own for one roll until the end of the Scene.
_Divine Gift_: After you make an action roll to influence someone, you can spend any number of Hope to add that many d4s to the result of the roll.

### Specialization Feature

**Seminal Works**: Your most influential art has garnered you respect amongst a [[Faction]] of your choosing. Gain a +1 bonus to the [[Attitude]] of all [[Adherent|Adherents]] of that faction.

### Mastery Feature

**Masterpiece of Conviction**: Once per session, spend 2 Hope when you complete a major public performance. Choose up to three allies within Far range. Each ally clears 1 Stress and gains Advantage on their next Presence roll.

## **True Believer**

_Play the True Believer if you want to wield your faith to garner yourself immense political power via ruthless cunning._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Blistering Sermon_: Once per long rest, you can invoke your deity's name in proselytizing to others. All adversaries within Far range take a -1 penalty to their Difficulty.
_Righteous Indignation_: When you make a roll to influence a target within Far who has a negative [[Attitude]] towards you, you can mark any number of Stress. For each 2 Stress marked, the target gains a +1 bonus to [[Attitude]] for your next interaction.

### Specialization Feature

**Polemic**: You have developed the ability to overwhelm others with your personal devotion. Once per long rest, you can give a character the _Stunned_ condition simply by talking to them. While _Stunned_, a character cannot make action or reaction rolls. This condition is cleared only when you stop talking to them.

### Mastery Feature

**Immutable Creed**: When a Character attempts to coerce or magically compel you, mark 1 Stress to ignore that effect and gain 1 Hope. Once per scene, an ally who hears your refusal may also clear 1 Stress.

## Mechanical Notes

Inspiration is flexible support: choose the benefit before the roll lands. The bonus to result is reliable; the Stress-clear option works best when allies are already under pressure; the Hit Point option is worth saving for moments where critical success is genuinely likely. The Impactful Artist path scales with Hope investment. Build your Hope pool intentionally and spend it on the influence rolls that matter most. The True Believer path is strongest when you have multiple adversaries or difficult attitude targets to work against; Blistering Sermon and Righteous Indignation both reward acting before the party's key social rolls rather than reacting to outcomes. Both paths are strongest when you're in reach of your allies and acting first.

title: Dossier Rogue
content:
|         Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Study]] & [[Thievery]] |        9         |          7          |

### Class Items

_A dossier on a target you haven't moved on yet_ or _a set of notes and tools organized with a professionalism that makes it unclear which field they belong to_

### Hope Feature

_Perfect Information_: Spend 3 Hope. You may choose all four options from Advanced Reconnaissance simultaneously.

### Class Feature

**Advanced Reconnaissance**
Once per scene, spend a moment analyzing a target, space, or object (no action during downtime; one action in combat). Choose two of the following:

- Learn one security measure protecting the target or space.
- Learn one item or piece of information the target possesses.
- Gain +2 to all Thievery rolls against this target or within this space for the rest of the scene.
- Identify one character in the scene who may be watching or acting against you.

### Subclasses

As a Dossier Rogue, choose between the following subclasses.

**Profile Artist**
_Play the Profile Artist if you want information to be your primary weapon, where the gap between what you know and what your target knows is the real advantage._

**Ghost Operative**
_Play the Ghost Operative if you want reconnaissance to mean the job is already done before you walk in._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What was the most detailed file you've ever compiled on someone? Did they ever know?
- What did you take that you later discovered you'd entirely misunderstood the value of?
- What made you combine your research skills with this particular line of work?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- I compiled information on you before we met. Do you know what I found?
- You've returned something I took after learning more about what it meant. Have you forgiven me for taking it?
- I've produced information that completely changed your plans. How do you feel about being surprised by me?

# Subclasses

## **Profile Artist**

_Play the Profile Artist if you want information to be your primary weapon, where the gap between what you know and what your target knows is the real advantage._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Full Dossier_: Once per session, for a target you have previously used Advanced Reconnaissance on, ask the GM a yes/no questions about that target. Answers must be truthful.

_Pattern Recognition_: When you succeed on a roll using information gained from Advanced Reconnaissance, your next roll against that same target gains +2. This bonus refreshes each time you succeed against the same target.

### Specialization Feature

**Vulnerability Assessment**: Once per scene, when you have used Advanced Reconnaissance this scene, designate one target as fully assessed. All attack rolls against that target by you or an ally add +2 to the damage roll.

### Mastery Feature

**The Complete Picture**: Once per session, use a short rest downtime move called _Documentation_ to ask the GM three true/false questions about any target, faction, or location you've visited this session. The GM must answer all three truthfully.

## **Ghost Operative**

_Play the Ghost Operative if you want reconnaissance to mean the job is already done before you walk in._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Informed Entry_: When you use Advanced Reconnaissance to learn a security measure, you automatically bypass that specific measure once this scene without a roll.

_Pre-Planned Exit_: Once per rest, before you enter a space, declare a specific exit route. When you take that exit, any successful roll to remain undetected grants you the _Hidden_ condition.

### Specialization Feature

**Clean Extraction**: Once per scene, after successfully acquiring an object or piece of information via thievery, you may ensure the acquisition is not discovered until the start of the next scene. Work with the GM; this doesn't rewrite what happened, only delays awareness of it.

### Mastery Feature

**The Job**: Once per session, when you execute a theft with a full plan (established between you and the GM in advance), treat every roll as having been made with Advantage. The plan must include a target, a method, and an exit. Improvising after the plan begins costs this benefit.

## Mechanical Notes

Your power scales with preparation. Use Advanced Reconnaissance consistently and early, since information gathered before you act is worth far more than information discovered after. The Profile Artist path wins through accumulated intelligence over a session; the Ghost Operative path wins through irreversible, clean execution. In either case, talk to your GM during downtime about upcoming jobs. This class is most satisfying when planning is rewarded.

title: Evangelist
content:
|           Domains           | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Faithful]] & [[Trickery]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A worn stole in the colors of a faith that isn't your own_ or _a small carved idol of something that may be listening_

### Hope Feature

_Divine Misdirection_: Spend 3 Hope. Until the end of the scene, every time you successfully deceive a target using Sacred Deception, you also cause them to clear 1 Stress. They feel briefly at peace — uplifted, even — about what just happened to them.

### Class Feature

**Sacred Deception**
Once per scene, you may frame a lie, misdirection, or manipulative act as a devotional offering to a power you claim to serve. When you do, the deception is bolstered by apparent sincerity: gain Advantage on any roll made as part of this deception, and anyone who would normally be suspicious of you gains a temporary +1 bonus to their [[Attitude]] towards you for the duration of the scene.

### Subclasses

As an Evangelist, choose between the following subclasses.

**Revival Circuit**
_Play the Revival Circuit if you want to work crowds, collect devotion like dues, and leave town before the faithful realize the miracle didn't take._

**Fool God's Envoy**
_Play the Fool God's Envoy if you want your con to carry an unsettling ring of prophecy — the kind of trickster that makes people wonder if something out there is actually using you._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What congregation, order, or flock did you first deceive — and do any of them still believe?
- A mark once showed you genuine faith in something, and it made you feel a way you didn't expect. What happened next?
- Whose divine authority do you invoke most often, and have you ever worried something might actually hear you?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- I performed a miracle for you once. Only one of us knows it wasn't real. Which one is it?
- You've watched me preach to a crowd and caught me believing it for a moment. Did you say anything?
- I promised you divine protection as part of a con. Something went right anyway. What do you think happened?

# Subclasses

## **Revival Circuit**

_Play the Revival Circuit if you want to work crowds, collect devotion like dues, and leave town before the faithful realize the miracle didn't take._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Pass the Burden_: When you use Sacred Deception in front of a crowd or group, its effects extend beyond your primary target. All witnesses within Very Close range have gain a temporary +1 bonus to their [[Attitude]] towards you for the rest of the scene.

_Altar Call_: Once per scene, after a successful Presence roll in a social context, treat the target as having granted you a [[Favor]]. You may call in this Favor at any point this session to make one reasonable request of that character; at the GM's discretion, the request is fulfilled. If the request is denied, you retain the Favor.

### Specialization Feature

**The Testimony**: Once per scene, publicly deliver a false account of your own divine encounter or personal transformation. Make a Presence roll against each target within Far range. On a success, each target has their Attitude toward you improve by 1 and is disinclined to question your motives for the rest of the scene. On a success with Hope, one character of your choice is also compelled to corroborate your account if asked.

### Mastery Feature

**Tent Revival**: Once per session, hold court as a preacher or prophet over a scene. Make a Presence roll against a Difficulty set by the GM based on the crowd's disposition. On a success, every character present will take one significant action you direct, believing it to be either their own conviction or a genuine divine instruction, provided that action does not require self-harm. On a success with Hope, you may also name one character in the crowd who is moved to become a persistent sympathizer for the rest of the session.

## **The Fool God's Envoy**

_Play the Fool God's Envoy if you want your con to carry an unsettling ring of prophecy — the kind of trickster that makes people wonder if something out there is actually using you._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Hallowed Mischief_: Once per rest, when you activate an effect from a Trickery domain card, describe a small, strange unintended side effect — something that wasn't part of your plan. The GM incorporates it into the scene. It may be inconvenient, useful, or simply inexplicable. If you do so, clear a Stress.

_Divine Confusion_: Once per scene, cause a Character to become confused about their own intentions. They must spend their next action reconsidering what they're doing rather than acting.

### Specialization Feature

**Oracle of the Absurd**: Once per scene, deliver a prophecy or divine proclamation to a target. Make an Instinct roll. On a success, name one specific thing that will occur before the end of the scene; the GM incorporates it as true. On a failure, the proclamation unsettles the target anyway — they mark 1 Stress and are distracted for the duration of the scene.

### Mastery Feature

**The Actual Miracle**: Once per session, something you faked or performed as misdirection turns out to be real. Describe the false miracle; the GM tells you what actually happened. It may be more, less, or differently wondrous than you intended, but it is genuine.

## Mechanical Notes

Use Sacred Deception early in a scene as the Attitude bonus shapes the whole interaction that follows, not just a single roll. The Revival Circuit is built around Presence rolls and crowds: Pass the Burden extends your deception's goodwill to everyone watching, so prioritize using Sacred Deception when there's an audience. Bank Favors from Altar Call rather than spending them immediately; a Favor held until a critical moment is worth far more than a quick ask. The Testimony is your mid-scene escalation. With it, a single Presence roll that can shift the Attitude of an entire room, and a success with Hope locks in a corroborator. Tent Revival is your session-defining move. If you set it up with prior deceptions so the crowd is already warmed up, you can see some extremely powerful results from its use. The Fool God's Envoy rewards leaning into the chaos of your own abilities: trigger Hallowed Mischief on every Trickery card activation to accumulate Stress clears, and let the GM's side effects complicate the fiction in ways you can exploit. Oracle of the Absurd is most powerful when you make a bold, specific claim and a successful Instinct roll lets you author one true fact about the scene, so name something tactically useful. The Actual Miracle is strongest when the false miracle you performed was something genuinely ambitious.

title: Herder
content:
|                       Domains                       | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Charm]] & [[Creature]] |        9         |          7          |
### Class Items
_A stick with a crook at its end_ or _a well-oiled and thick animal hide_
### Hope Feature
_Old Bushman's Trick_: Make a Spellcast Roll against a [[Creature]], on a success give the creature the _Calm_ condition. A _Calm_ creature will not attack the party even if it had previously been aggressive. This condition is cleared when the creature is next attacked or the scene ends, whichever comes first.
### Class Feature
**Creature Companion**
You gain a special downtime project called _Lure_ with a Countdown Tracker of 3. When you complete this project, you gain a unique item called a _Herder's Lure_. You can use this item to attempt to befriend a creature you encounter in Tidwell. This creature follows the rules provided for [[Ranger Companions]].

### Subclasses
As a Herder, choose between the following subclasses.

**Animist**
_Play the Animist to embrace your animalistic side and serve as a bridge between Characters and Creatures._

**Creature Wrangler**
_Choose Creature Wrangler to use your will to orchestrate the creatures around you to enforce your will._
### Background Questions
_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- Do you have a formative experience with a creature from Tidwell? How did it change you?
- Where do you spend your time when you're relaxing? What about that place soothes you?
- Have you lost a person or a companion who was close to you? Who were they?
### Connections
_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- Does my affinity for creatures change your opinion of me at all? In what way?
- We share a common connection with a creature. What is it, and how do you feel about that?
- You knew of me before you knew me. What was my reputation, and did you believe it?
# Subclasses
## **Animist**
_Play the Animist to embrace your animalistic side and serve as a bridge between Characters and Creatures._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Animal Intuition_: You can spend a Hope to key into your intuitive understanding of the world. If you are within Close range of a character or creature that intends to harm you, you pick up on subtle clues invisible to others and can anticipate this before it happens.
_Creature Ambassador_: Animals have an [[Attitude]] score towards you that can be affected in the same way as Characters.

### Specialization Feature

**Tandem Strikes**: Once per rest, you can make a tag team attack with your _Creature Companion_. This does not cost Hope as a normal tag team attack would.

### Mastery Feature

**Elder Pack Bond**: Your [[Creature Companion]] gains +1 Evasion and can clear 1 Stress whenever you clear Stress. Once per session, either you or your companion can each immediately move within Close range of the other without a roll.
## **Creature Wrangler**
_Choose Creature Wrangler to use your will to orchestrate the creatures around you to enforce your will._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Stern Correction_: Once per rest when you use your Class Feature, you can issue a harsh rebuke to the creature and cause it to flee. A creature that has fled in this manner will not return until your next rest.
_Dominant Energy_: Make a Spellcast Roll against a creature within Close range. On a success, you can spend a Hope to issue a simple command to a creature that it will follow to the best of its ability, even if it would be out of its nature to do that thing. This effect ends when the creature completes this task or it takes damage.

### Specialization Feature

**Culling the Herd**: Once per rest when you receive damage from a Creature, you can give it the _Marked for Destruction_ condition. When you deal damage to a creature with _Marked for Destruction_, roll your damage dice again and add the result to the damage dealt. This condition is cleared when the creature dies or your next rest.

### Mastery Feature

**Apex Handler**: Once per scene, when a [[Creature]] follows your command from _Dominant Energy_, you can spend 1 Hope to extend that command for the rest of the Scene. The creature gains Advantage on one roll made while carrying out that command.

## Mechanical Notes

Befriending a companion is a long-term investment: complete _Lure_ during downtime between significant scenes and treat your companion as a persistent resource, not an occasional tool. The Animist path connects your social and combat work to your companion directly. Your companion's Attitude system means building relationships with it actively, not just issuing commands. Tandem Strikes is your most reliable action-economy play; use it when you need an extra action without spending Hope. The Creature Wrangler path is about opportunistic control: apply Calm or Dominant Energy on creatures that are complications rather than primary threats, and use Culling the Herd on the most dangerous target to shift burst damage dramatically.
