title: Hex Raider
content:
|         Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Thievery]] & [[Weird]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A cursed item you haven't sold_ or _a small round object worn smooth from constant fidgeting_

### Hope Feature

_The Prize I Kept_: Spend 3 Hope. Choose two effects from Cursed Procurement this scene instead of one.

### Class Feature

**Cursed Procurement**
You carry a collection of supernaturally-charged items recovered through your work. Once per scene, invoke one to gain one effect:

- **Spectral Transit**: Step through a wall, closed door, or barrier that is not made of stone or magically warded material.
- **Unbidden Activation**: Force _all_ characters within Close range to mark a Stress or become _Stunned_ for one turn as the item activates unexpectedly.
- **Ward the Body**: Gain a bonus to your Evasion equal to your Proficiency until the end of the scene.

### Subclasses

As a Hex Raider, choose between the following subclasses.

**Relic-Marked**
_Play the Relic-Marked if you want the things you've collected to change you and to wield that accumulated strangeness like an arsenal._

**Midnight Acquisitor**
_Play the Midnight Acquisitor if you want the weird to be a professional tool and nothing more: reliable, controlled, and used cleanly._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What is the most dangerous item in your collection, and why haven't you sold it?
- One of your items has been trying to tell you something. What do you think it wants?
- What was the job that first made you realize these things have a cost?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You've noticed something about me that's changed since I started this work. What is it?
- One of my items reacted to you in a way I didn't expect. What happened?
- I've refused to steal something you wanted. What was it, and why do you think I refused?

# Subclasses

## **Relic-Marked**

_Play the Relic-Marked if you want the things you've collected to change you and to wield that accumulated strangeness like an arsenal._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Resonant Collection_: When you use Cursed Procurement, you may choose to have the effect emanate from a specific named item you describe. This gives the GM license to develop that item as an ongoing story element that may begin responding to events or attracting attention.

_Warded Skin_: Once per rest, before taking damage, expend the active charge of one item. Reduce that damage by d6. The item is temporarily drained and cannot be used again until your next long rest.

### Specialization Feature

**The Gathering**: Once per scene, spend 2 Hope. All supernatural effects in the current area (curses, wards, active Weird domain card effects) are briefly drawn toward you. Until the end of the scene, you are immune to their effects and every effect that targets you in this manner provides a +2 bonus to your Spellcast Rolls. This effect can stack.

### Mastery Feature

**The Marked's Inheritance**: Once per session, invoke the combined weight of everything you've ever carried. For the rest of the scene, all three effects of Cursed Procurement are active simultaneously. You carry the resonant weight of every cursed thing you've touched.

## **The Midnight Acquisitor**

_Play the Midnight Acquisitor if you want the weird to be a professional tool and nothing more: reliable, controlled, and used cleanly._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Enabled Access_: When using Spectral Transit from your class feature, you do not alert wards, alarms, or detection effects when entering a space.

_Known Risk_: Once per rest, when you are about to make a Thievery roll, describe one risk you've already accounted for using your supernatural items. That specific risk cannot be the source of failure on this roll.

### Specialization Feature

**Silent Approach**: Once per scene, when you use Spectral Transit from your class feature, you may take one willing ally within Very Close range with you.

### Mastery Feature

**The Perfect Take**: Once per session, when you use Cursed Procurement to enter a secured space, you may take anything in that space not on a Character's person without a roll, provided you have a few uninterrupted moments. Describe what you take and how the supernatural access made it possible.

## Mechanical Notes

Engage with the narrative of your collection. Give individual items names and histories and describe them specifically when you invoke them. The more present they are in the fiction, the more the GM can build situations around them. The Relic-Marked path treats the collection as a growing and changing supernatural inheritance; the Midnight Acquisitor uses it as reliable professional equipment. In either case, the Hope Feature is your big-moment enabler, so save it for scenes where you genuinely need both effects.

title: Hypnomancer
content:
|        Domains        | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Charm]] & [[Weird]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A series of interesting rocks and shells on a string_ or _a wickedly carved animal skull_

### Hope Feature

_Hex_: Spend 3 Hope to give an adversary a penalty to their next roll equal to your Spellcast Trait.

### Class Feature

**Invader of Dreams**
Once per session, on a successful Spellcast Roll against a target with whom you have talked in the past day, infiltrate their dreams to gain specific of information about the target's inner thoughts, feelings and plans.

**Sleepwalkers**: Sleepwalkers follow your movements to the best of their abilities. When you enter into combat, Sleepwalkers will converge on a target you set for them.

Roll attacks for your Sleepwalkers as a group. Make a Knowledge roll for them. On a success, deal 2d6 damage for every Sleepwalker under your control, and they immediately are destroyed. If your Sleepwalkers are targeted, use your Evasion as their own. If the attack is successful, remove a Sleepwalker for every point 2 points of damage.

### Subclasses

As a Hypnomancer, choose between the following subclasses.

**Deathwarden**
_Choose the Deathwarden if you want to play a mysterious and powerful enchanter who can turn their adversaries into allies with a little forceful coercion._

**Social Enchanter**
_Choose the Social Enchanter if you want to play a charming social climber who's not above a little direct manipulation to achieve their goals._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- Did you find your curse or did you ask for it? What was life like before you had it?
- There's a time you regretted using your abilities. What did you do and why did you do it?
- What do you enjoy most about being a Hypnomancer, and what do you enjoy least?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- I've invaded your dreams before, but you caught me. How, and what was I looking for?
- Since we first met, you've held a belief about the morals of my abilities. What is that belief?
- There's a story about someone's dreams I've only shared with you. What is it, and why do we keep coming back to talk about it?

# Subclasses

## **Deathwarden**

_Choose the Deathwarden if you want to play a mysterious and powerful enchanter who can turn their adversaries into allies with a little forceful coercion._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Somnambulist_: Whenever you take a rest, you automatically _Prepare_. This does not count towards your normal budget of downtime moves.
_Endless Supply of Friends_: When you down an adversary, you can spend 2 Hope to immediately raise them as a Sleepwalker. You may have any number of Sleepwalkers active. Each Sleepwalker follows you until you dismiss them, they attack, or they are removed.

### Specialization Feature

**Rising Dreams**: Once per scene you can make a Spellcast Roll (16). On a Success, call forth a creature from a dream. Choose one of the following options:

- _From My Own Dreams_: You summon a group of 5 [[Sleepwalker|Sleepwalkers]].
- _From a Captured Dream:_ You summon an apparition you stole from a target's dream. Describe this apparition how you like, but treat it like a group of 5 [[Sleepwalker|Sleepwalkers]]. If this apparition attacks the dreamer that conjured it, treat any success as a critical success, and any failure as a Success with Fear.

### Mastery Feature

**Dream Legion**: Once per session, when you summon [[Sleepwalker|Sleepwalkers]], add one additional Sleepwalker for every 2 Hope you currently hold (max +3).

## **Social Enchanter**

_Choose the Social Enchanter if you want to play a charming social climber who's not above a little direct manipulation to achieve their goals._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_In the Know_: You can divine information from first impressions. Make Presence rolls when investigating a character, on a success you learn a bit of history about any [[Character|Characters]] involved in the investigation.

_Charmed, I'm Sure_: You can spend a Hope to gain a temporary +1 bonus to the [[Attitude]] of a character with whom you're interacting. You cannot increase a character's attitude of you beyond +2 via this ability.

### Specialization Feature

**Puppets on a String**: When you make a Spellcast Roll against a character with a +0 or better Attitude, you can spend a Hope to gain one of the following benefits:

- _Enchanted_: Give the target the _Enchanted_ condition. _Enchanted_ characters treat any request as if it were made with a [[Favor]]. After the request is fulfilled, the condition is cleared.
- _Fearful_: The target moves at maximum speed away from you and towards the nearest place they feel safe. This condition is cleared only when they can no longer sense you.
- _Abashed_: Give the character the _Abashed_ condition. All rolls to influence this character are made with Advantage. This condition clears on your next rest or until you give another character the _Abashed_ condition, whichever is first.

### Mastery Feature

**Commanding Presence**: Once per scene, when you apply _Enchanted_, _Fearful_, or _Abashed_, you can also move that target up to Close range in any direction that does not directly cause damage.

## Mechanical Notes

Invader of Dreams is once per session and requires prior conversation, so set it up deliberately — use downtime or early scene dialogue to qualify a target before the situation becomes urgent. The Deathwarden path builds a persistent force: raise Sleepwalkers opportunistically on downed adversaries, then use Rising Dreams when you need a concentrated push. Dream Legion pays out best when you've been banking Hope rather than spending it liberally. The Social Enchanter path is sustained condition control: build Attitude with Charmed, I'm Sure before applying Puppets on a String, and choose your condition based on what the scene needs — Enchanted for compliance, Fearful for removal, Abashed for a party-wide social advantage. Commanding Presence lets you combine the condition with repositioning, which is most valuable when you need a target away from an ally or into a specific spot.

title: Intercessor
content:
|         Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Faithful]] & [[Study]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A field journal covered in both calculations and prayers_ or _a set of instruments that could belong to a healer or a researcher depending on who's asking_

### Hope Feature

_Empirical Grace_: Spend 3 Hope. Apply both effects of Sacred Methodology simultaneously: the mechanical insight and the divine authority activate together.

### Class Feature

**Sacred Methodology**
Once per scene, when you observe a phenomenon that could be either divine or explicable (a miracle, a curse, a strange occurrence), make a Knowledge roll. On a success, choose one effect:

- **Analyze**: Determine the mechanical nature of the phenomenon and share it with your party, granting all allies Advantage on the next roll to counter, navigate, or neutralize it.
- **Consecrate**: Interpret the phenomenon as sacred, invoking divine authority. One [[Character]] present treats you as a religious authority for the rest of the scene, granting a +2 bonus to their Attitude toward you and a willingness to follow one reasonable instruction.

### Subclasses

As an Intercessor, choose between the following subclasses.

**Annotated Theologian**
_Play the Annotated Theologian if you want to build a systematic understanding of sacred phenomena and turn that understanding into irreversible advantage._

**Field Priest**
_Play the Field Priest if you want your scholarly grounding to make your divine work more precise, more durable, and harder to dismiss._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What is the question at the intersection of faith and reason that you still haven't resolved?
- What did you observe that your religious order told you to document differently than you saw it?
- Who taught you that belief and evidence were not always in conflict?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You've asked me to explain something I did that looked like a miracle. What did I tell you?
- I've been more critical of your beliefs than most would dare. Have you thanked me for it?
- We've disagreed about whether something was divine or explicable. Who was right? And does it matter?

# Subclasses

## **Annotated Theologian**

_Play the Annotated Theologian if you want to build a systematic understanding of sacred phenomena and turn that understanding into irreversible advantage._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Field Notes_: Once per rest, when you use Sacred Methodology, record the result. On subsequent uses this session against the same type of phenomenon, your roll gains a +2 bonus.

_Doctrinal Clarity_: When you succeed on a Knowledge roll, you may ask the GM one yes/no question about the topic at hand. The answer must be truthful.

### Specialization Feature

**Contradicts the Canon**: Once per scene, when you use Sacred Methodology, you may instead declare both available effects invalid and describe what the phenomenon actually is. Work with the GM to make this a true detail of the world unless it directly contradicts established lore.

### Mastery Feature

**The Grand Synthesis**: Once per session, during a rest, write up your findings on a category of phenomenon. Until the end of the session, all rolls by you or your party to deal with, counter, or understand phenomena of that type gain Advantage.

## **The Field Priest**

_Play the Field Priest if you want your scholarly grounding to make your divine work more precise, more durable, and harder to dismiss._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Informed Ministry_: When you use Sacred Methodology to invoke divine authority, you may also have the character clear 1 Stress as part of the same action.

_Practical Blessing_: Once per rest, when you successfully consecrate a phenomenon using your class feature, choose one ally. They gain a +1 bonus to all rolls until the end of the scene.

### Specialization Feature

**Rites of Passage**: Once per scene, perform a brief ceremony for a willing character. They receive one of the following: clear 2 Hit Points, clear 2 Stress, or clear one current condition.

### Mastery Feature

**Divine Sanction**: Once per session, when you invoke divine authority using Sacred Methodology, you have the full organizational backing of a religious faction you are affiliated with. They will send aid, provide resources, or speak on your behalf in one meaningful way before the end of the session.

## Mechanical Notes

Your class feature rewards paying attention to the details your GM presents. The more you engage with specific events and phenomena in the fiction, the more clearly you can choose between the two effects. Save Hope for moments when you need both at once. The Theologian path builds information advantages that compound; the Priest path keeps your party functional under pressure. Both paths reward active curiosity and consistent engagement with the sacred and the strange.

%% armor rec: med %%

title: Knave
content:
|         Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Charm]] & [[Violence]] |        9         |          7          |

### Class Items

_An intricate piece of headwear_ or _an aristocratic dress item_

### Hope Feature

_Roguish Charm_: Spend 3 Hope to double your Presence on your next roll.

### Class Feature

**Criminal Connections**
Once per session, you can call on your contacts in a local criminal organization that you know. Choose one of the following options when you do:

- _Item Drop_: Deliver a mundane item to a location of your choosing
- _Make a Ruckus:_ A flurry of activity happens at a time and place you designate. [[Character|Characters]] caught up in this take a -2 Penalty to their Difficulty
- _Hideout:_ You and your party can go to ground with cover from the organization. Anyone pursuing you will temporarily lose your trail. Treat this time as a long rest.
  If you don't know any criminal organizations in your region, you can undertake a special downtime project called _Making Contacts_ with a Countdown Tracker of 2. On completion of this project, you are introduced to a local criminal element.

### Subclasses

As a Knave, choose between the following subclasses.

**Enforcer**
_Play the Enforcer if you want to play a strong, willful character who uses their actions to back up their words._

**Pretty Face**
_Play the Pretty Face if you want to play a character whose worst crimes are far overshadowed by their legendary charisma._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- How did I end up in this life? Was there a point where I could have made a different choice?
- What is the worst trouble I've been in, and how did I get out of it?
- Who have I had to leave behind in order to live the way I do now?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- I put you in a situation where you had no choice but to lie. What was it and how did you feel about me afterwards?
- As long as I've known you, we've had what I consider a friendly rivalry. Do you agree, or do you secretly resent me?
- I put on a very polished front, but you always see right through it. What do you know about me that makes you immune to my charm?

# Subclasses

## **Enforcer**

_Play the Enforcer if you want to play a strong, willful character who uses their actions to back up their words._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Strong but Silent_: When you choose to influence a character without words, spend 2 Hope to add your Strength score as a bonus to the Presence roll.
_Sending a Message_: After you down an adversary, you can spend a Hope to gain a +3 bonus per Level to your next roll to intimidate a character.

### Specialization Feature

**Crusher**: After a successful hit on a target within Melee range of you, spend a Hope to give the target the _Crushed_ condition. While _Crushed_, the target cannot move and attacks made against a _Crushed_ adversary have Advantage. This condition ends when the target expires or you move beyond Melee range.

### Mastery Feature

**Underworld Reputation**: When you use **Criminal Connections**, choose two options instead of one. If either option directly harms an organized rival, gain 1 Hope.

## **Pretty Face**

_Play the Pretty Face if you want to play a character whose worst crimes are far overshadowed by their legendary charisma._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Sharp Comeback_: If you fail a roll with Hope to influence a character, you can mark a Stress to immediately attack them. This attack is made with Advantage and, if it hits, you roll your damage dice again and add the result to the damage dealt.
_Wink and a Smile_: When you attempt to deceive a character, you can spend a Hope to gain Advantage on the roll.

### Specialization Feature

**Don't Fall in Love**: When you influence a character, you can spend a hope to roll a d6. On a 6, gain a +1 permanent bonus to that character's [[Attitude]] towards you.

### Mastery Feature

**Deadly Charm**: Once per scene, when you succeed on a Presence roll to influence a Character, you can immediately make an attack against that Character with Advantage. Treat any success as a Critical Success.

## Mechanical Notes

Criminal Connections is once per session, so don't save it for a perfect moment that never arrives. Use it at the first significant obstacle where it would meaningfully change the situation. The Enforcer path builds slowly: collect trophies through play, then deploy your intimidation bonus when you need to flip an adversary's behavior quickly. Crusher costs Hope but shuts down mobility; use it on high-priority targets you want to hold in place. The Pretty Face path converts social momentum directly into attacks: use Wink and Smile to set up advantage, then follow with Deadly Charm when a Presence success opens the window. Sharp Comeback turns a social failure into an offensive opportunity, so lean into it rather than treating failures as dead turns.

title: Mosaic Vessel
content:
|          Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Artistry]] & [[Oddity]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_An animated instrument that has its own motivations_ or _a tool given to you by a mythical creature_

### Hope Feature

_Stolen Palette_: Spend 3 Hope. Until the end of the scene, your artistic manifestations can mimic the behavior of any single creature or character you have observed this session.

### Class Feature

**Living Canvas**
Once per scene, when you perform an artistic act or use a Domain Card with a creative or social component, declare a brief reality distortion as part of it. A small manifestation of your art exists in the world, no larger than yourself, lasting until the end of your next turn. The GM determines what it can physically interact with. Spend 1 Hope to make it linger until the end of the scene instead.

### Subclasses

As a Mosaic Vessel, choose between the following subclasses.

**Waking Dream**
_Play the Waking Dream if you want your creations to press against reality until it gives way to see what comes through._

**Grand Performer**
_Play the Grand Performer if you want your art to move rooms, shape crowds, and leave people uncertain what they just witnessed._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- When did you first realize your art wasn't staying where you left it?
- What is your preferred medium, and what happens when you push it too far?
- Something you made once came back to find you. What was it?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You were there when I made something I didn't mean to. What did you see?
- I tried to make something that looked like you. Did it capture something real?
- Something I made once unsettled you. Do you think I meant it to?

# Subclasses

## **Waking Dream**

_Play the Waking Dream if you want your creations to press against reality until it gives way to see what comes through._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Bleed-Through_: Once per rest, the residue of an artistic act you performed creates a distraction or sensory disturbance within Far range. Make a Spellcast Roll (13). On a success, all [[Character|Characters]] in the area must mark a Stress to take any action until the end of their next turn unless they leave the area.

_Persistent Impression_: Your manifestations remember what you showed them. When you activate your class feature, you choose one thing your manifestation cannot do rather than the GM choosing its limitations.

### Specialization Feature

**Self-Sustaining Work**: Once per scene, after you activate your class feature, your manifestation can take one independent action of your choice without further input from you, as if it were a basic adversary with your Proficiency.

### Mastery Feature

**The Piece Completes Itself**: Once per session, when your manifestation would dissipate, spend 2 Hope. It persists until the end of the session, acting on your behalf during scenes you describe to it. The GM can spend 2 Fear to cause it to deviate from your intent.

## **Grand Performer**

_Play the Grand Performer if you want your art to move rooms, shape crowds, and leave people uncertain what they just witnessed._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Genuine Reaction_: When you use your class feature during a social scene, the reality distortion works in your favor. Characters who observe the manifestation treat you with a +1 bonus to Attitude for the rest of the scene.

_Crowd Turned_: Once per rest, when you address more than one character at once, spend 1 Hope. One character of your choice who witnessed your address must mark a Stress before taking any action against you or an ally this scene.

### Specialization Feature

**Center Stage**: When you perform a social action and succeed, you can immediately trigger your class feature as part of it. This does not cost Hope and the manifestation lasts until the end of the scene.

### Mastery Feature

**Unforgettable Work**: Once per session, when you succeed on a Presence roll, every character who witnessed your performance gains a permanent +1 bonus to Attitude toward you (until a meaningful betrayal). Additionally, your next class feature manifestation can affect an area up to Far range.

## Mechanical Notes

Your manifestations are intentionally open-ended. Talk with your GM before the session about what they can and can't interact with physically. Don't try to engineer perfect mechanical outcomes; let the weirdness create openings. Save Hope for the moments when you want a manifestation to stick around or do something consequential. The Waking Dream path rewards patience; the Grand Performer path rewards working the room before anything goes wrong.

title: Naturalist
content:
|           Domains           | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Artistry]] & [[Creature]] |        9         |          7          |

### Class Items

_A piece of hide or hair from an animal_ or _a piece of carved wood or bone_

### Hope Feature

_Natural Balance_: Spend 3 Hope and until the end of the scene you may use your Presence and Instinct in place of one another until the end of the scene.

### Class Feature

**Keen Senses**
Spend a Hope when you make an Instinct roll to investigate. On a success, learn the normal details you otherwise would, plus gain one of the following details or benefits:

- How recently [[Character|Character(s)]] or [[Creature|Creature(s)]] last touched or interacted with the object of your investigation
- General details about the species of those character(s) or creature(s) (e.g. They were aquatic, mammals, arthropods, etc)
- A +1 bonus to the [[Attitude]] of characters witnessing you investigate

### Subclasses

As a Naturalist, choose between the following subclasses.

**Feral Wonder**
_Play the Feral Wonder if you want to lean into your creature aspects and be a deadly and efficient operator in the natural world._

**Practiced Observer**
_Play the Practiced Observer if you want to use your animalistic traits to bolster your impressive influential abilities._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- When did you first notice you were built differently from most of your peers? How did that affect you?
- Has your appearance changed? What are you becoming, and why?
- Where is your favorite place in the wilds? What makes it special?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- What have I done in your presence that you would consider strange? Did you tell me it was strange?
- What was our first meeting like, and why did you decide you liked me?
- There's a secret I told you in confidence. What was it, and have you kept it?

# Subclasses

## **Feral Wonder**

_Play the Feral Wonder if you want to lean into your creature aspects and be a deadly and efficient operator in the natural world._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Natural Weapons_: When you make an unarmed attack, treat it as if you had a corresponding weapon of your current Tier. You may mark a Stress to make a second attack immediately after your first.
_Survivalist_: During a rest, roll a number of d6 using your Proficiency, and store the results. You can add the results to any Instinct roll made by you or an ally. Clear unused dice at the start of your next rest.

### Specialization Feature

**At Home In The Wild**: You become unremarkable to all but the most territorial [[Creature|Creatures]] in Tidwell. Once per rest, you can activate this feature. When you do, for the rest of the scene most creatures will ignore you unless you interact with them directly.

### Mastery Feature

**Apex Form**: Once per scene, spend 1 Hope to gain Advantage on all unarmed attacks until the end of your next turn. During this time, you also ignore difficult terrain.

## **Practiced Observer**

_Play the Practiced Observer if you want to use your animalistic traits to bolster your impressive influential abilities._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Sniff it Out_: After you use _Keen Senses_, you can spend a Hope to determine the direction characters or creatures identified via this manner have gone. When you arrive at the destination, you can use your Class Feature again without spending a Hope.
_Jumpy_: Unless a creature with the _Hidden_ condition attacks you or your party first, you can choose to start combat with the spotlight. If you do so, you can move up to Far range this turn without making a roll.

### Specialization Feature

**Strive to Stay Alive**: When you fail an Instinct roll with Fear, you can mark 2 Stress to add your Agility, Finesse or Strength score to the total of your Hope die. If this changes the result to a failure with Hope, you gain a Hope as you would normally. If this changes the result to a success with Fear, clear a Stress. If it changes the result to a success with Hope, treat it as a Critical Success.

### Mastery Feature

**Perfect Track**: After using _Sniff it Out_, you can spend 1 Hope to ask the GM one direct question about the tracked target's immediate plans. The answer must be truthful and useful.

## Mechanical Notes

Keen Senses rewards curiosity: spend Hope on Instinct investigation rolls to gain tactical details, species information, or a social edge simultaneously. The Feral Wonder path is strongest in natural environments where unarmed attacks and _Survivalist_ dice can swing fights; distribute your stored dice to allies at critical moments rather than holding them until they expire. The Practiced Observer path builds directly on your class feature: use _Sniff it Out_ to refresh Keen Senses for free, keeping your investigative momentum running through multi-scene situations. Both paths benefit from treating Instinct investigations as a core part of your action economy, not as side activities.

title: Night Broker
content:
|          Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Oddity]] & [[Thievery]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_Something that was in a locked room you've never actually entered_ or _a tool that works in ways you've stopped trying to explain_

### Hope Feature

_Slip the World_: Spend 3 Hope. Your next use of Between Places includes up to two willing allies within Very Close range, who travel with you.

### Class Feature

**Between Places**
Once per scene, move through a barrier, wall, closed door, or secured space as if it didn't exist, provided it is not made of stone or magically warded material. You appear on the other side quietly and without trace. While using this movement, you have the _Hidden_ condition until you take an action.

### Subclasses

As a Night Broker, choose between the following subclasses.

**Fold-Walker**
_Play the Fold-Walker if you want to treat the space between places as your territory and use it to move in ways no one can follow._

**Acquisitor**
_Play the Acquisitor if you want impossible access to be the foundation of a very efficient criminal practice._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- When did you first pass through something you shouldn't have been able to? Did anyone see?
- What is the strangest thing you've found on the other side of a place you weren't supposed to be?
- There are places you avoid because something lives between the walls. What kind of place is it?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- I brought you to a place no one else knows about. Where was it?
- You've asked me not to bring you along on a job again. What happened last time?
- You once saw me return from somewhere I had no business being. Did you ask where I'd been?

# Subclasses

## **Fold-Walker**

_Play the Fold-Walker if you want to treat the space between places as your territory and use it to move in ways no one can follow._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Spatial Memory_: You know when a space you've previously entered via Between Places has been changed, entered by others, or disturbed. You do not need to be present to know; you simply become aware of it.

_Between Moments_: Once per rest after you use Between Places, you may remain in the _Hidden_ condition even after taking a non-attack action. An attack or a failed roll to stay hidden ends the condition.

### Specialization Feature

**Fold Point**: The first time you use Fold Point in a scene, designate a location within Far range as a fold point. Once per scene hereafter, you may teleport to that point, including when you are targeted by an attack (which then resolves with Disadvantage against you). You may otherwise move to that location as an Action until the end of the scene.

### Mastery Feature

**Unmappable**: Once per session, while using Between Places, you may take one willing ally into the space between places. In this space, time does not pass for either of you, no damage can reach you, and you can observe the scene around you without being perceived. You must both emerge after a brief period (no longer than you can hold your breath).

## **Acquisitor**

_Play the Acquisitor if you want impossible access to be the foundation of a very efficient criminal practice._

### Spellcast Trait

Finesse

### Foundation Features

_Clean Hands_: Whenever you use Between Places to enter a space, you may take one item from that space that is not actively held by a character. Describe what you take. If it is not secured or locked, no additional roll is required.

_Quick Exit_: Once per rest immediately after a Thievery action, you may use Between Places even if you've already used it this scene.

### Specialization Feature

**Perfect Access**: Once per scene, when you use Between Places to enter a secured space, you have uninterrupted time to act within that space before anyone notices you entered. Describe one thing you accomplish fully before the scene resumes.

### Mastery Feature

**The Take**: Once per session, plan and execute a theft without requiring a separate downtime project. State what you intend to take and where it is. The GM tells you the Difficulty. Make a Finesse roll against that Difficulty. On a success, you have it.

## Mechanical Notes

Between Places is a positioning tool first. Use it to set up advantages, not just as an escape hatch. The Fold-Walker path rewards long-term spatial planning and treating locations as relationships; the Acquisitor path is about decisive, irreversible action with clean recovery. In either case, use your class feature every scene; the class loses much of its power if you hold Between Places in reserve indefinitely.

title: Oathbreaker Mystic
content:
|         Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Faithful]] & [[Weird]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A remnant of your former faith kept for reasons you haven't fully examined_ or _something that appeared the day you broke your vow and hasn't left you since_

### Hope Feature

_The Price Made Power_: Spend 3 Hope. Your next use of Broken Vow grants both its damage effect and one additional effect of your choice.

### Class Feature

**Broken Vow**
Once per scene, invoke one of your broken oaths as supernatural fuel. Describe the vow you violated: a promise to a deity, a sacred commitment abandoned, a religious duty betrayed. Choose one effect:

- Deal d8 magic damage to a target within Close range, using your Proficiency.
- Create a supernatural disturbance in the immediate area, treating this as the effect of a Level 1 Weird domain card of your choice.
- Gain a +2 bonus to your next Spellcast Roll this scene.

### Subclasses

As an Oathbreaker Mystic, choose between the following subclasses.

**Apostate**
_Play the Apostate if you want to wield the strange forces that haunt you after your broken oath._

**Branded Penitent**
_Play the Branded Penitent if you want the tension between what you owe and what you've taken to be the engine that drives you._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What did you swear, and to whom? Do you remember the moment you broke it?
- What appeared to you on the other side of that breaking? Did it speak?
- Is there anything you would refuse even now, or have you accepted you are past that point?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You witnessed me doing something I had sworn I would never do. Did you try to stop me?
- I've told you about the thing that answered when I broke my vow. Do you believe me?
- There's a faith practice I still carry, even now. You've noticed it. What is it?

# Subclasses

## **Apostate**

_Play the Apostate if you want to wield the strange forces that haunt you after your broken oath._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Open Vessel_: When you use Broken Vow, you may also manifest a minor supernatural phenomenon in the area: doors close sharply, lights bend, a voice half-heard. [[Character|Characters]] who witness this and are unaccustomed to the supernatural mark a Stress.

_Alien Inheritance_: Once per rest, when you take damage from a supernatural or magical source, spend 1 Hope to absorb its nature. Until the end of the scene, your attacks deal an additional 2d6 damage and count as that type damage in addition to your usual type.

### Specialization Feature

**Unsacred Ground**: Once per scene, spend 2 Hope. The space within Very Close range of you becomes hostile to all characters except you. They must mark a Stress at the start of each of their turns while they remain in that space. This lasts until the end of the scene or you dismiss it.

### Mastery Feature

**What Answered Back**: Once per session, the power you traded your faith for grants you a favor. Choose one spell-like effect from any Weird domain card of Level 3 or lower and apply it now without requiring a Spellcast Roll.

## **Branded Penitent**

_Play the Branded Penitent if you want the tension between what you owe and what you've taken to be the engine that drives you._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Penitent's Endurance_: When you use Broken Vow, clear 1 Hit Point in addition to the chosen effect. The divine wound feeds you even as it punishes you.

_Sacred Scar_: Once per rest, when you take damage, mark a Stress instead of a Hit Point. When you do, place a token on this card. At the end of the scene, spend these tokens to add +2 per token to your next Spellcast Roll.

### Specialization Feature

**Still Bound**: Once per scene, invoke the name of the deity whose vow you broke. All characters with any religious conviction must mark 1 Stress or grant you the right to speak without interruption through your next action. Adversaries with religious convictions make their next attack against you with Disadvantage.

### Mastery Feature

**The Debt Remembered**: Once per session, the divine power you betrayed intervenes, not in forgiveness but in use. Choose one: the GM sends you a vision with tactical or narrative significance, or a divine protective effect activates for your allies for the rest of the scene (treat as Aura of Mercy at full strength), whether you want it to or not.

## Mechanical Notes

Your class feature is tied directly to your backstory. Work with your GM to establish a list of the specific vows you broke and keep it accessible during play. The more precise the vow you invoke, the more resonant the fiction. The Apostate path plays aggressively with supernatural effects and builds toward a growing arsenal of alien power; the Penitent path builds resilience through sacrifice and draws mechanical strength from the ongoing relationship with a power that still has claim on you.

title: Obscuritan
content:
|          Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Creature]] & [[Oddity]] |        9         |          7          |

### Class Items

_A shimmering pelt of a strange creature_ or _a collection of foul-smelling dusts_

### Hope Feature

_Phase Alignment_: Spend 3 Hope to cause an [[Etherotaxia|Etherotaxic]] creature to lose the _Ethereal_ condition until the end of the Scene.

### Class Feature

**Of Two Worlds**
You can alter space around you to get at things most couldn't. Once per scene you can spend 2 Hope to give a [[Character]] or [[Creature]] you can see the _Warped_ condition. You may interact with a target with _Warped_ as if it were in Melee range regardless of its actual range.

### Subclasses

As an Obscuritan, choose between the following subclasses.

**Ethereal Stalker**
_Play the Ethereal Stalker if you want to use your strange abilities to unleash devastating attacks from strange angles._

**Curiosity**
_Play the Curiosity if you want to lean into oddity to unlock unique social abilities._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- How did you first notice that you didn't interact with the world around you the same way as your peers?
- What was your first experience dealing with [[Etherotaxia]], and how did it change how you saw the world?
- Where do you find commonality with your peers? Where do you find differences?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- The first time we met, I did something unusual that only you noticed. What was it?
- You have a nickname for me, and even though I hate it you keep using it. What do you call me?
- Over time, you've gotten used to my strangeness, except for one thing. What is it?

# Subclasses

## **Ethereal Stalker**

_Play the Ethereal Stalker if you want to use your strange abilities to unleash devastating attacks from strange angles._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Best Defense_: After a successful attack you can mark a Stress to give your target and yourself the _Overwhelmed_ condition. An _Overwhelmed_ target gains a -2 penalty to Evasion and Damage Thresholds.

_Gnasher_: You can make an attack roll against a target in Melee range. On a success, deal d8 damage using your Proficiency. If the target marks any hit points, any conditions currently affecting you are spread to your target.

### Specialization Feature

**Mind over Matter**: Mark a Stress to give yourself one of the following conditions:

- _Ethereal_: An _Ethereal_ creature takes no physical damage, gains a bonus to its Evasion equal to its Proficiency. Once per scene, an _Ethereal_ creature can move up to Very Close range through stone, wood, or dirt as if it were open air. This condition ends at the start of your next rest or within 1 hour of activation, whichever comes first.
- _Enraged_: An _Enraged_ target gains a bonus to its attack rolls equal to its Proficiency. Attack rolls against an _Enraged_ target gain Advantage. This condition ends at the end of the Scene or when the target is successfully attacked.
- _Docile_: A _Docile_ target deals no damage on attacks, gains a +2 bonus to Presence -2 penalty to Knowledge and Instinct. The _Docile_ condition ends after the affected target makes a successful attack against a [[Creature]] or [[Character]].

### Mastery Feature

**Fractured Reality**: Once per scene, when you apply a condition through your subclass features, choose one additional nearby target within Very Close range. That second target gains the same condition until the end of the Scene.

## **Curiosity**

_Play the Curiosity if you want to lean into oddity to unlock unique social abilities._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Charming Gaze_: Make a Spellcast Roll against a [[Character]]. On a success, that character gains the _Charmed_ condition and cannot tell you a lie unless they mark a Stress. After two Stress are marked in this manner, the _Charmed_ condition ends.

_Weave a Yarn_: When you spend a few moments recounting a story to a Character, you can make a Spellcast Roll against them. On a success, you can slip a detail into your story that the target character will fully believe.

### Specialization Feature

**Venomous Rebuke**: Once per rest, you can launch a stream of invectives at a Character that poisons their mind. Make a Spellcast Roll against your adversary. On a success, they gain the _Needled_ condition. Before a _Needled_ Character can act, they must make a Presence action roll (13). On a success, the condition ends. On a failure, they must mark a Stress.

### Mastery Feature

**Dreadful Fascination**: When a Character fails against _Venomous Rebuke_ or _Charming Gaze_, gain 1 Hope. Once per scene, you may spend 1 Hope to force that Character to reveal one truthful, relevant detail.

## Mechanical Notes

Of Two Worlds costs 2 Hope, so use it deliberately: give the Warped condition to targets you need in Melee range for specific subclass features, not as a general mobility fix. The Ethereal Stalker path rewards stacking conditions. Gnasher spreads your current conditions to targets on a hit, so enter fights with beneficial conditions already active where possible. Mind over Matter gives you control over your own condition going into an attack; choose Enraged for offense, Ethereal for escape, and Docile only when deescalation is the goal. The Curiosity path is sustained social control: apply Charmed or Needled early and let the Stress cost erode the target's ability to act freely over time. Dreadful Fascination pays out best in long social encounters where conditions have time to trigger more than once.

title: Peaceknife
content:
|         Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Charm]] & [[Thievery]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A concealed blade or tool kept somewhere polite_ or _a weapon concealed in a mundane item_

### Hope Feature

_Double Exit_: Spend 3 Hope. Use both effects of Always an Out simultaneously this scene without requiring a roll for either.

### Class Feature

**Always an Out**
Once per scene, when a social situation breaks down or a Thievery action fails, choose one before consequences are declared:

- **Redirect**: Describe how you smoothly steer the conversation or scene away from scrutiny. You gain the _Hidden_ condition and your next roll benefits from it.
- **Talk Through It**: Describe what you say to defuse the moment. Make a Presence roll (13). On a success, the immediate negative consequence is delayed until the start of the next scene.

### Subclasses

As a Peaceknife, choose between the following subclasses.

**Silver Diplomat**
_Play the Silver Diplomat if you want to read the room perfectly and make every exit look like an arrival._

**Moonlit Agent**
_Play the Moonlit Agent if you want to move quietly through situations and take what needs taking before anyone decides to make things complicated._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- You've walked out of a confrontation everyone expected to end badly without throwing a punch. How?
- What is the most valuable thing you've acquired in a room full of people who thought they were watching you?
- When did you last use your skills to help someone who didn't know you were helping them?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- I once played a role in front of you so convincingly you forgot it wasn't real for a while. What role was it?
- You've wondered whether my helpfulness is always genuine. What made you doubt?
- I've talked us out of a situation my actions got us into. Do you hold that against me?

# Subclasses

## **Silver Diplomat**

_Play the Silver Diplomat if you want to read the room perfectly and make every exit look like an arrival._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Read the Room_: Once per rest, before making a social roll, spend a moment observing the target. Ask the GM one question about the target's current emotional state, what they want from this encounter, or what would change their mind. The answer must be truthful.

_Graceful Exit_: Whenever you gain the _Hidden_ condition through your class feature, you also gain Advantage on your next roll in addition to the feature's normal benefit.

### Specialization Feature

**The Long Game**: Once per scene, when you succeed on a social roll, choose one character you influenced. Until the end of the session, they will take one small action in your favor without prompting. The GM determines the form it takes.

### Mastery Feature

**Everyone's Confidant**: Once per session, any character you speak with alone for more than a moment will volunteer one piece of information they didn't intend to share. You select the moment this happens after it occurs; the GM tells you what they revealed.

## **The Moonlit Agent**

_Play the Moonlit Agent if you want to move quietly through situations and take what needs taking before anyone decides to make things complicated._

### Spellcast Trait

Finesse

### Foundation Features

_Clean Extraction_: When you use your class feature to gain the _Hidden_ condition, you may immediately make one Thievery-related action without breaking the condition, provided the target doesn't have direct sightline to you.

_Soft Landing_: Once per rest, when you make a Thievery roll with Fear, spend 1 Hope to reroll the Fear die and take the better result.

### Specialization Feature

**No Trace**: Once per scene, after a successful Thievery action, you can ensure that the action leaves no evidence traceable to you this scene. The GM cannot use evidence of this specific act to advance adversary awareness.

### Mastery Feature

**Without a Trace**: Once per session, when you enter a secured space through your own skill or planning, nothing you do inside that space leaves any evidence unless you choose to leave it. This effect ends when you leave the space.

## Mechanical Notes

Peaceknife rewards knowing in advance whether you'll need to talk or run. Read the situation before you commit to either. Use backgrounds and Connections to establish your character as someone who plans every conversation like a job. The Diplomat path plays the long social game and builds relationships that pay out later; the Moonlit Agent path prefers decisive action with clean recovery. In either case, your class feature is strongest when you use it early, before consequences have a chance to compound.

title: Prizebreaker
content:
|           Domains           | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Creature]] & [[Violence]] |        9         |          7          |

### Class Items

_Scars or natural weapons that have been maintained and used_ or _a weapon that has marks of repeated repair_

### Hope Feature

_Hunt's End_: Spend 3 Hope. Your next attack against your Prey is made with Advantage. On a Critical Success, they take double damage and gain the _Slowed_ condition until the end of their next turn. While _Slowed_, a creature may only move up to Very Close range on their turn.

### Class Feature

**Blood Scent**
Once per scene, mark a target as your Prey. While a creature is your Prey, your attacks against them deal +1 damage, and if they attempt to create distance from you, they mark a Stress. You can only designate one Prey at a time. Designating a new Prey immediately releases the previous one.

### Subclasses

As a Prizebreaker, choose between the following subclasses.

**The Houndtooth**
_Play the Houndtooth if you want to end fights through inevitability by tracking, cornering, and closing in until there's nowhere left to go._

**The Berserker**
_Play the Berserker if you want to trade hits until you're the one still standing and get stronger with every blow you absorb._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- Something changed in how you fight. When did you stop thinking and just act?
- What do you feel in the moment before an attack that most people don't have a word for?
- Have you ever hunted something and found it was hunting you back? What happened?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You flinched at something I did in combat. What was it?
- I've tried to protect you from something I considered beneath my notice. Did you appreciate it?
- There's something about the way I move or behave that reminds you of an animal. What is it?

# Subclasses

## **The Houndtooth**

_Play the Houndtooth if you want to end fights through inevitability by tracking, cornering, and closing in until there's nowhere left to go._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Chase Instinct_: When your Prey moves away from you, you may immediately move up to Very Close range without spending your action. This cannot place you in immediate danger beyond the act of pursuit.

_Sense the Wound_: When your Prey has marked at least one Hit Point, you gain Advantage on all rolls against them.

### Specialization Feature

**Relentless Tracking**: Once per rest, when you designate a Prey, you know their general location relative to you for the rest of the session, even if they are _Hidden_ or out of sight.

### Mastery Feature

**No Escape**: Once per session, when your Prey attempts to flee a scene by any means, spend 2 Hope. You follow them, appearing in the same location they escape to at the start of the next scene.

## **The Berserker**

_Play the Berserker if you want to trade hits until you're the one still standing and get stronger with every blow you absorb._

### Spellcast Trait

Strength

### Foundation Features

_Pain Is Fuel_: Whenever you mark a Hit Point while you have a Prey designated, gain a +1 bonus to your next damage roll against that Prey.

_Savage Rush_: Once per rest, move to Melee range as part of your attack action without spending a separate move.

### Specialization Feature

**Feed the Frenzy**: Once per scene, after you deal damage to your Prey, mark a Stress to immediately make a second attack against them. This second attack is made with Advantage.

### Mastery Feature

**Titan's Weight**: Once per session, when you reduce your Prey to their last Hit Point, spend 1 Hope. All adversaries within Very Close range who witnessed the attack mark a Stress. Until the end of the session, adversaries will not willingly approach you without first succeeding on a roll to overcome their hesitation.

## Mechanical Notes

You need a Prey designated to be at your best. Make it a priority at the top of every combat encounter. This class rewards aggressive forward movement; standing back or acting defensively works against your strengths. The Houndtooth path is for players who want fights to end through positioning and relentlessness; the Berserker path is for players who want to absorb damage and come out ahead every time. Switching Prey mid-fight is always an option, but losing momentum on a kill costs you the bonus damage buildup.

title: Provocative Liar
content:
|          Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Oddity]] & [[Trickery]] |        10        |          6          |
### Class Items
_A prop that is different every time you look at it_ or _a record of lies you've told that have later become true_
### Hope Feature
_True Story_: Spend 3 Hope. The reality created by Narrative Collapse lasts until the end of the session rather than the scene, and can extend to Far range.
### Class Feature
**Narrative Collapse**
Once per scene, after you successfully deceive a target, you may declare that a small element of your lie has become temporarily real. Work with your GM: this manufactured reality holds for the duration of the scene and affects only the space within Close range of where the lie was told. Examples: a false door becomes solid, a claimed companion materializes briefly, an invented item appears in hand.

### Subclasses
As a Provocative Liar, choose between the following subclasses.
**Architect of Fictions**
_Play the Architect of Fictions if you want every lie to be part of something larger: a world of your own telling that outlasts the scene._

**Living Contradiction**
_Play the Living Contradiction if you want reality to stop arguing with you and start agreeing._
### Background Questions
_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._
- What is the lie you told that you noticed starting to become real? Did you mean for it to happen?
- Who do you know that can tell the difference between what you've said and what is? Do they trust you?
- What is the line you still won't cross? A lie you wouldn't tell even if you could make it real?
### Connections
_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._
- You watched a reality I manufactured hold together longer than it should have. What were you expecting to happen?
- I told you a lie that you later found to be true. Did that change how you listen to me?
- You've asked me to stop making things up in your presence. I haven't. Why do you keep asking?
# Subclasses
## **Architect of Fictions**
_Play the Architect of Fictions if you want every lie to be part of something larger: a world of your own telling that outlasts the scene._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Foundational Lie_: Once per session, at the start of a new location or social encounter, establish one foundational lie about your identity (your name, your role, your purpose). For as long as this lie is sustained, gain Advantage on all rolls to maintain it and a +1 bonus to Attitude from everyone who believes it.

_Corroborating Details_: Whenever you use Narrative Collapse, you may also establish three specific true-seeming details that NPC characters will independently corroborate without being prompted.

### Specialization Feature

**The Second Layer**: Once per scene, when someone challenges a deception you've established, you may reveal that the exposed lie was itself cover for a deeper, true deception. The character who challenged you becomes convinced of the deeper lie instead, and gains a +1 bonus to Attitude from the sense that they've seen through you.

### Mastery Feature

**The Architecture Complete**: Once per session, the foundational lie of this session can be made permanent. The GM must incorporate it as true going forward if it does not directly contradict established lore. Describe what has now always been true.
## **Living Contradiction**
_Play the Living Contradiction if you want reality to stop arguing with you and start agreeing._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Casual Contradiction_: Once per rest, state something false as fact in front of a character. Make a Spellcast Roll (12). On a success, the false thing becomes briefly physically real in a minor way, confirming your claim to any observer.

_Unraveling_: Whenever you use Narrative Collapse, characters who actively disbelieve your lie must mark 2 Stress as contradicting realities press against each other in their minds.

### Specialization Feature

**The World Agrees**: Once per scene, while your class feature is active, extend the manufactured reality to include one physically impossible element: a wall appears, gravity inverts in a small space, a sound plays from no source. This element persists until the scene ends.

### Mastery Feature

**Reality Debt**: Once per session, collapse a truth. Choose one established fact about the current scene that a character knows. That fact becomes untrue until the end of the session. Work with your GM. This cannot affect character death, major campaign-altering facts, or player-character defining truths without consent.

## Mechanical Notes

Commit fully to whatever you've said; half-hearted deceptions don't generate Narrative Collapse. Establish lies boldly and early, then pay attention to what happens when they start holding. The Architect path rewards long-term planning and complex social environments; the Living Contradiction path thrives in chaotic scenes where the impossible is expected. Engage your GM before sessions about the scope of your reality manipulation so you both know where the edges are.

content:
|           Domains           | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Artistry]] & [[Violence]] |        9         |          7          |

### Class Items

_A set of wraps given to you by a mentor_ or _a costume you use for prize fights_

### Hope Feature

_Stick and Move_: Spend 3 Hope and gain a +1 bonus to your Evasion until the end of the scene.

### Class Feature

**Showmanship**
When you make an attack, you can describe the flashy way you engage the adversary. When you do, add your Spellcast Trait instead of your Strength on the attack roll.

### Subclasses

As a Pugilist, choose between the following subclasses.

**Graceful Combatant**
_Play the Graceful Combatant if you want to fluidly move in a way that confounds others and delights observers._

**Brutal Technician**
_Play the Brutal Technician if you want to punish missteps by your opponent in both the battlefield and social gatherings._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What excites you about a fight, and when did you discover that?
- When did you find the most glory in adversity, and how has it shaped you as a character?
- Where did you first learn your technique, and who or what taught you?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- How did you first discover that I might be dangerous, and what was your reaction?
- You saw me fight something unusual. What was it, and how did it end?
- You've seen my softer side. What did I do that taught you I wasn't just fights and bravado?

# Subclasses

## **Graceful Combatant**

_Play the Graceful Combatant if you want to fluidly move in a way that confounds others and delights observers._

### Spellcast Trait

Agility

### Foundation Features

_Move Like Water_: After you hit an enemy, you can spend a Hope to make an additional move up to Close range away.
_Like a Glove_: Describe how you contort your body to fit into an otherwise inaccessible space. When you do so, mark a Stress and gain access through a normally inaccessible means.

### Specialization Feature

**Raining Blows**: After a successful hit, spend a Hope to make a subsequent attack. This can be repeated as long as your attack is successful.

### Mastery Feature

**Center of the Ring**: Once per scene, when you move after a successful hit, choose one ally within Close range. That ally may also move up to Close range immediately.

## **Brutal Technician**

_Play the Brutal Technician if you want to punish missteps by your opponent in both the battlefield and social gatherings._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Powerful Mind, Powerful Body_: When you spend an action studying a target, you gain a +3 bonus to all damage rolls against that target. Similarly, when you roll a successful Instinct roll against a [[Character]], you gain a +3 bonus to Presence rolls concerning that character.
_Snub Nose_: When you fail a roll with Fear, you can mark 2 Stress to change the failure to Hope.

### Specialization Feature

**Speed Bag**: When you roll a second consecutive success with Hope against a target, treat it as a Critical Success and reset the count for this feature.

### Mastery Feature

**Technical Knockdown**: After you trigger **Speed Bag**, the target gains the _Off-Balance_ condition. While _Off-Balance_, their next attack is made with Disadvantage.

## Mechanical Notes

Showmanship is always available. As long as you describe your attack with flair, you're rolling with your strongest trait. The Graceful Combatant path rewards constant movement. Move Like Water generates free positioning, and Raining Blows lets you chain attacks as long as you keep hitting, so don't stand still. The Brutal Technician path rewards focus. Study one target to build your damage bonus, then spend consecutive successful rolls to chain up to Speed Bag's critical trigger. Snub Nose is an insurance policy for when the dice turn against you. Both paths work best when you commit to a primary target early and apply consistent pressure rather than spreading attacks.

title: Scavenger
content:
|           Domains           | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Creature]] & [[Thievery]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A collection of rare egg shells_ or _a tool for creating wax impressions_

### Hope Feature

_Close Inspection_: Spend 3 Hope to double your Instinct on your next roll.

### Class Feature

**Finely Tuned Senses**
You are adept at noticing the small details that would escape most. Once per scene, when you make an Instinct roll to investigate or an attack roll, choose one of the following benefits:

- Gain Advantage on the roll.
- Add your Instinct to the result.
- If this is an Instinct roll and you fail with Hope, treat the result as a success with Fear instead.

### Subclasses

As a Scavenger, choose between the following subclasses.
**Carrion Creature**
_Play the Carrion Creature if you want to use strong Instincts to lead to major benefits on successful investigations._

**Needler**
_Play the Needler if you want to make highly opportunistic moves to take down much stronger foes._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- You've always noticed details that others simply cannot. How has this saved your life in the past?
- Are there things that your heightened senses make you love or hate more than most people?
- How has a lifetime of attention to detail changed how you interact with others?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You've forgotten something behind that I've pocketed for safekeeping. Were you grateful or suspicious?
- My skills once got you out of a major jam. What was that and what did I do?
- Have there been instances where my need to check and re-check things has made you frustrated with me? When and why?

# Subclasses

## **Carrion Creature**

_Play the Carrion Creature if you want to use strong Instincts to lead to major benefits on successful investigations._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Reading the Room_: You can mark a Stress and make an Instinct roll against a [[Character]]. On a success, you learn the [[Attitude]] of a character towards you or another member of your party.

_Detail-oriented_: On a successful Instinct roll to investigate, gain one of these benefits immediately:

- Gain a Hope. This is in addition to any Hope gained normally via your roll.
- Clear a Stress.
- Find a mundane item of your choosing.

### Specialization Feature

**Make the Most of it**: On a failure with Fear, you can spend a Hope to give all other party members a Hope.

### Mastery Feature

**Relentless Scrounger**: Once per scene, after you use _Finely Tuned Senses_, you can immediately move to Close range. If the triggering roll succeeds, clear 1 Stress.

## **Needler**

_Play the Needler if you want to make highly opportunistic moves to take down much stronger foes._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Weak Spots_: Make an Instinct Action Roll against an adversary. On a success, roll your damage dice again and add the result to the damage dealt to that adversary until the end of the scene.
_Quick Snap_: When you take damage from an adversary, you can spend a Hope to gain Advantage on all rolls against that adversary.

### Specialization Feature

**Vicious strike**: On a successful attack, you may mark a Hit Point to give the target the _Exposed_ condition. Treat the next successful attack against an _Exposed_ creature as a critical success no matter the outcome.

### Mastery Feature

**Needlepoint Finish**: When you hit a target with _Exposed_, gain 1 Hope and clear 1 Stress. If this attack downs the target, you may immediately mark another target within Close range as _Exposed_.

## Mechanical Notes

Finely Tuned Senses is flexible so read the situation before you decide which option to take. Advantage is safest. Adding your Instinct is strongest when your score is high. The failure-to-success flip is most valuable to steer outcomes when you need a success. The Carrion Creature path converts every investigation into a resource. Look for scenes where you can stack Hope, Stress clears, and items from a single roll sequence. Make the Most of it turns bad group luck into a recovery moment; use it before your party fully processes a fear-failure. The Needler path is pure burst: double damage from one Instinct strike, then build the Exposed chain to maximize a single target. Both paths benefit from using your class feature every scene.

title: Scholar
content:
|                    Domains                    | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Charm]] & [[Study]] |        10        |          6          |
### Class Items
_A well-worn notebook_ or _a lead weight on a string_
### Hope Feature
_Knowledgeable Adversary_: Spend 3 Hope to add your Knowledge to any roll.
### Class Feature
**Impressive Recall**
Your ability to remember details about the people you meet has endeared you to many. Once per rest, you can spend a Hope to learn an inconsequential detail about a [[Character]] with whom you are interacting. When you drop this detail in your conversation with this [[Character]], they will treat you with a +1 bonus to [[Attitude]] until the end of the scene.

### Subclasses
As a Scholar, choose between the following subclasses.

**Socialite**
_Play the Socialite if you want to use your impressive knowledge to bolster your natural charm._

**Bookworm**
_Play the Bookworm if you want to gain powerful benefits from your knowledge of the physical world._
### Background Questions
_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._
- At some point I've chosen books over people. Do I regret this, and do I choose differently now?
- What discoveries have I made as part of my studies, and have I gained any notoriety from them?
- Do I seek out others to form deep connections, or do I use my social skills to keep people satisfied with less than my true self?
### Connections
_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._
- I once woke you up from rest to excitedly tell you about something I'd discovered. What was it, and how did you respond?
- What was our first meeting like? Where did it happen, and how did you feel about me?
- I've experimented on you before. How did you discover it, and what was your reaction?
# Subclasses
## **Socialite**
_Play the Socialite if you want to use your impressive knowledge to bolster your natural charm._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Mover and Shaker_: When you first meet a new [[Faction]], you can spend a Hope to gain Advantage on any roll to influence its [[Adherent|Adherents]].
_Calculated Pressure_: When you _Prepare_ as a downtime move, you can choose a character that you know to give the _Mark_ condition. When you attempt to influence a character with the _Mark_ condition, treat a success with Hope as a _Critical Success_. The _Mark_ condition ends after your first roll to influence that character.

### Specialization Feature

**Cruel Words**: Once per scene, before you deal damage to a [[Character]] you can issue a scathing remark to your target and deal an additional d8 damage using your Proficiency.

### Mastery Feature

**Networked Intellect**: Once per session, after you successfully influence a [[Faction]] [[Adherent]], choose one ally. That ally gains Advantage on their next roll against the same faction this session.
## **Bookworm**
_Play the Bookworm if you want to gain powerful benefits from your knowledge of the physical world._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Eidetic Memory_: Spend a Hope to gain Advantage on your next Knowledge roll.
_Deep Research_: When you _Prepare_ as a downtime move, you can clear 1 Stress in addition to the normal benefits.

### Specialization Feature

**Studied Threat**: Spend a Hope to add your Knowledge score to your Evasion until the end of the scene.

### Mastery Feature

**Grand Thesis**: Once per scene, after a successful Knowledge roll, you can spend 1 Hope to either clear 1 Stress for yourself or grant an ally within Far range a +2 bonus to their next roll.

## Mechanical Notes

Impressive Recall rewards social preparation, so use it at the start of conversations rather than mid-scene. That way, the Attitude bonus shapes the whole interaction. The Socialite path rewards consistent factional work. Mover and Shaker applies every time you meet a new group, so treat each faction as an opportunity rather than a complication. Calculated Pressure is most valuable on high-stakes influence targets. Apply the Mark before your most important roll, not on easy targets. The Bookworm path trades social momentum for defensive and informational leverage. Use Studied Threat proactively when a scene shifts from dialogue to violence, so your Evasion is already reinforced before the first attack lands.
