title: Silver Tongue
content:
|           Domains           | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Artistry]] & [[Thievery]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A prop or instrument used in performance that conceals a second purpose_ or _a set of tools kept somewhere no one expects to look_

### Hope Feature

_Full House_: Spend 3 Hope. Until the end of the scene, when you use your class feature, you may choose two targets instead of one. The second attempt is made with Advantage.

### Class Feature

**Misdirected Hand**
Once per scene, when you successfully distract, entertain, or influence a target through performance or social interaction, you may immediately make one Thievery-related action against that same target (steal an item, plant an object, learn a concealed detail) without requiring an additional roll. Describe how the performance conceals the act.

### Subclasses

As a Silver Tongue, choose between the following subclasses.

**Dazzler**
_Play the Dazzler if you want the crowd watching you to be the whole point, with everything else happening in that perfect moment of distraction._

**Quiet Hand**
_Play the Quiet Hand if you want to be genuinely hard to catch, using just enough charm to keep people looking the wrong way._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What was the first thing you stole while someone was watching you do something else entirely?
- Your performance has a tell: a small habit that gets bigger when you're pulling a job. What is it?
- When did the two halves of your life first overlap in a way that got complicated?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You've watched me work a room. What did you notice that you haven't mentioned?
- I slipped something to you during a performance you didn't know about. What was it? Do you know yet?
- You've asked me to stop performing in situations where it put you at risk. What happened that prompted that?

# Subclasses

## **Dazzler**

_Play the Dazzler if you want the crowd watching you to be the whole point, with everything else happening in that perfect moment of distraction._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Captive Audience_: Once per rest, when you perform for a group of at least two characters, make a Spellcast Roll (13). On a success, all witnesses are _Stunned_ for one round unless they choose to mark a Stress to shake free. This cannot affect adversaries who are already alert to your criminal intent.

_Beguiling Act_: When you use your class feature, your distraction is so convincing that even suspicious characters become momentarily fixed on you. Gain Advantage on any Presence roll made in the same scene where you've activated your class feature.

### Specialization Feature

**The Only Show in Town**: Once per scene, choose a target who has witnessed you perform. Until the end of the scene, all rolls made by that target to notice anything other than you are made with Disadvantage.

### Mastery Feature

**Standing Ovation**: Once per session, after a significant performance, spend 2 Hope. Every character who witnessed it will speak favorably about you to anyone who asks for the rest of the session, potentially opening doors your reputation would normally close.

## **Quiet Hand**

_Play the Quiet Hand if you want to be genuinely hard to catch, using just enough charm to keep people looking the wrong way._

### Spellcast Trait

Finesse

### Foundation Features

_Practiced Grace_: When you use your class feature, you may also choose to leave something behind without detection. Useful for marking, planting, or quietly altering a [[Character|Character's]] inventory.

_Slipped Away_: Once per rest, after using your class feature against a target, you may immediately move up to Far range without a roll.

### Specialization Feature

**Clean Job**: Once per scene, when you succeed on a Thievery roll using your class feature, treat the result as a Critical Success.

### Mastery Feature

**Ghost of the Night**: Once per session, when you activate your class feature, you may use it against up to three different targets in the same scene. Each attempt benefits from the cover established by the previous success.

## Mechanical Notes

The distraction always comes before the action. Your class feature depends on the social moment landing first. Work to set it up in every scene with a social element before the situation turns. Your Thievery actions become considerably more reliable when attached to this feature, so think beyond objects in pockets: information, planted items, and quiet access all count. The Dazzler path rewards patience and planning; the Quiet Hand path rewards opportunism and clean exits.

title: Starbound Sentinel
content:
|         Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Valiance]] & [[Weird]] |        9         |          7          |

### Class Items

_A Hygiane Order field badge you may or may not have earned_ or _an instrument for measuring Creep density that hums near Etherotaxia_

### Hope Feature

_Manifest Protection_: Spend 3 Hope. When you activate The Ward, it also immediately deals d6 magic damage using your Proficiency to all Creep-touched or Etherotaxia adversaries within Close range.

### Class Feature

**The Ward**
Once per rest, activate The Ward as an action. Until the end of the scene, you project a protective field:

- Allies within Close range cannot be directly targeted by effects originating from the Creep, Etherotaxia, or haunts of Forestdown. They can still be affected by area effects.
- When an adversary bearing Creep corruption or Etherotaxia targets you specifically, gain a bonus to your Evasion equal to your Spellcast Trait.
  You may freely deactivate and reactivate The Ward during the scene.

### Subclasses

As a Starbound Sentinel, choose between the following subclasses.

**Threshold Keeper**
_Play the Threshold Keeper if you want to be the boundary between what your allies face and what they should never have to face._

**Rift-Touched**
_Play the Rift-Touched if you want to understand what you guard against well enough to turn it back and eventually wield it._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What was the first time the Creep or something from Forestdown threatened someone you cared about? Were you enough?
- Did the Hygiane Order train you, or did you come to this work another way? What do you make of the Order now?
- What do you know about what walks in Forestdown that you haven't told the party?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You've noticed my protection has limits and you haven't used that knowledge against me. Why?
- I told you something about what I've witnessed in Forestdown that most people can't process. What did I tell you?
- Being near me when The Ward is active affects you differently than it affects others. What do you feel?

# Subclasses

## **Threshold Keeper**

_Play the Threshold Keeper if you want to be the boundary between what your allies face and what they should never have to face._

### Spellcast Trait

Agility

### Foundation Features

_Hold the Line_: Once per rest, while The Ward is active, if an ally within Close range would mark their last Hit Point from a Creep or Etherotaxia source, they mark a Stress instead, and you mark 1 Stress.

_Fearless Stand_: While The Ward is active, you project a suppressive [[Aura]] (Maintenance Difficulty 13). At the end of each of your turns, each ally within Very Close range is shielded once from the next condition or harmful effect applied to them by a Creep or Etherotaxia source before the start of your next turn. If you are hit while maintaining this aura and fail the Maintenance roll, the aura collapses until the start of your next turn.

### Specialization Feature

**Warden's Challenge**: Once per scene, while The Ward is active, activate a challenging [[Aura]] (Maintenance Difficulty 17). While this aura is active, any adversary within Close range must target you rather than any other character. If you are hit and fail the Maintenance roll, the aura collapses immediately.

### Mastery Feature

**Unbroken Vigil**: Once per session, expand The Ward to Far range instead of Close range for the entirety of a scene. During this time, you cannot take actions that don't directly protect an ally; all your focus is on the boundary you're holding.

## **Rift-Touched**

_Play the Rift-Touched if you want to understand what you guard against well enough to turn it back and eventually wield it._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Counter-Resonance_: While The Ward is active, you can sense the nature of Creep manifestations and Etherotaxia in use within Far range without a roll. The GM describes what you detect in broad terms — enough to act on, not enough to fully understand.

_Absorbed Strangeness_: Once per rest, when you successfully protect an ally from a Creep or Etherotaxia effect using any ability, gain 1 Hope and add +2 to your next Spellcast Roll, as the redirected energy sharpens your attunement.

### Specialization Feature

**Turn the Weird**: Once per scene, redirect one Creep or Etherotaxia effect targeting an ally you are protecting. That effect now targets an adversary of your choice within Close range instead. Make a Spellcast Roll (14). On a success, the effect is fully transferred. On a failure, it dissipates harmlessly.

### Mastery Feature

**The Carried Weight**: Once per session, absorb a powerful Creep or Etherotaxia effect entirely into yourself, preventing it from affecting anyone else in the scene. You mark 3 Stress, but the effect is fully nullified. Something from Forestdown leaves a mark on you. Work with your GM to determine what changes.

## Mechanical Notes

The Ward needs to be active before the Creep or Etherotaxia reach your allies — raise it at the first sign of supernatural involvement, not after someone has already been targeted. The Threshold Keeper path is built around holding position. Stack Fearless Stand and Warden's Challenge to concentrate all incoming threat on yourself, but be ready for the Maintenance rolls to fail under sustained pressure. Hold the Line is your emergency release valve so don't spend it too early. The Rift-Touched path rewards attentiveness. Counter-Resonance gives you advance information every turn The Ward is up, so use it to actively plan. Absorbed Strangeness fires once per rest and stacks a +2 onto your next Spellcast Roll — save that roll for Turn the Weird or a high-stakes moment. The Carried Weight is a session-defining sacrifice and should be treated as a last resort.

title: Steadfast
content:
|           Domains           | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Creature]] & [[Valiance]] |        9         |          7          |

### Class Items

_A heavy chain wrap or collar_ or _an ornamental tabard bearing a symbol_

### Hope Feature

_Proud Defense_: Spend 3 Hope. If incoming damage does not meet your Major damage threshold, you take no damage instead.

### Class Features

**Flashy Display**
When you damage an adversary, you can mark a Stress to give them the _Enthralled_ condition. An _Enthralled_ creature can only attack you. This condition lasts until the GM spends a number of Fear equal to your Spellcast trait to remove it or you damage the creature again.

**Hide Before Honor**
After a long rest, you can choose to give yourself the _Honorbound_ condition. While _Honorbound_, you gain a penalty on all rolls equal to your Tier. Allies within Far range can take a bonus equal to your Tier on all rolls.

### Subclasses

As a Steadfast, choose between the following subclasses.

**Pillar Beast**
_Play the Pillar Beast if you want to use your prowess in martial matters to boost the abilities of your allies._

**Hidewarden**
_Play the Hidewarden if you're looking to put yourself deep into bad situations to gain benefits in self-sacrifice._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- You found yourself in a particularly bad situation. What was it, and how did you ultimately come out better for it?
- Your self-sacrifice has sometimes been taken as disingenuous. Who have you had the hardest time to convince you're not just being self-serving?
- What have you learned about yourself through a life of harsh adversity?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- I've done something for you that you've never told anyone. What was it, and why do you think I did it?
- How does my protective nature affect our relationship? Is it welcome or is it grating?
- You've rarely seen my aggressive side, except for once. Why did it come out and how did you feel seeing it?

# Subclasses

## **Pillar Beast**

_Play the Pillar Beast if you want to use your prowess in martial matters to boost the abilities of your allies._

### Spellcast Trait

Agility

### Foundation Features

_Diverting Blow_: After you successfully attack an enemy, you can make a Spellcast Roll against them. On a success, their next attack roll receives a -2 penalty.
_Social Parachute_: You can use your Agility to make a strategic retreat from a social situation. Once per rest, you can make a Spellcast Roll against a target with whom you or an ally is speaking. On a success, you and an ally within Very Close range may exit the interaction unnoticed.

### Specialization Feature

**Pounce**: When an ally within Close range is attacked, you can make a Spellcast Roll against the attacker. On a success, you move to the attacker's location and the attacker gains a -3 penalty on the attack roll.

### Mastery Feature

**Unbreakable Line**: Once per scene, when you intercept an attack with **Pounce** or your positioning features, reduce the attack's damage by your Tier before it is applied.

## **Hidewarden**

_Play the Hidewarden if you're looking to put yourself deep into bad situations to gain benefits in self-sacrifice._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_My Body is a Shield_: When you take damage from an attack, you can spend a Hope and gain a +2 bonus to your damage threshold for every Hit Point you currently have marked.
_Power Through_: Once per scene, mark a stress to clear a condition on you. If you clear a condition in this manner, gain Advantage on your next roll.

### Specialization Feature

**Legendary Flex**: Once per session when you have one or more marked Armor Slots, you can spend 2 Hope to clear one Armor slot. When you clear an Armor Slot in this manner, your next success with Hope during this scene counts as a Critical Success.

### Mastery Feature

**Last to Fall**: The first time each session you would mark your last Hit Point, mark 2 Stress instead and remain at 1 unmarked Hit Point. You immediately gain 1 Hope.

## Mechanical Notes

You have two class features. Decide during each rest whether to activate Hide Before Honor as the trade of your own roll penalty for an ally bonus is significant and shouldn't be a passive afterthought. Flashy Display costs a Stress, so use it on the most dangerous adversary in a scene to lock their targeting onto you. The Pillar Beast path plays as a mobile interceptor. Pounce is a reaction that repositions you and penalizes the attacker, so position yourself where you can reach the most likely attack targets. Diverting Blow stacks on top of normal attacks for free. The Hidewarden path rewards absorbing punishment. My Body is a Shield becomes stronger the more Hit Points you have marked, so don't treat damage as something to avoid. Last to Fall is a guaranteed once-per-session survival moment.

title: War Theorist
content:
|         Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Study]] & [[Violence]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_Tactical notes on a fight you've already won_ or _a weapon that's been repaired and modified so many times it's more yours than the original was_

### Hope Feature

_Decisive Action_: Spend 3 Hope. Apply all three effects of Combat Assessment simultaneously.

### Class Feature

**Combat Assessment**
Once per scene, take a brief moment at the start of combat or as a scene opens to read the battlefield. Choose one:

- **Expose Weakness**: One adversary of your choice has -2 penalty to their Difficulty for the rest of the scene.
- **Identify Advantage**: You and all allies in the scene gain +1 to Evasion for the rest of the scene.
- **Calculated Prediction**: Declare something that will happen in this combat (an enemy flees, a character moves to a specific position). If correct, your next attack roll deals d6 additional damage using your Proficiency.

### Subclasses

As a War Theorist, choose between the following subclasses.

**Siege Scholar**
_Play the Siege Scholar if you want to engineer the perfect kill and deliver it with precision by knowing exactly where to hit before you swing._

**Front-Line Theorist**
_Play the Front-Line Theorist if you want taking damage to make you smarter and being smarter to make you hit harder._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What battle taught you more than any training ever could?
- Who have you studied thoroughly and then been forced to fight? What happened?
- Where is the line between tactics and brutality, and do you know when you've crossed it?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You saw me apply a lesson I'd clearly learned somewhere hard. What did I do?
- I've given you tactical advice that saved your life. Do you owe me for that?
- You've questioned whether my approach to combat has replaced something more human. What were you really asking?

# Subclasses

## **Siege Scholar**

_Play the Siege Scholar if you want to engineer the perfect kill and deliver it with precision by knowing exactly where to hit before you swing._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Structural Analysis_: When you use Combat Assessment to expose a weakness, gain an additional effect: anyone targeting that adversary this scene may reroll one damage die on a successful hit.

_Applied Physics_: Once per rest, before making an attack, spend a moment calculating angles. The attack is made with Advantage and deals d4 additional damage using your Proficiency on a hit.

### Specialization Feature

**Optimal Angle**: Once per scene, when you succeed on an attack, immediately direct an ally within Close range to attack the same target. Their attack is made with Advantage.

### Mastery Feature

**Calculated Strike**: Once per session, after using Combat Assessment, choose one adversary. Your next attack against them automatically hits without a roll and deals maximum damage. Reserve this for the moment you have confirmed their weakness.

## **Front-Line Theorist**

_Play the Front-Line Theorist if you want taking damage to make you smarter and smarter to make you hit harder._

### Spellcast Trait

Knowledge

### Foundation Features

_Pain Informs_: When you mark a Hit Point in combat, you may immediately use Combat Assessment and choose either of the first two options, without consuming your scene use.

_Aggressive Adaptation_: Once per rest, after you take damage, your next attack against the source of that damage deals an additional d8 damage using your Proficiency.

### Specialization Feature

**Improvised Excellence**: Once per scene, when you fail a roll in combat, describe what you learned from the failure. On your next roll of the same type in the same scene, gain +3 to the result.

### Mastery Feature

**The Final Lesson**: Once per session, when you have been hit at least three times in a single combat, your next attack is automatically a Critical Success without a roll. Describe what you observed in this fight and how you applied it.

## Mechanical Notes

Combat Assessment works best when you use it at the very start of any fight. Get your chosen effect down before the chaos starts. Expose Weakness is strong but requires your party to press the advantage on the same target, so coordinate with your allies before spending it. The Siege Scholar path builds toward one devastating, perfectly positioned strike. The Front-Line Theorist path rewards staying in the thick of it and improving as you take hits. Both paths benefit from paying attention to adversary behavior between your turns.

title: Wraithcaller
content:
|         Domains          | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :----------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Creature]] & [[Weird]] |        10        |          6          |

### Class Items

_A clutch of opalescent feathers_ or _a jug of bitter-tasting green liquid_

### Hope Feature

_Recall_: Spend 3 Hope to call a dissipated _Ghostly Companion_ back to service.

### Class Features

**Ghostly Companion**
You gain a special downtime project called _Attract Companion_ with a Countdown Tracker of 3. When you complete this project, you gain a unique item called a _Wraithcaller's Lure_. You can use this item to attempt to befriend an ethereal creature you encounter in Tidwell. This creature follows the rules provided for [[Ranger Companions]].

**Veilstep**
Once per scene, spend 1 Hope to move up to Very Close range through creatures and objects as a flicker of spectral motion. This movement cannot end inside solid matter.

### Subclasses

As a Wraithcaller, choose between the following subclasses.

**Grovetender**
_Play the Grovetender if you want to command companion spirits and hold the line between the real and ethereal._

**Mire Whisperer**
_Play the Mire Whisperer if you want to manipulate fear, portents and luck via supernatural abilities._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- What first answered your call from the ethereal, and why did it choose you?
- What have you taken from Forestdown that you haven't shared with anyone?
- Which part of your uncanny abilities do you hide from allies, and why?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- You witnessed one of my spectral rites. What did you see, and what did you assume about me afterward?
- I asked you to carry a trinket that attracts Etherotaxia. Why did you agree?
- We share a memory that no one else can verify. What happened, and who do you believe?

# Subclasses

## **Grovetender**

_Play the Grovetender if you want to command companion spirits and hold the line between life and death._

### Spellcast Trait

Instinct

### Foundation Features

_Silent Procession_: Once per scene, you may direct your Ghostly Companion to appear anywhere within Close range. This happens instantaneously and without notice by adversaries.
_Pale Shelter_: Once per scene, when an ally within Close range receives damage, spend 1 Hope to reduce that damage by your Spellcast Trait.

### Specialization Feature

**Unquiet Chorus**: Mark 1 Stress to _Empower_ your companion until end of Scene. While empowered, it gains Advantage on one attack roll. While _Empowered_, your companion can impose _Hidden_ on you or an ally within Very Close range once.

### Mastery Feature

**Sovereign of Echoes**: Once per session, when your companion dissipates via accumulated stress, it immediately reforms with no Stress marked. When this happens, you gain 1 Hope.

## **Mire Whisperer**

_Play the Mire Whisperer if you want to manipulate fear, omen, and rumor through eerie social pressure._

### Spellcast Trait

Presence

### Foundation Features

_Whisper Through Bone_: Once per scene, make a Spellcast Roll against a [[Character]] you can see. On a success, ask one direct question they must answer honestly or mark 1 Stress.
_Bad Omen_: Once per scene, when you succeed with Fear on a roll, choose a [[Character]] within Far range. That character takes a -1 penalty to their next roll before the end of the scene.

### Specialization Feature

**Hex of Unrest**: Once per scene, make a Spellcast Roll against a [[Character]] within Far range. On a success, they take a -2 penalty to their next roll. If they are already affected by _Bad Omen_, they also mark 1 Stress.

### Mastery Feature

**Name in the Dark**: Once per scene, when a target fails while affected by _Bad Omen_ or **Hex of Unrest**, you can either gain 1 Hope or clear 1 Stress.

## Mechanical Notes

Your two class features work together: use Veilstep to reposition before key actions, then apply your subclass features from the resulting position. Both are Hope-free or Hope-light, so conserve Hope for subclass abilities. The Grovetender path centers on your Ghostly Companion's placement. Silent Procession lets you reposition it without telegraphing; use it before combat reaches your allies rather than reactively. Sovereign of Echoes is most valuable when your companion has been under sustained pressure. Let it work hard, then bring it back if it falls. The Mire Whisperer path rewards patience: apply Bad Omen first, then stack Hex of Unrest, and let Name in the Dark pay out when targets fail. Don't spend the condition early before it has a chance to trigger.

title: Youthful Protagonist
content:
|           Domains           | Starting Evasion | Starting Hit Points |
| :-------------------------: | :--------------: | :-----------------: |
| [[Valiance]] & [[Violence]] |        9         |          7          |

### Class Items

_Something handed down from someone who believed in you before you believed in yourself_ or _a weapon that's a little too big for you, and you've never mentioned it_

### Hope Feature

_Against All Odds_: Spend 3 Hope. Not Done Yet lasts until the end of the scene rather than the end of your next turn. While active, whenever an ally within Close range takes damage, you may absorb it instead.

### Class Feature

**Not Done Yet**
When you take damage that would force you to mark at least one Hit Point, you can instead mark a Stress. While Not Done Yet is active (until the end of your next turn):

- You cannot be moved, knocked down, or forced to retreat by any means.
- All allies within Close range gain +1 to their Evasion.
- If you have more Stress marked than Hit Points, your next attack deals +d6 additional damage.

### Subclasses

As a Youthful Protag, choose between the following subclasses.

**Reluctant Chosen**
_Play Reluctant Chosen if you want to be the one who didn't ask for any of this and somehow can't stop stepping up anyway._

**Burning Conviction**
_Play Burning Conviction if you want every blow you take to be paid back tenfold by the ones who hurt people you cared about._

### Background Questions

_Answer any of the following background questions. You can also create your own questions._

- When did someone first tell you that you couldn't do this, and what made you decide they were wrong?
- There was a moment you almost gave up. What made you keep going?
- What are you actually fighting for that you haven't said out loud yet?

### Connections

_Ask your fellow players one of the following questions for their characters to answer, or create your own questions._

- I've done something reckless to protect you that I'd do again without hesitation. Have you forgiven me for the cost?
- When everything stops making sense, you're the reason I keep going. Does that scare you?
- You told me once that there was another way. Were you right?

# Subclasses

## **Reluctant Chosen**

_Play Reluctant Chosen if you want to be the one who didn't ask for any of this and somehow can't stop stepping up anyway._

### Spellcast Trait

Agility

### Foundation Features

_Anybody Else Would Have Stopped_: Once per rest, when you use Not Done Yet, you can immediately clear 1 Stress after marking it. You pushed through something that should have ended you.

_Always in the Way_: Once per rest, when an ally within Very Close range would mark a Hit Point, you may step in front and mark it instead. When you do, clear 1 Stress.

### Specialization Feature

**Just This Once**: Once per scene, until the start of your next turn any attack that would target an ally within Close range targets you instead. Attack rolls against you during this time gain Disadvantage.

### Mastery Feature

**The Part Where I Don't Give Up**: Once per session, activate Not Done Yet without needing to take damage to trigger it. While active, you may intercept one hit per round from any ally within Close range. The effect lasts for the rest of the scene.

## **Burning Conviction**

_Play Burning Conviction if you want every blow you take to be paid back tenfold by the ones who hurt people you cared about._

### Spellcast Trait

Strength

### Foundation Features

_It's Making Me Stronger_: When you use Not Done Yet and mark a Stress instead of a Hit Point, your next attack deals +d8 additional damage. This bonus stacks once per use of Not Done Yet per scene.

_Running on Something_: When more than half your Hit Points are marked, gain a bonus to all damage rolls equal to the number of marked Hit Points.

### Specialization Feature

**Spite as a Strategy**: When Not Done Yet is active, your attacks against any adversary who has targeted you during this scene gain Advantage. Add your Level to damage dealt.

### Mastery Feature

**I Told You So**: Once per session, when you have used Not Done Yet at least twice in a single combat, your next attack is automatically a Critical Success. Describe what you were thinking about during this fight that made you refuse to stop.

## Mechanical Notes

Not Done Yet is an active decision: you must choose to use it when damage is declared, not before or after. It rewards knowing when protecting yourself through Stress is worth it versus letting a Hit Point land. The Reluctant Chosen path rewards staying close to your allies and making your body a wall between them and harm; the Burning Conviction path rewards staying in the thick of it and converting every wound into momentum. Both paths require careful Stress management. Don't trigger Not Done Yet when you're near your Stress limit, or the thing that keeps you going will be the thing that ends you.

title: Badlander
content:
_You hail from the hot, arid interior of the [[Gyhrra Badlands]]._

**Staunch**: You gain Advantage on Strength reaction rolls.

title: Bent Trunkish
content:
_You were raised in a community adherent to the Church of the Bent Trunk. You hold charity and humility as core values._

**Charitable**: Once per session, you may spend a Hope to grant another player character Advantage on their next roll.

title: Bogdown
content:
_You come from the rumored city at the heart of The Waste, where the strange creatures that live there have formed a society unlike any other on Tidwell._

**Tahekaija-Touched**: You bear the mark of the city-sized spider, Tahekaija. You gain Advantage on rolls to intimidate creatures.

title: Carnithian
content:
_You are a part of a community located in the bountiful plains and forests of Carnithia._

**Forager**: Once per session, when you fail a roll to find or identify an item, you may spend a Hope to attempt the roll again.

Either way, they’ll likely bear the name of a specific place or collective within your lore.

title: Davite
content:
_You are a strict adherent of the [[Church of the Revenant God]], which believes all life exists in the dream of a **seven year old boy named Dave**. As such, you hold a fatalistic view and can bear most situations either with grace or humor._

**Fatalist**: Once per Rest, you can choose to mark a Stress instead of a Hit Point, or vice versa.

title: Ethereal
content:
_You bear a strong affinity for the Etherotaxia of Tidwell, and have become comfortable with them and in their presence. As such, you are much more deft in dealing with the numerous non-corporeal creatures which inhabit the continent._

**Ghost-touched:** You gain Advantage on any roll taken to interact with Etherotaxia, and gain Resistance to damage from creatures and characters of that type.

title: Gatehouse
content:
_Born to the communities supporting Gatehouse, you are intimately familiar with combatting Creep and Etherotaxia_

**Incorruptible**: You have Advantage on reaction rolls to resist effects of the Creep.

title: Kybon Isle
content:
_You were born on the distant Isle of Kybon. As every former inhabitant of that island, you have no clear memory of your time there, but you bear the mark of the shadow._

**Shadow-marked:** When you roll a 1 with your Fear Die, you may re-roll it. If the new roll is rolled with Fear, gain a Hope.

title: Medilaac
content:
_Your time in Medilaac shaped your views on family and society. You have gained an intense appreciation for their adaptability to different cultures._

**Cultural Shapeshifter**: During character creation, choose another community. You gain the benefits of that community. Once per long rest, you may spend a downtime action to gain the benefits of any other Community card. This benefit persists until you next take the action.

title: Mercy & Ascension
content:
_You hail from the holy city of the [[Davites]]. Your time spent there has accustomed you to the ascetic lifestyle of a warrior or monk._

**Self-Reliant**: When you Prepare alone, you may clear a Stress in addition to gaining a Hope.

title: Mnojest-Gora
content:
_You hail from the mountains and fjords of western Tidwell, where the great mountain Rhodia stands and bears the World Book. Your time in the region has given you a deep appreciation of the past ages of Tidwell._

**Inspired**: You have Advantage on rolls relating to information from the Past Ages of Tidwell.

title: Monaster
content:
_You served at Monaster, the seat of power of the [[Church of the Revenant God]]. Regardless of whether this time was spent voluntarily or involuntarily, you have grown accustomed to unreasonable demands of the [[Petrarch]]._

**Duty-bound**: Once per session, you can mark any number of Stress. For every two stress spent this way, gain 1 Hope.

title: Poireau Islands
content:
_You were raised amongst the seafaring clans of the Poireau Island. You have gained an advanced knowledge of tight-knit, insular communities and can navigate them with ease._

**Attuned to Custom**: Once per session, you may make an attempt to socially influence a character when the character would be otherwise unwilling. Work with your GM to determine the Difficulty and manner of this interaction.

title: Reveille
content:
_You have spent a considerable amount of time in the Capitol city of Tidwell, and have become accustomed to dealing with the crowded and lively city streets._

**City Dweller**: Whenever you would roll a die during a short rest, you can roll a d6 instead.

title: Risbullah
content:
_Your time in the major trading hub of Tidwell has granted you an expertise in navigating the intricacies of negotiation._

**Trader**: You gain Advantage on rolls to negotiate with authorities, identify valuable items, or find the best deals in the area.

title: Varjalune Republic
content:
_You hail from one of the two major cities of the Varjalune Republic - either Alipinn or Taliga. You are accustomed to the harsh conditions of the wet season._

**Hardy Spirit**: Once per long rest, you may swap the values of your Hope and Fear Dice.

title: Wastelander
content:
_You hail from [[The Waste]], and as such you are subject to the isolation and madness that infects most of that land. It is not without its advantages, as it often allows you to peak behind the veil of reality._

**Mad**: Once per session, the GM can take an action on your behalf. If they do so, you may ask one question of the GM relating to the game. If they refuse to answer, you may choose for the action to not happen or to gain 2 Hope.

title: (Level 1) Acolyte
content:
Your experience in a structured belief system has left you adept at navigating their structures and orders.

After a long rest, place a number of tokens on this card equal to your Instinct. When you deal with a religious organization, you can spend a token from this card to gain one benefit from the following options:

- Gain Advantage on a roll to negotiate, persuade, or influence
- Clear a Stress
- Gain a Hope

When you take a long rest, clear all unspent tokens.

title: (Level 1) Analysis
content: When you spend a turn studying an adversary, gain Advantage on rolls against that target for the rest of the scene.

title: (Level 1) Any Means Necessary
content:
**Grimoire**

**Knuckle Under**: When you fail a roll with Hope, mark a Hit Point to reroll. 

**Toughen Up**: Mark a Stress to gain a +1 bonus to your Evasion until the end of the scene. 

**Headbutt**: When you succeed in an attack roll, you can mark a Stress to do an additional d6 damage using your Proficiency.

title: (Level 1) Aura of Bravery
content:
**Aura**

Once per rest, you can activate an [[Aura]] of Bravery until the end of the scene. When the Aura of Bravery is active, any allies in Close range of you gain a +1 Bonus to their Major and Severe damage thresholds. Maintenance Difficulty (13).

title: (Level 1) Beguiling
content: Your presence is distracting. When a [[Character]] is interacting with you, you can spend a Hope to reduce the Difficulty for allies attempting to go unnoticed by that character.

title: (Level 1) Bewitch
content:
You may use a piece of your art to influence a [[Character]] or [[Creature]]. Make a Spellcast Roll against a target within Close range. If you succeed, the affected target becomes _Ensorcelled_. The Ensorcelled target is affected for the duration of its next turn. Choose from one of the following commands to issue to the target:
- Stop - The affected target stops its current activity and freezes in place, taking no action
- Retreat - The affected target moves as far away from you as it can
- Come - The affected target approaches you until it is in Very Close range

title: (Level 1) Blinding Dust
content: Make a Spellcast Roll against all targets within Very Close range. Targets you succeed against gain the _Blinded_ condition. While _Blinded_, they're _Vulnerable_ and unable to see. This condition is cleared after the target is successfully attacked or they spend time on their turn to clear the dust from their eyes.

title: (Level 1) Conjured Weapon ★
content:
Make a Spellcast Roll against a target in Very Close range. On a success, deal d8+2 damage using your Proficiency. You may mark a Stress to use this again against a second target.
***
**Curse**: Repeated use of this spell makes you tired, as the weight of the weapons you are not carrying is given back to you slowly over time.

title: (Level 1) Courageous Words
content: You can spend a few moments speaking encouraging words to your allies. When you do so, each party member gains a Hope.

title: (Level 1) Craft
content: Once per session, during a rest where you work on a project as a downtime activity, you may mark an additional tick on the progress countdown to your project completion.

title: (Level 1) Cunning
content:
---
einstructions: Add a token control to this card.
---

When you roll with Fear, place a token on this card. You can spend tokens from this card to gain one of the following effects:
- Add +1 to the result of an Instinct roll.
- While out of combat, gain the _Hidden_ condition.
- Add +1 to the result of a Damage roll.
After a long rest, clear any unspent tokens from this card.

title: (Level 1) Divine Grace ★
content:
Once per session, when you succeed on a trait roll with Fear, you can mark a Stress to roll a d4. Divide the result as Hope and distribute it how you choose within your party.
***
**Curse:** When you use this ability, you begin to glow with the intensity of candlelight. This light does not illuminate the space around you, but instead makes you Vulnerable. This effect ends when you either mark a Hit Point or take a long rest.

title: (Level 1) Entreat
content: Once per rest, you gain a temporary +1 bonus to the [[Attitude]] of a [[Character]] to whom you make a request. Make a Presence roll. On a success with Fear, the target fulfills your request but gains a -1 penalty to their [[Attitude]] towards you until your next rest.

title: (Level 1) Erratic Movements
content: Once per rest, you may move up to Far range without a roll. You control your destination, but the means of conveyance is instantaneous and inexplicable.

title: (Level 1) Erudition
content: You gain a +1 bonus to [[Attitude]] to [[Adherent|Adherents]] of [[Faction|Factions]] which have a [[Primary Goals|Primary Goal]] associated with knowledge.

title: (Level 1) Fancy Fibs
content: When you fail a roll to deceive a target, you can mark a Stress to re-roll the result.

title: (Level 1) Fighter's Presence
content: You gain a bonus to Presence rolls equal to the number of Hit Points you have marked.

title: (Level 1) Hardy
content:
---
einstructions: When this card is in loadout, add an option to the damage calculator to mark one stress and reduce incoming hit points by one. Add reminder text when that button is present that it has a "once per scene" limitation.
---

Once per Scene when you would normally mark a Hit Point, you may mark a Stress instead.

title: (Level 1) Inspiration
content:
---
einstructions: include a button to add presence value's worth of tokens to this card. long rest refresh
---

You can draw from a deep well of inspiration to complete certain tasks. After a long rest, place a number of tokens on this card equal to your Presence. You can spend a token from this card to give yourself a benefit from the following options:
- Clear a Stress
- Clear a Hit Point
- Gain a Hope
When you take a long rest, clear all unspent tokens

title: (Level 1) Inspired
content:
You fill yourself with inner strength granted by your belief in a deity. You can spend a Hope to clear a Hit Point or a Stress.

Once per long rest, when you use this card, you can proselytize about your deity to an unbeliever. If you do so, clear 2 Hit Points or 2 Stress instead.

title: (Level 1) Instigator
content: When you are the first to attack, gain Advantage on your attack roll. On a success, spend a Hope to deal an additional d8 physical damage using your Proficiency.

title: (Level 1) Interstices ★
content:
When you succeed a roll with Fear, you can spend a Hope to place a token on this card. For every token on this card, gain an additional downtime activity on your next rest.
***
**Curse**: Your rest is fitful, and comes in spurts. You gain a perpetual air of being tired.

title: (Level 1) Keen Senses
content: When you use any of your senses to detect, observe or investigate, you gain Advantage on Instinct rolls.

title: (Level 1) On the Lam
content: When you are the target of an Attack, you can spend a Hope to gain a +2 bonus to your evasion until the attack resolves.

title: (Level 1) On the Send
content: Once per rest when you succeed at a Trait Roll to deceive a character, describe how you orchestrate a continuance of the lie. Mark a Stress to place a number of tokens on this card equal to your Spellcast trait. You may spend a token from this card to cause your target to fail a roll related to your deception. At the end of the scene, clear all tokens from this card.

title: (Level 1) Powerful Insight
content: Once per rest when you make an Instinct roll, you can add your Proficiency to the result.

title: (Level 1) Practiced Liar
content:
---
einstructions: add a button to gain tokens equal to presence score
---

Gain a number of tokens on this card equal to your Presence. You can spend these tokens to gain any of the following effects:
- Add +1 bonus to the result of a roll to deceive or trick a [[Character]] into believing a lie you told them
- Add a -1 penalty to the Difficulty of a roll to avoid scrutiny when you are disguised as another character

title: (Level 1) Precocious
content:
---
einstructions: when selected, add control to select a level 2 domain card. That domain card displays in place of that.
---

Choose a Level 2 Domain Card from the Charm Domain. This acts as a copy of that card, and grants you all the abilities of the original.

title: (Level 1) Research
content:
You gain a short rest downtime move called _Research_ which replaces _Prepare_. You can _Research_ with one or more members of your party who use _Prepare_ as if they were also doing _Research_, but only you gain the additional benefits.

When you _Research_, gain the normal benefits of _Prepare_ and place two tokens on this card. Any number of tokens can be spent from this card. For each spent token, you can gain one of the following effects:
- +1 bonus to the result of a Knowledge roll
- +1 bonus to the result of a Damage roll
- +1 temporary bonus to your Evasion which expires after being attacked

title: (Level 1) Spark ★
content:
Make a Spellcast Roll against a target. On a success, deal d6 damage using your Proficiency.
***
**Curse**: You carry inside you a small stone that borrows heat from you to fuel this fire. Any time you use this ability, your fingers develop a sheen of frost and a little more of the heat inside of you disappears.

title: (Level 1) Tapped Into the Ether
content: Once per scene, spend a Hope to add your Knowledge to your next roll.

title: (Level 1) Unburdened Grace
content: When you are wearing light or no armor, gain a bonus to your evasion equal to half your Finesse.

title: (Level 1) Whispered Thoughts ★
content:
Once per scene, you can transmit a short message via whisper to a willing target within Very Far range. They can return a short message immediately after receiving yours.
***
**Curse**: While using this ability, your voice is audible to all within Very Close range of your target. If the target chooses to respond, their voice is audible to all within Close range.

title: (Level 2) Abnormal Physiology
content:
You develop a physical feature unusual for your Species. After a rest, choose one of the following effects that lasts until your next rest:
- _Extrasensory perception_: Once per scene, you can mark a Stress to re-roll your Fear die.
- _Punishing heft_: Once per scene when you succeed at at an attack roll, add d6 physical damage using your Proficiency.
- _Hypnotic movements:_ Once per scene, you can momentarily _Stun_ a creature that can see you within Close range. While _Stunned_, they can't use reactions and can't take any other actions until they clear this condition.

title: (Level 2) Applied Entropy ★
content:
Make a Spellcast Roll against a target within Far range. On a success, roll d20 dice using your Proficiency and deal damage to the target equal to the highest result. The target is also _Decaying_ until after its next action. While a target is _Decaying_, it takes a -2 penalty to its Evasion score.
***
**Curse**: Whenever you use this class feature, you visibly age. Cumulative uses cause you to age further. Though this aging has no mechanical effects on you, the perception of others may be affected. After a long rest, half of this visible aging is reversed.

title: (Level 2) Aura of Destiny ★
content:
**Aura**

Once per rest, you can activate an [[Aura]] of Destiny until the end of the scene. When the Aura of Destiny is active, any allies in Close range of you gain a +3 bonus to damage rolls. Maintenance Difficulty (13).
***
**Curse**: It is exceptionally hard for you to move inconspicuously, as you attract a small following of admirers whenever you're in public.

title: (Level 2) Benevolence ★
content:
You can spend a Hope to grant an ally a token that can be redeemed for one of the following benefits:
- Reroll a die before the result of the roll is known
- Gain Advantage on one attack
- Gain the benefits of a short rest downtime activity

---
**Curse:** When you use this ability, you are stricken with the sense that your generosity is unrepaid. You feel resentment towards the recipient until your next long rest unless they make a concerted effort to show you gratitude.

title: (Level 2) Calming Presence ★
content:
---
einstructions: add a button to give the player the "wracked with doubt" condition, which enforces a -1 penalty to presence. This condition can be gained multiple times. Add another button to "Clear Doubt" which removes all instances of "Wracked with Doubt"
---

Make a Spellcast Roll (13) and target a [[Character]] or [[Creature]] within Melee range. On a success with Hope, clear 2 Hit Points and Stress on the target. On a success with Fear, clear 2 Hit Points on the target. On a failure, clear 1 Stress or Hit Point on the target.
***
**Curse**: When you fail this roll, gain the _Wracked with Doubt_ condition. Gain a -1 penalty to your Presence. This condition can stack. On your next rest, clear all instances of _Wracked with Doubt_.

title: (Level 2) Corner Man
content: Once per session during a short rest, you can spend a Hope to take the long rest downtime move **Clear all Hit Points**.

title: (Level 2) Ethereal Buddy
content:
Your comfortability with the supernatural has attracted a curious specimen of [[Etherotaxia]] that follows you intently. You can issue this [[Creature]] basic commands (e.g. Come, Go, Stay) and it will follow them to the best of its understanding.

After a long rest, gain a number of tokens on this card equal to your Proficiency. You may spend these tokens to activate any of the following tricks:
- **Share senses**: You gain a brief glimpse of the world through the senses of your Ethereal Buddy. When you do so, you can determine general details about a scene but specific information may be hard to decipher
- **Alert**: Your Ethereal Buddy will make a noise audible only to you if a general activity you describe to it takes place within Close range of it. 
- **Aid**: Before you roll, describe how your Ethereal Buddy assists you. Lower the Difficulty of your roll by 1.

Clear unspent tokens at the start of a long rest.

title: (Level 2) Flatterer
content:
---
einstructions: Add a button to gain 2 hope
---

Once per long rest, when you flatter an ally, you gain 2 Hope and they clear 2 Stress.

title: (Level 2) Iconoclast ★
content:
---
einstructions: Include token controls. Include a button to add proficiency's worth of tokens to card when pressed. long rest refresh.
---

After a long rest, gain a number of tokens equal to your Proficiency. When you interact with a [[Character]] which has a negative [[Attitude]] towards you, you can spend any number of tokens on this card. For every token spent, gain one of the following effects:
- Add +1 to the result of a roll involving the character
- Spend a Hope to cause the target of your interaction to mark a Stress
Clear unused tokens at the start of a long rest.
***
**Curse**: If you have a negative [[Reputation]] with a [[Faction]], members of that faction have a -1 penalty to their [[Attitude]] towards you.

title: (Level 2) Imposing Figure
content: When you make a roll to influence a character, you can spend a Hope to add either your Finesse, Agility or Strength to the roll.

title: (Level 2) Influencer ★
content:
---
einstructions: Include token controls. Include a button to add presence value's worth of tokens to this card. long rest refresh.
---

After a long rest, add a number of tokens to this card equal to your Presence. When you make a roll to influence a [[Character]] which has a positive [[Attitude]] towards you, you can spend a token from this card to add +2 to the result of your roll.
Clear unspent tokens at the start of a long rest.
***
**Curse**: After a long rest, choose an ally. That ally marks a Stress.

title: (Level 2) Inquisition ★
content:
Once per Scene, make a Spellcast Roll against a target within Very Close range. On a success, your questions press the person being interrogated, causing them to gain Stress for every lie told or unanswered question. If they acquire 2 Stress this way, the effect ends.
***
**Curse**: You bear a symbol of a religion. It can be any religion of Tidwell or one of your own creation. When speaking, you must show penitence to the deity or figurehead of that religion. If you speak without doing so, you must mark a Stress.

title: (Level 2) Marking Contraption
content: When you touch a creature, either via an attack or an interaction, you can mark a Stress to implant a small, trackable object on their person. If you spend a few moments concentrating on the object, you can discern its direction relative to you.

title: (Level 2) Mechanical Prowess
content:
**Grimoire**

In a pinch, you can create mechanical solutions to common problems.

**Fashion Lock**: Make a Spellcast Roll (15) on an object you can see. On a success, you can craft a lock which will only open unless conditions you specify are met. A [[Character]] can spend an hour of study time to break the lock if left alone with it.

**Mobility Device**: Make a Spellcast Roll (13). You fashion a device which gives you the ability to do one of the following:
- Fly up to Far range without a roll
- Climb up to Far range without a roll
- Swim up to Far range without a roll
Once the Mobility Device is used, it is destroyed.

**Sentry**: Make a Spellcast Roll (15). On a success, you create a device which alerts you to an activity of your description. The device alerts on any trigger activity within Close range of it. It is reasonably inconspicuous and relatively easy to hide.

title: (Level 2) Menacing
content: When you succeed with Fear on an attack roll against an adversary, they mark a Stress. If you mark three such attacks in one combat, remaining adversaries gain the _Unsteady_ condition. While _Unsteady_, a [[Character]] or [[Creature]] cannot attack without marking a Stress.

title: (Level 2) No Malarkey
content: When you interact with a [[Character]], you can spend a Hope to determine whether they are lying to you.

title: (Level 2) Odd Perspective
content:
When you are witness to a failed non-attack roll by an ally, you can spend a Hope to attempt the same action with one of the following bonuses:
- Gain a bonus to your roll equal to the difference between the roll's Difficulty and your ally's roll.
- Roll d4 using your Proficiency bonus. Take the highest result and have the target mark that much Stress.
- All allies within Close range can mark a Stress to clear a Hit Point.

title: (Level 2) Paranormal Attunement
content: When you spend a few minutes concentrating, you can spend a Hope and determine whether magic or supernatural entities have recently been in the area. You can make a Knowledge or Instinct roll to determine some specific details.

title: (Level 2) Parasitic Synergy
content: When an ally within Close range succeeds on a roll with Hope, you can make a Spellcast Roll (13). On a success, mark a Stress to gain 2 Hope.

title: (Level 2) Patently Unsafe
content: After you make a roll but before you know the result, you can mark any number of Hit Points and gain a +2 bonus to the result of the roll for each.

title: (Level 2) Reactive
content:
After a long rest, place a number of tokens equal to your Strength on this card. When you are the target of an incoming attack, you can spend a token before the result of the roll to take one of the following options:
- _Divert_: Make a Spellcast Roll against your target. On a success, the attack is instead made against a target within Very Close range.
- _Fortify:_ Until the attack is resolved, if you would mark a Hit Point, you mark a Stress instead.
- _Counter:_ After the attack resolves, you can immediately make an attack. Successes with Hope on this attack do not retain the spotlight.

title: (Level 2) Slippery ★
content:
---
einstructions: When this card is in the loadout, add a bonus to the character's evasion equal to their agility score
---

Gain a bonus to your Evasion equal to your Agility.
***
**Curse**: You develop a coating of your choice that is oily, watery, or slimy. For species that would normally have this type of coating, yours becomes particularly prodigious. This is off-putting to other [[Character|Characters]], and causes them to treat you with a -1 penalty to [[Attitude]] until you next gain [[Reputation]] with their [[Faction]].

title: (Level 2) Spectral Aspect ★
content:
Make a Spellcast Roll (15). Once per rest on a success, you and belongings on your person become _Semi-Corporeal_. While _Semi-Corporeal_ you gain a bonus to Evasion equal to your Proficiency, and can move through any solid object except for stone. This effect ends if you make an attack, cast another spell, or at the end of the scene.
***
**Curse**: You leave pieces of you behind when you transition between worlds. Whenever you use this ability, roll a d6. On a 1, describe how a belonging or a small piece of your body does not transition with you.

title: (Level 2) Striking Figure
content:
---
einstructions: Once roller is implemented, Add a button that spends one hope and adds strength to the next roll
---

When you make a presence roll, you can spend a Hope to add your Strength to the roll.

Additionally, once per rest when interacting with an otherwise neutral party, they gain a +2 bonus to their [[Attitude]] score towards you.

title: (Level 2) Takedown
content: When you succeed on an attack roll, you can mark a Stress to give you and your target the _Knocked Down_ condition. While _Knocked Down_, attack rolls the affected character makes are with Disadvantage. Attack rolls against the character gain Advantage. This condition lasts until the start of their next turn, or until another character takes an action to clear the condition for them.

title: (Level 2) Three Card Shuffle ★
content:
When you succeed a roll with Fear, you can spend 2 Hope to place a token on this card. Store the result of your successful roll, and re-roll a new result. You must use the new roll. 
You can spend the token to use that stored roll on another Action, Spellcast, or Trait roll.

At the end of this scene, clear all tokens on this card and mark 2 Stress for each token cleared this way.
***
**Curse**: You have an obviously rigged device somewhere on your person. If it is discovered by an authority or any [[Character]] who would object to you possessing this device, you must send this card to your Vault until your next rest.

title: (Level 2) Two Man Game
content: When another player engages in a scheme to deceive with you, you can mark two Stress for them to gain the benefits of a Domain Card that is currently in your Vault until the end of the scene.

title: (Level 3) Aposematic Display
content:
---
einstructions: Add token controls to this card. long rest refresh
---

You develop markings that advertise to potential adversaries that you are not worth the cost of attacking. After a long rest, place a number of tokens on this card equal to your Proficiency. When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you can spend a token from this card to enact one of the following effects:

- *Threat Display*: The attack roll against you gains Disadvantage.
- *Toxic Display*: When an adversary attacking you succeeds, after you have taken damage the adversary marks a Hit Point.
- *Müllerian Display:* Gain a bonus to your Evasion equal to your Presence until the attack resolves.
Clear unused tokens on this card at the start of a long rest.

title: (Level 3) Aura of Mercy ★
content:
**Aura**

Once per rest, you can activate an Aura of Mercy until the end of the scene. When the Aura of Mercy is active, any allies in Very Close range of you at the end of your turn can clear one Hit Point or Stress. Maintenance Difficulty (13).
***
**Curse**: While this card is in your loadout, your Auras are visible, as is their origin.

title: (Level 3) Aura of Peace ★
content:
**Aura**

Once per rest, you can activate an [[Aura]] of Peace until the end of the scene. When the Aura of Peace is active, any [[Character]] within Close range of you that chooses to make an attack must mark 2 Stress. Maintenance Difficulty (15).
***
**Curse**: If you activate this ability and then later attack in the same scene, you must mark 2 additional Stress.

title: (Level 3) Combined Systems
content:
**Grimoire**

You are especially adept at creating tools suited to aggressive situations.

**Deadfall Trap**: Make a Spellcast Roll (13) to create a physics-based trap within Melee range. On a success, a trap is created that will trigger based on a condition you describe. When this trap triggers, any [[Character|Characters]] or [[Creature|Creatures]] in Melee range take d20 damage using your Proficiency.

**Harpoon**: Make a Spellcast Roll against a target within Far range. On a success, deal d4 damage using your Proficiency and give your target the _Tethered_ condition with the origin as your current location. While _Tethered_, a creature or character cannot move except towards the origin of the tether. At the start of your turn, make a Strength roll with Advantage against the _Tethered_ creature. On a success, you can pull the creature one range distance closer.

title: (Level 3) Commune ★ ↔
content:
You have gained the ability to intuit details from the land of Tidwell itself. Once per long rest, make a Spellcast Roll (13). On a success with Hope, you may learn three details about the land within Far range. On a success with Fear, you may learn one detail. Any information that transpired within the space of the land can be learned, but is confined only to what the land understands. The land may know how many creatures passed through an area, but it would not know what they were doing there. On a failure, the land ignores you.
***
**Curse:** Your attunement to the land has come at the cost of your attunement to the actual beings that inhabit it. After using this ability, you become mute until the end of the Scene. If you have the [[(Level 3) Provocateur ★ ↔]] domain card, you can imbue a command into the land. It will do everything it can to comply with that command.

title: (Level 3) Conman ★ ↔
content:
You gain Advantage on all rolls to deceive [[Character|Characters]].

Additionally, once per session, you can spend 2 Hope to implant an idea in a character's mind after a few minutes of conversation. The character will believe this idea wholeheartedly until the end of the Scene.

**Curse**: There are wronged parties tracking you down to seek recompense. This manifests in a number of mildly negative and inconvenient ways when interacting with characters throughout Tidwell. If you have the [[(Level 3) Reality-Bent ★ ↔]] domain card, characters will forget they have been conned by you once the scene ends.

title: (Level 3) Crushing Intellect
content:
When you Prepare as a downtime move, add two tokens to this card. You can spend these tokens to gain one of the following benefits:
- Gain Advantage on a Knowledge roll
- After a failed roll with Fear, re-roll your Hope Die
- Recall information on a detail which you are not directly familiar without a Knowledge roll (e.g. the general layout of a city, the customs of a community, whether a plant is edible)

title: (Level 3) Everybody's Best Friend ★
content:
---
einstructions: 'Add a button to mark a Stress called "1 stress: re-roll on failure"'
---

Once per long rest, you can make a Spellcast Roll against a [[Character]] with whom you are currently speaking. On a success, that character gains a +1 bonus to their [[Attitude]] towards you for the duration of the scene. On a failure, you may mark a Stress to re-attempt the roll.
***
**Curse**: Whenever you use this ability, the weight of inauthenticity hits you by the next scene. Describe what steps you take to shake off this feeling or mark a Stress.

title: (Level 3) Fence
content:
During a long rest, you may make a special downtime move called _Fence_. When you take this move, choose one of the following effects:
- Purchase a stolen item at 75% of its base cost
- Sell a stolen item at 75% its base cost
- Remove a stolen item worth at least 1 Bag from your inventory and gain 1 [[Favor]] with a criminal [[Faction]] in the region

title: (Level 3) Immersion ★
content:
Make a Spellcast Roll against a target within Close range. On a success, spend a Hope to expose the target to a feature of your body or mind that momentarily breaks their brain. 

The target gains the _Immersed_ condition. While _Immersed_, they cannot take actions other than to move the nearest ally with the goal of touching them. If a target is touched in this manner, the new target gains the _Immersed_ condition and the original target clears the _Immersed_ condition and becomes immune to its effects. If no valid targets remain when a target gains the _Immersed_ condition, it does d12 damage using your Proficiency and ends immediately.
***
**Curse**: You gain a trinket that is roughly hand-sized and made of black stone. It sticks firmly to your person. When you use the ability on this Domain Card, it grows by 50%. As it gets larger, it introduces new complications determined by your GM. You can take a special downtime project called _Centering_ with a Countdown Tracker of 4. On completion of this special downtime project, the trinket returns to its original size.

title: (Level 3) Inspiring Presence
content: Once per rest, when you critically succeed on an attack roll, all allies attacking the same target gain Advantage on their next roll to attack the same target.

title: (Level 3) Intimidator ★ ↔
content:
---
einstructions: Add token controls to this card.
---

You develop an aura of imposition and threat. When attempting to frighten, intimidate, or negotiate via threat, gain Advantage. When you succeed at a roll in this manner, place a token on this card. You can spend a token to gain one of the following benefits:
- Give a [[Character]] or [[Creature]] the Vulnerable condition. This condition lasts until the first successful attack against them.
- Gain a bonus to a roll against an [[Adherent]] equal to double your negative reputation with their [[Faction]].

---
**Curse**: Your brutish nature isn't easily dismissed. If you interact with a Character in a way that isn't attempting to frighten, intimidate, or negotiating via threat, you gain Disadvantage. If you have the [[(Level 3) Prey ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, you gain 2 Hope whenever you fail a roll in this manner.

title: (Level 3) Manifesting ★
content:
Once per rest, you can manifest in reality a figment of your imagination. It can be of any shape or form you desire, but no larger than yourself. No matter the shape it takes, this manifestation is alive and has its own motivations and personality. The manifestation will remain active for the duration of the scene, at which point it will dissipate. While active, you can have the manifestation take any action reasonable for it to take, provided it agrees and wants to as well.

When this manifestation attacks an adversary, make a Spellcast Roll against it. On a success, it does d10 damage using your Proficiency and immediately dissipates.
***
**Curse**: You have difficulty keeping your imagination to yourself. Once per session, you must describe a scenario, situation or character you have invented to an ally or [[Character]]. The session after failing to do so, your GM will describe a manifestation that springs forth from your head, with motivations determined by your GM.

title: (Level 3) Mugger ★ ↔
content:
You have grown proficient in accosting [[Character|Characters]] and taking valuable items from them. As part of any attack, you can roll a d4. On a four, you may steal one item from their person even if the original attack failed. If the item has already been described, you gain that item. If you are seeking an item that hasn't been described, work with your GM to create one mundane item of your choosing.
***
**Curse**: You've stolen from the wrong person. At the GM's discretion, but no more than once after choosing this domain card, you will be informed of an organization that wants to force you to work for them or to imprison or harm you. They will take actions to effect this outcome. If you have the [[(Level 4) Diplomat ★ ↔]] domain card, they will instead ingratiate themselves to you. Should you encounter them, you may instead roll a d6 and recruit the result to do your bidding at a later date.

title: (Level 3) Performer ★ ↔
content:
---
einstructions: Add a short rest downtime move for this character called "Workshopping." When selected, add three tokens to this card.
---

You become exceptionally gifted in performance, and have the ability to command a crowd's attention. You gain an additional short rest downtime action called "Workshopping." Every time you engage in this action, put three tokens on this card. You can spend one of these tokens to gain any of the following effects:
- Add your Proficiency bonus to a Presence roll
- A [[Character]] treats you with a +1 bonus to [[Attitude]]
- Within the same scene, re-use the result of a Presence roll
***
**Curse**: Your sensibilities sometimes veer towards the "avant garde." Whenever you fail a Presence roll with Fear, the GM may introduce an unintended consequence. If you have the [[(Level 3) Prophet ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, you may spend a Hope to force this to be a positive effect.

title: (Level 3) Philosopher ★ ↔
content:
You have spent a long time thinking about the big questions in life.

After a long rest, place a number of tokens equal to the number of Study domain cards in your loadout and vault on this card.

When you would make a Knowledge, Presence, or Instinct trait roll, spend any number of tokens after the roll to gain a +1 bonus for each token spent.

Whenever you critically succeed in a trait roll using Knowledge, Presence, or Instinct, place a token on this card.

When you take a long rest, clear all unspent tokens.

---
**Curse**: You are seen as insufferable by many [[Character|Characters]], especially those who have a bias towards action. When a target for a roll to influence a character is declared, the GM will inform you if the curse is active. If it is, the roll will gain Disadvantage.

If you have the [[(Level 3) Scrapper ★ ↔]] domain card in your loadout, you may spend a token from this card to negate the Disadvantage granted by the Curse.

title: (Level 3) Predator ★ ↔
content:
You are considerably more cunning than most characters, and your traits that define you as a predator become more apparent. Attacks made against you gain Disadvantage so long as you have any Hope.
***
**Curse**: You frighten prey animals. [[Character|Characters]] and [[Creature|Creatures]] which would fear the creature you have come to resemble actively distrust you. Gain a -1 penalty to the [[Attitude]] of these creatures. They will seek to either hide or defend themselves against you. If you have the [[(Level 4) Safecracker ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, you may attempt to use you heightened senses against these characters. Make a Strength or Presence roll against a Difficulty of 15. On a success, the character will fearfully oblige you and you treat their attitude as if it were +3 for the duration of the scene. On a failure, they will attempt to flee immediately.

title: (Level 3) Preternatural Healing ★
content:
You can spend a Hope at any time to clear a Hit Point. If you use this ability to clear yourself even after your last Hit Point is marked. If you do so, you do not get a Death Move, and you do not take any scars as you would with the Avoid Death move.
***
**Curse**: If you have no Hope and you mark your last Hit Point, you must choose the Blaze of Glory death move.

title: (Level 3) Prey ★ ↔
content:
You become adept at avoiding conflicts. Whenever you make rolls in an attempt to avoid a fight, add your Agility to the result. If you fail a roll in this manner, you may mark a Stress to re-roll.

---
**Curse**: Predatory creatures are hell-bent on eating you. If a creature that would eat you feels that could, it will try do so by entering into conflict with you. If you have the [[(Level 4) Time-Bent ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, once per session when you fail to avoid this kind of conflict, you may rewind to the end of the previous scene.

title: (Level 3) Profundity ★
content:
---
einstructions: Add a button to roll a spellcast roll when a dice roller is implemented
---

Make a Spellcast Roll against all [[Character|Characters]] which can hear you. On a success, choose from one of the following effects:
- _Convince_: For the duration of the scene, affected characters gain a +1 bonus to [[Attitude]] towards you. 
- _Confuse_: Temporarily _Stun_ affected targets, causing them to mark a Stress. _Stunned_ characters cannot use reactions and cannot take other actions until this condition is cleared. This can affect allies.
- _Castigate_: Temporarily _Shame_ affected targets, causing them to mark a Stress. _Shamed_ characters gain a -2 penalty to their Difficulty.
***
**Curse**: Your newfound depth makes connecting with others a chore. When you use the abilities on this Domain Card, place a token on it. When you take a long rest before any downtime activities, mark a Stress for every token on this card. After the rest, clear all tokens.

title: (Level 3) Prophet ★ ↔
content:
**When chosen, declare the deity to whom you are bound as a prophet. This cannot be changed later.**

You gain an aura of power, and any adherent of a religion treats you more favorably. When interacting with adherents of an organized religion, you may add your tier to influence their actions or opinions.

Additionally, once per session you can make a Spellcast Roll against a [[Character]]. On a success, they abandon their current endeavor to follow you as a disciple. This effect ends when used again.

---
**Curse**: You bear the weight of responsibility as the mouthpiece of your deity. Once per session, at the GM's discretion, they may speak as your deity through you. If you have the [[(Level 3) Conman ★ ↔]] domain card, you may speak as your deity instead.

title: (Level 3) Provocateur ★ ↔
content:
You are adept at pushing the right buttons to manufacture action where it did not exist previously. Once per Scene, you can say a few words or take an action towards another character. Make a Spellcast Roll (13). On a success with Hope, the target will endeavor to carry out an action of your choosing until the end of the Scene. On a success with Fear, the target will endeavor to carry out the described action unless they mark 2 Stress.

**Curse**: Your time spent manipulating others has left you cynical and hardened. Your cynicism has manifested in a hypervigilance. During a short rest, you can only take one downtime action. If you have the [[(Level 4) Sacrifice ★ ↔]] domain card, the sense of inevitability has brought you peace. Instead, during a short rest you may take three downtime actions.

title: (Level 3) Psychic ★ ↔
content:
Once per scene, when you roll with Hope, you may "save" the result of your Duality Dice. When you do so, add a number of tokens to this card equal to the value of your Hope die, and then re-roll your Hope die. Those tokens can be spent to add a +1 modifier to subsequent rolls.

At the end of the scene, clear all unused tokens.
***
**Curse**: Your extrasensory perception has come at cost to your regular senses. Any roll which involves using one of your senses directly gains Disadvantage. If you have the [[(Level 3) Sharpshooter ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, ignore the Hidden condition when making attacks or Spellcast Rolls against your target.

title: (Level 3) Reality-Bent ★ ↔
content:
You have become detached from the reality around you. Once per session, work with your GM to change a minor detail about the world. If this cannot be granted, you instead place a token on this card. You may spend this token at any time to grant yourself an automatic success on one roll.

At the end of session, clear all unspent tokens.

**Curse**: The world of Tidwell is actively rejecting you. You are hunted by an entity within Tidwell who seeks to expel you from the world at all costs. You will eventually encounter this entity, and it will succeed, causing your character to no longer be playable. If you have the [[(Level 3) Universalist ★ ↔]] Domain Card, you have an opportunity to negotiate with this entity. Roll against an 18 Difficulty. On a success, the entity will agree and leave you forever.

title: (Level 3) Scrapper ★ ↔
content:
You are a practiced and proficient fighter.

When you mark a Stress, place a token on this card. On your next successful attack, gain a +3 bonus to your damage for each token on this card, then clear all tokens.

---
**Curse**: You've developed a bit of shell shock from repeated beatings. Any time you mark a Hit Point, gain a Stress. When this happens, if you have the [[(Level 4) Animalistic ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, you can immediately make an attack.

title: (Level 3) Shadow ★ ↔
content:
You have become accustomed to existing unseen.

You automatically gain the _Hidden_ condition in the dark until you dismiss it explicitly or via actions.
When you succeed on an attack with Advantage against a [[Creature]] or [[Character]], spend a Hope to deal additional d8 physical damage using your Proficiency.
***
**Curse**: Your adherence to the dark has left you ill-conditioned for polite society. You take on a pallid appearance, and rolls to influence [[Character]]s are done with Disadvantage. If you have the [[(Level 4) Possessed ★ ↔]] domain card, your appearance and mannerisms are treated as a novelty. Once per session, you may ask a [[Favor]] of a friendly character. That character will do their best within reason to accommodate that request.

title: (Level 3) Sharpshooter ★ ↔
content:
You are particularly deadly at range. After a long rest, add a number of tokens to this card equal to your Instinct. You can spend these tokens in any of the following ways:
- Add +5 to an attack roll against a target at Far range
- Add +5 damage to a damage roll against a target at Far range
- When no adversaries are at Close or Melee range, gain the Hidden condition. Attacks made from this Hidden condition gain Advantage.

When you take a long rest, clear any unspent tokens.

---
**Curse**: You've become extremely vulnerable at Close range. When you take damage from an adversary in Close or Melee range, treat it as if it hit at a damage threshold one higher (e.g. Minor => Major).

If you have the [[(Level 4) Gifted ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, you may add your Knowledge to your armor score.

title: (Level 3) Sudden Step ★
content:
Spend a Hope to move up to Close range. You may use this ability at any point, including when you are the target of an attack or effect.
***
**Curse**: After using this ability, it takes a moment for your senses to readjust. Until the end of the scene, your next roll is made with Disadvantage.

title: (Level 3) Taking It on the Chin
content: When more than half your Hit Points are marked, add your Strength to your Evasion.
