filePath: Domains\Violence\(Level 3) Taking It on the Chin.md

title: (Level 3) Teleport ★
content:
Once per long rest, you can instantly teleport yourself and any number of willing targets within Close range to a place you’ve been before. Choose one of the following options, then make a **Spellcast Roll (16)**:

- If you know the place very well, gain a +3 bonus.
- If you’ve visited the place frequently, gain a +1 bonus.
- If you’ve visited the place infrequently, gain no modifier.
- If you’ve only been there once, gain a −2 penalty.

On a success, you appear where you were intending to go. On a failure, you appear off course, with the range of failure determining how far off course.
***
**Curse**: Your violation of causality does not go unnoticed by the supernatural forces responsible for Tidwell. When you use this ability, put a token on this card and roll a d12. If the result is lower than the number of tokens on this card, clear all tokens and inform your GM.

title: (Level 3) Universalist ★ ↔
content:
You create works which inspire others even without the use of language. You can attempt to influence the actions of characters with whom you share no common language or culture. Describe the actions you take to effect communication with these entities, and treat these [[Creature]]s as [[Character]]s.
***
**Curse**: You adopt odd mannerisms and patterns of speech as your thinking about language has changed significantly. You often invent words on the spot and use them as if others should understand you. When using language to communicate ideas, you may take penalties at the GM's discretion. If you have the [[(Level 3) Commune ★ ↔]] domain card, your oddities are seen as charming instead of off-putting, and you instead gain advantages at the GM's discretion.

title: (Level 4) Animalistic ★ ↔
content:
You have developed a deep affinity for the [[Creature]]s of Tidwell. You may speak with [[Creature]]s as if they were [[Character]]s. This does not extend to [[Etherotaxia]].

---
**Curse**: You are alienated from the social norms of society. When interacting with Characters for the first time, you must mark a Stress. If you have the [[(Level 4) Stalwart ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, you may spend a Hope to learn one detail about the Character based on information derived from your animalistic senses.

title: (Level 4) Aura of Sacrifice ★
content:
**Aura**

Once per rest, you can activate Aura of Sacrifice. While active, allies in Very Close range gain an additional Action Roll on their turn. You do not get an Action Roll, allowing you to only take actions which require no rolls. Maintenance Difficulty (15).

In addition, if a player's roll result is Fear, you may dismiss the Aura immediately to change the result to be with Hope.
***
**Curse**: You develop a deep resentment for those using you. While this Aura is active, you must mark a Stress for every player in range of this Aura for more than two turns.

title: (Level 4) Burglar ★ ↔
content:
You have become adept at getting into places that are otherwise barred to you. When attempting to enter into any space which has been secured against intruders, gain Advantage. If you succeed at one of these rolls, place a token on this card. You may spend a token from this card to add +1 to the result of a roll to enter into any space which has been secured against intruders.

---
**Curse**: You are too good at your job. Either you attract the attention of an organization that wants to force you to work for them, or a squadron that seeks justice for something you've stolen. They will pursue you in this campaign until dealt with directly by your character. If you have the [[(Level 4) Orator ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, you gain a means to call on the pursuing organization directly, and attempt to set them against an adversary. Work with your GM to determine benefits of this action.

title: (Level 4) Charlatan ★ ↔
content:
You gain the ability to cast supernatural spells. This spellcasting comes from your ability to deceive reality into believing it is something that it is not. If you have the [[(Level 7) Weird-Touched]] domain card in your loadout, when you use your magic against a [[Character]] or [[Creature]] that would not cause damage, gain Advantage on any Spellcast Roll.

Gain a number of tokens equal to your Level. You may spend these tokens to cast spells. These spells are of your own creation and not limited by existing spells on other domains. Work with your GM by describing the spell so that they can set an appropriate Difficulty to create the spell. You may spend additional tokens to reduce the Difficulty by one per token spent.

---

**Curse**: Your magic isn't magic at all, but rather unmaking and remaking the reality of Tidwell to suit your purposes. This has angered one of the deities of Tidwell, and they and their devotees have a vendetta against you. If you have the [[(Level 3) Mugger ★ ↔]] Domain Card, you have instead stolen this ability from the deity, and a sect of adherents of that faith now worship you instead.

title: (Level 4) Curse-stricken ★ ↔
content:
After a long rest, gain a number of tokens equal to your level plus the amount of domain cards with a **Curse** feature in your current loadout, including this one. 

When casting a spell that would not harm a [[Creature]] or a [[Character]], you can spend a token to reduce the Difficulty of the Spellcast Roll by 5.

When casting a spell that would harm a Creature or a Character, you can spend any number of tokens after the roll to gain a +2 bonus to the result of this roll for each token spent.
***
**Curse**: You are laden with cursed items, and find them harder to shed than most. You cannot freely change domain cards with a **Curse** feature during downtime. If you have the [[(Level 3) Shadow ★ ↔]] Domain Card, after a long rest, choose a Level 4 or below domain card with a **Curse**. You may ignore the effects of the **Curse** until your next long rest.

title: (Level 4) Diplomat ★ ↔
content:
---
einstructions: include a button to add Proficiency value's worth of tokens to this card
---

You're an adept and practiced interlocutor, and have gained the ability to influence others effectively via communication. Place an amount of tokens equal to your Proficiency on this card. You may spend a token to gain one of the following benefits:

- When influencing another [[Character]], you may re-roll a duality die of your choosing before the result of the roll is known.
- Gain Advantage on a roll to de-escalate a situation
- When influencing another Character, swap a success with Fear to a success with Hope.
- Once per scene, gain a +1 bonus to your [[Reputation]] score with a target [[Faction]].

Additionally, when interacting with an unfamiliar culture, describe a period of observation you undertake. At the conclusion of this period, you may spend a Hope to learn key details about that culture.
***
**Curse**: Your skill at settling differences is intuited by other Characters. At the GM's discretion, roughly once per session, you will be approached by these parties to settle disputes. If you have 2 or more Stress, all rolls to handle this interaction will be done with Disadvantage. If you fail to resolve the dispute, mark a Stress. If you have the [[(Level 4) Curse-stricken ★ ↔]] domain card, a success will clear all Stress.

title: (Level 4) Dirty Fighter ★ ↔
content:
**Grimoire**

**Blind**: On a successful melee attack, spend a Hope to give the condition **Blind** to your target.

**Sucker Punch**: On a successful attack using Advantage, after rolling for damage, spend 2 Hope to double the total damage your target receives.

**Shell up**: On a successful melee attack targeting you, mark a Stress and gain a bonus to your damage threshold equal to your Proficiency. This effect ends after damage is resolved.

---

**Curse**: Your reputation proceeds you. When you are present, all attempts by you or an ally to de-escalate a situation gain Disadvantage. Additionally, on a successful roll to de-escalate a situation where you are present, you must mark a Stress. 

If you have the [[(Level 4) Fop ★ ↔]] domain card in your loadout, when a roll to de-escalate a situation succeeds, gain a Hope instead of marking a Stress.

title: (Level 4) Dish It Out
content:
**Grimoire**

**Roundhouse Smash**: Make an attack roll against all enemies within Very Close range. On a success, targets take your normal weapon damage and an additional d10 physical damage using your Proficiency. 

**Vicious Strike**: Make an attack roll with your primary weapon. On a success, gain a Hope to give your target the condition _Trauma_. Before a target affected with _Trauma_ takes any action, they take the damage received on the original attack. _Trauma_ is cleared when the affected target takes an action to clear it or they have received damage from _Trauma_ twice.

title: (Level 4) Excel in Adversity ★ ↔
content:
When you interact with an [[Adherent]] of a [[Faction]] with whom your party has a negative [[Reputation]], you can spend a Hope to gain a bonus to your next roll equal to double the penalty with that Faction.
***
**Curse**: You gain a brand, mark, trinket, or scar that indicates to others you have made an enemy of a powerful faction. Others are wary to trust you because of this. You gain a -1 penalty to [[Attitude]] for your first interaction with adherents of a Faction you're meeting for the first time. If you have the [[(Level 3) Performer ★ ↔]] domain card in your loadout, you can spend a token from that card to ignore this curse.

title: (Level 4) Fop ★ ↔
content:
---
einstructions: When this card is in loadout, give character a +1 bonus to Presence. Add token controls to this card. Add a special downtime move (short and long) called "Take Tea" that when chosen gains two hope and sets current stress to 0
---

You gain a +1 bonus to your Presence as long as this card is in your loadout, and gain Advantage on rolls to de-escalate a situation. If you succeed at de-escalating a situation, place a token on this card.

You can spend tokens on this card to take a special downtime move called _Take Tea_. You can _Take Tea_ with party members who are using the _Prepare_ downtime move. You gain all the same benefits of _Preparing Together_ as well as clearing all Stress.
***
**Curse**: When you mark a Hit Point or an Armor slot, you must mark a Stress. If you have the [[(Level 3) Philosopher ★ ↔]] domain card in your loadout, any Stress marked this way is cleared at the end of the scene.

title: (Level 4) Gifted ★ ↔
content:
Talents inherent in you since birth are awakening. You gain the ability to cast supernatural spells.

These spells are of your own creation and description. Work with your GM by describing the spell so that they can set an appropriate Difficulty to create the spell.

After a long rest, place a number of tokens on this card equal to your Level. After you cast a spell, you can spend tokens to recall a previously crafted spell, or to add one of these effects to it.

- **Destructive**: Deal d8 damage to the target using your Proficiency. At Tier 3, this increases to d10. At Tier 4, this increases to d12.
- **Deafening**: [[Character|Characters]] and [[Creature|Creatures]] within Close range of the target cannot hear until the end of the Scene. 
- **Blinding**: Characters and Creatures within Close range of the target cannot see until the end of the Scene.
- **Opportune**: Characters and Creatures affected by this spell choose to clear one Stress or one Hit Point.
---
**Curse**: Your spellcasting is more an "art" than a science. Whenever you use your spellcasting, roll a d4. If the result is 1, the GM describes an unintended consequence of their choosing. If you have the [[(Level 4) Physicist ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, roll a d10 instead of a d4.

title: (Level 4) Grace Note
content:
---
einstructions: Include a button to add a hope
---

Whenever you are involved in a Tag Team Roll initiated by another player, gain a Hope. Tag Team Rolls initiated by you cost 2 Hope instead of 3.

title: (Level 4) Heretic ★ ↔
content:
You have made an enemy of an order of religion, and sought protection from its machinations. This may be any [[Faction]] with whom you have a positive [[Reputation]]. Gain a +1 improvement to the [[Attitude]] of [[Adherent]]s in factions that oppose the faction you have chosen.

---
**Curse**: You develop a physical brand on your body that tells others of your hereticism. Characters who hold religious beliefs are suspicious of you and will seek to avoid contact with you. During the first scene where you encounter adherents of a new faction, you have a -1 penalty to Attitude. If you have the [[(Level 3) Intimidator ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, you gain Advantage on any rolls to intimidate these characters.

title: (Level 4) Inventor ★ ↔
content:
You gain an innate understanding of an advanced engineering concept, and create a device unique to your character. Work with your GM to outline the rules of this device. After a long rest, place a number of tokens equal to your Knowledge on this card. It may be used to enact one of the following effects:
- One of two effects chosen by you and agreed to by the GM
- Gain the benefits of a Short Rest action immediately

When you take a long rest, clear any unspent tokens on this card.

---
**Curse**: You struggle to think about the world in any other way than via analysis, and the Divine escapes you. When interacting with these [[Adherent|Adherents]] of a religious [[Faction]], you gain Disadvantage. If you have the [[(Level 4) Mad ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, once per rest you may treat a success involving [[Etherotaxia]] as a critical success instead.

title: (Level 4) Mad ★ ↔
content:
You gain insight into the world that others couldn't possibly understand. Once per session, you may ask a question of the GM directly about the world of Tidwell or any of its inhabitants. The GM must answer honestly. This answer is only delivered to you.

---
**Curse**: Your grasp on this world is tenuous at best. once per session, the GM may take control of your character for one action or interaction. This action cannot cause you to lose your last Hit Point. If you have the [[(Level 5) Investigator ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, once per session, you may ask a question of the GM directly about the world of Tidwell or any of its inhabitants. The GM must answer honestly. This is distinct from all other effect limitations, (i.e. does not count against the "once per session" limit of the base "Mad" ability).

title: (Level 4) Opportunistic ★ ↔
content:
Once per rest when an adversary within Very Close range fails a roll, you may spend a Hope to make a roll against them with Advantage. Treat all successes on this roll as a _critical success_.
***
**Curse**: You are prone to long bouts of fasting, often through no choice of your own. The world intervenes in subtle ways to keep you from finding things easily. When you use this ability and it results in a _critical success_, your next failure is treated as a _critical failure_, and will give the GM two Fear. If you have the [[(Level 3) Psychic ★ ↔]] domain card in your loadout when this failure happens, roll a d6 and on a result of 6 you ignore the cost.

title: (Level 4) Orator ★ ↔
content:
---
einstructions: add a button to gain two tokens.
---

You are an adept speaker, and you effectively sway opinions on entire groups. When your [[Reputation]] with a [[Faction]] improves, gain two tokens on this card, and you can spend any number of these tokens to earn [[Favor|Favors]] with that faction.
***
**Curse**: Not everyone is easily swayed by pretty words. When you gain reputation with factions of this sort, the group may ask a favor of you. If you do not honor that favor, gain a -1 penalty to the attitude of adherents of that faction. If you have the [[(Level 3) Predator ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, you gain an additional favor upon completing this request.

title: (Level 4) Physicist ★ ↔
content:
You deeply understand the physical world around you, and can apply this fundamental understanding in interesting ways. After a long rest, place a number of tokens on this card equal to your Knowledge. You can spend one of these tokens to enact one of the following abilities:
- Roll your Knowledge trait in place of another trait
- Add your Knowledge to your Proficiency for a damage roll
- Learn one piece of information about the environment around you without a roll

Clear unspent tokens at the beginning of a long rest.

---
**Curse**: Your knowledge is seen as heretical by the majority of religious communities within Tidwell due to your attempts to explain occurrences best left to religion. Religious adherents have a worsened attitude towards you. If you have the [[(Level 4) Heretic ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, you may spend one of the tokens on this card to dismiss a religious adherent from the scene.

title: (Level 4) Pig in a Poke ★ ↔
content:
Once per rest, after you have sold or given away an item since your last rest, you may make a Spellcast Roll targeting the recipient. On a success, you may replace the item you parted with by swapping it for any item you are currently carrying.
***
**Curse**: Every time you do this, it results in a deep animosity by the fooled party. They take a -2 penalty to their [[Attitude]] towards you in addition to any other non-curse effects may worsen their attitude. If you have the [[(Level 4) Righteous Fire ★ ↔]] domain card in your loadout, you can give the target of this ability the _Blazing_ condition in addition to its normal effects.

title: (Level 4) Pigeon Drop
content: Once per session, when you are caught in a deception, you can mark 3 Stress to invent an outside authority who will verify your story. Until the end of the scene, the target of this Ability will believe a lie they have previously seen through. While this will not hold up to further scrutiny, it will work to deceive all but the most fastidious investigators.

title: (Level 4) Possessed ★ ↔
content:
You are a conduit for the [[Etherotaxia]] of Tidwell. These entities possess unique capabilities which you do not. 

Once per session, you can activate this card's ability to choose a Domain Card from **any** Domain. If you meet its level requirements, you may put it in your loadout. At the end of the Scene, remove the selected card from your Loadout.

**Curse**: The entity possessing you has different aims and goals than you do, and does not communicate directly with you about those goals. After a scene in which you activated this card's ability, you must roll a d4. On a 1, the entity possessing you takes over and pursues its goals. You must take actions towards satisfying the goal the GM describes for you. If you have the [[(Level 4) Writer ★ ↔]] Domain Card, you can describe a scene where you attempt communication with the entity possessing you, after which you must make a Spellcast Roll (15) to convince the entity to abandon its goals in favor of your own.

title: (Level 4) Righteous Fire ★ ↔
content:
Once per scene make an attack with your primary weapon against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal your normal weapon damage plus d8 additional damage using your Proficiency. The target gains the _Blazing_ condition. While _Blazing_, attacks on the target gain Advantage. 

If the target acts while _Blazing_, they must take an extra 2d8 magic damage if they are still _Blazing_ at the end of their action.
***
**Curse**: The anger that comes with your strong sense of justice can sometimes be hard to control. Roll a d6 after using this ability. On a 1, you also gain the _Blazing_ condition. If you have the [[(Level 4) Excel in Adversity ★ ↔]] domain card in your loadout, every time you make a successful attack against a character while _Blazing_ your target gains the _Blazing_ condition as well.

title: (Level 4) Sacrifice ★ ↔
content:
You are destined to be sacrificed as part of a ritual to a higher power. You may choose the higher power to whom you are sacrificed. You gain an aura of inevitability, and a number of tokens equal to your Proficiency. These tokens are refreshed at the beginning of the session.

You may spend these tokens for one of the following benefits:

- Gain Advantage on any roll to endure a psychological effect
- Clear a Stress
- Halve all incoming damage of one type until the end of the Scene (round down)

---

**Curse**: There is an hour and a day unknown to you where devotees of the higher power will expect your death in its honor. They seek your capture and will pursue you to that effect. If they manage to capture you, you will be delivered to those administering your sacrifice. If you have the [[(Level 4) Charlatan ★ ↔]] Domain Card, there is no pursuing group. Instead, you must complete a major downtime project (6 ticks) to manufacture evidence of a pursuing group. While the project is active, you must Work on this Project during a long rest or clear all tokens on this card.

title: (Level 4) Safecracker ★ ↔
content:
You are preternaturally gifted with locks of all types. When dealing with any magical or mundane lock, gain Advantage when attempting to bypass it. If you successfully bypass the lock, place a token on this card. 
You may spend a token from this card to gain any of the following effects:
- Gain Advantage on an Instinct roll
- Gain a +5 bonus to the result of an attempt to bypass a device securing an area
- Ignore a target's Hidden condition until the end of the scene
***
**Curse**: Your focus on small technical details sometimes causes you to miss the big social ones. Presence rolls to influence a [[Character]] are done with Disadvantage. If you have the [[(Level 4) Pig in a Poke ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, once per rest when you fail a Presence roll in this manner you can re-roll your attempt without Disadvantage.

title: (Level 4) Stalwart ★ ↔
content:
You are built to endure even the worst physical stresses. Once per scene, you may reduce the severity of incoming damage to yourself by one threshold.

---
**Curse**: When an ally within Very Close range would mark a Hit Point, you must mark 1 Stress and take it instead. If this causes you to mark your last hit point, you must choose the Blaze of Glory death move. If you have the [[(Level 4) Opportunistic ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, once per scene when you take damage in this way, you can deal half of the incoming damage back to its source immediately.

title: (Level 4) Time-Bent ★ ↔
content:
You no longer sit nicely in the confines of linear time, and you find you can move through it with some difficulty. Once per Scene, you may choose any [[Character]], [[Creature]] or [[Player]] to reroll a result.

---
**Curse**: You find it hard to stay stuck in time when you need to. Whenever you take a Long Rest, roll a d4. On a 1, you may only take Short Rest Actions. If you have the [[(Level 4) Inventor ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, after a long rest, add a number of tokens to that Domain Card equal to your Instinct.

title: (Level 4) Writer ★ ↔
content:
You have the ability to craft a narrative, persuasive, or expository piece of writing with expertise. During a long rest, you may work on a special project to influence a whole [[Faction]] with whom you share a common language. Describe the goal of your writing, the speed at which you aim for the influence to happen, and work with your GM to set the necessary ticks for completion. This can affect [[Reputation]] with that faction.

Only one special project may be active at a time. Inactive projects no longer provide the benefits originally granted unless explicitly stated otherwise by the GM.
***
**Curse**: You struggle with internal demons. On selecting this domain card, work with your GM to craft a custom condition relating to this affliction. Once per session, the GM can spend a Fear to force you to succumb to that Condition. If you have the [[(Level 4) Dirty Fighter ★ ↔]] Domain Card, you instead revel in this affliction. Work with your GM to positively change the effects of this condition accordingly.

title: (Level 5) Ambitious Score
content:
Gain a long rest downtime move called _Heist_. Roll a d6, and gain that many tokens on this card. During any rest, you can spend those tokens to receive any of the following stolen items:

**1 token**: Common Item/Consumable
**4 tokens**: Tier 1 Armor/Weapon or Uncommon Item/Consumable
**7 tokens**: Tier 2 Armor/Weapon or Rare Item/Consumable
**10 tokens**: Tier 3 Armor/Weapon or Legendary Item/Consumable
**13 tokens**: Tier 4 Armor/Weapon

title: (Level 5) Champion
content:
When this card first enters your load out, choose a [[Faction]]. When you take an action which aligns with your chosen faction's [[Primary Goals]], you gain a bonus to ALL rolls equal to your [[Reputation]] with the chosen Faction. 

You gain a special downtime project called _Shifting Allegiance_ with a Countdown Tracker of 6. When this project completes, you may choose a new faction for the benefits of this card and remove the old faction (if it exists).

title: (Level 5) Credo
content:
You gain a one-time special downtime project called _Scripture_ with a Countdown Tracker of 4. Once you start this project, you can gain a permanent +1 bonus to either you Presence, Instinct or Knowledge score. When this project completes, you can choose to gain one of the following effects permanently:
- Gain 2 [[Reputation]] bonuses. You can distribute these bonuses to any [[Faction]] whose current score Reputation is 0 or better.
- Create a new [[Faction]]. [[Adherent]]s see you as the [[Figurehead]] of this faction.
- Replace this card in your loadout with **(Level 5) Aura of Destiny** permanently. This transformation can only be triggered by completing the _Scripture_ project, not selected during loadout creation.
  
  **(Level 5) Aura of Destiny**
  
  **Aura**
  
  Once per rest, you can activate Aura of Destiny. While active, allies in Close range cannot mark their last Hit Point. If a character were to mark their last Hit Point, you must mark a Stress instead. If you have no more Stress to mark, this aura effect ends immediately.

%% back-end: Aura of Destiny should be a non-selectable domain card that can only enter a character's loadout by completing the Scripture project on this card. %%

title: (Level 5) Disciple
content: You pledge yourself fully to a [[Faction]] that exists in Tidwell. You gain a +2 bonus to [[Attitude]] with all [[Adherent]]s of that faction. If you violate one of the [[Primary Goals]] of that faction, you lose this bonus until you undertake a special project called _Penance_. Work with your GM to determine the details of that project should you need it.

title: (Level 5) Doomsday Device
content:
You gain a special downtime project called _Doomsday Device_ with a Countdown Tracker of 6. While this project is underway, gain a +1 bonus to your Knowledge, Presence, or Instinct. When the project completes, choose from one of the following effects:
- Gain a single-use weapon that does d20+5 damage using the sum of all party members' Proficiency scores.
- Gain a single-use piece of equipment. For one scene, all characters that are within Very Close range of the device will not take damage as long as they take no actions.

This project can only be completed once.

title: (Level 5) Endemia
content: Once per rest, you can choose a location, region, or geography and give yourself the _Endemic_ condition. This allows you to ignore any effects inherent in the setting. For instance, an _endemic_ condition for a lake would allow you to breathe underwater, or an _endemic_ condition for [[Forestdown]] would allow you to ignore the effects of [[Creep]]. In addition, [[Creature|Creatures]] and [[Character|Characters]] that are native to the chosen location, region or geography treat you with a +1 bonus to [[Attitude]].

title: (Level 5) Infiltrate
content:
Gain a special downtime project called _Infiltration_ with a Countdown Tracker of 4. While this project is active, gain a +2 bonus to your Finesse. When the project completes, gain one of the following permanent effects:
- Choose a [[Faction]]. You gain a new downtime move for long rests called _Gather Intel_. When you take this move, roll a number of d4 dice equal to your Proficiency and take the highest result. You may ask that many questions of the GM about that faction and its movements.
- Gain detailed information about forthcoming movements of your adversaries. You learn of the next tick on any long-term countdown trackers that are currently running.

title: (Level 5) Intelligence Network
content:
---
einstructions: Add a special long rest downtime move called "Establish Contacts." Add explanation text from this card to that downtime move.
---

You gain a special long rest downtime move called _Establish Contacts_. When you undertake this project, roll a number of d4s equal to your Tier. You may ask the GM a number of questions equal to the highest result, each about specific information on a topic of your choice, as long as that information is known to a Faction you are currently in contact with or to the inhabitants of your current region.

title: (Level 5) Investigator ★ ↔
content:
You become a veritable savant at sleuthing. After a long rest, gain a number of tokens on this card equal to your Proficiency. You can spend a token to enact any of the following effects:
- Come from another angle: Add an additional trait to a trait roll when investigating.
- Interrogation: Gain a +5 bonus to a roll used to question a [[Character]]
- Dogged Pursuit: Move up to Far distance without it counting as your action for that turn

Clear unspent tokens on this card when you take a long rest.

---
**Curse**: Your new abilities and mannerisms lead others to believe you might be involved with enforcing laws or regulations. Characters invested in avoiding attention (criminals, heretics, etc) have a worsened attitude towards you and will refuse to cooperate with even the most simple requests. If you have the [[(Level 4) Burglar ★ ↔]] Domain Card in your loadout, you may spend a token from this card to gain Advantage on a roll involving a character otherwise affected by this [[Curse]].

title: (Level 5) Logomancy ★
content:
---
einstructions: Add a button to add the "exhausted" condition to the character.
---

Your words are imbued with the power to fundamentally change how a [[Creature]] or [[Character]] thinks. Make a Spellcast Roll against a creature that can hear you. On a success, you can speak a thought into a target's mind which they will adopt wholeheartedly as their own. Only one such thought can be maintained at a time. This effect lasts until you dismiss it, you implant another thought in this or another target, or the target is incapacitated.
***
**Curse**: Your speech is powerful and is no longer something you can responsibly use sparingly. Spoken words are difficult for you. Mark a Stress any time you speak more than five words in a thought. If you speak more than three sentences to any one character during a scene, you gain the _Exhausted_ condition until your next rest. While _exhausted_, you may only move or take one action on your turn. Any roll that requires you to speak while _exhausted_ is made with Disadvantage.

title: (Level 5) Masterwork
content:
---
einstructions: Add a button to create a project called "masterwork" with 5 ticks to completion. When Masterwork is complete, have player select one of the three options on this card, and on confirmation of selection, apply the benefits to the character sheet.
---

You gain a special, one-time downtime activity called _Masterwork_ with a Countdown Tracker of 5. While this project is underway, you gain a +1 bonus to your Presence, Instinct, or Knowledge. Once complete, select one of the following permanent benefits:
- _Hearts and Minds_: Place a number of tokens on this card equal to your Tier. You can spend these tokens to gain a +1 bonus to [[Reputation]] with any [[Faction]] whose current score is -1 or greater.
- _Authority_: Choose Presence, Instinct, or Knowledge. You gain a +2 bonus to the chosen trait, and allies gain a +1.
- _Screed_: Choose a Faction. That Faction takes a –1 Reputation penalty with every Faction whose current Reputation with them is +0 or higher.

This domain card does not need to remain in your loadout for these benefits to persist.

title: (Level 5) Metamorphosis
content: You gain a special downtime project called _Metamorphosis_ with a Countdown Tracker of 2. This project may be undertaken multiple times. When you complete this project, you gain the _Altered_ condition which grants you a key feature or ability of a [[Creature]] you have encountered previously. You may have two active _Altered_ conditions active at one time. When you complete a _Metamorphosis_ project with two already active _Altered_ conditions, choose one to dismiss.

title: (Level 5) Slithy Toves
content: You become immune to effects which alter perception from sources other than your domain cards. Once per session, you may make a Spellcast Roll (15). On a success, you can share your perception of a thing with all allies within Far range. Once an ally sees the object as you see it, they retain a perfect memory of your perception even after the effect ends.

title: (Level 5) Sow Discontent
content:
---
einstructions: Add a button to create a project called "Sow Discontent" with 6 ticks to completion. When complete, prompt the player with the completion benefits from this card.
---

You gain a special downtime project called _Sow Discontent_ with a Countdown Tracker of 6. Choose a target [[Faction]] with whose [[Adherent|Adherents]] you have direct contact. When this downtime project is complete, a portion of the targeted faction splinters into a new Faction. Work with your GM to set the new [[Primary Goals]] of this faction, aligned against at least one primary goal of the original faction. 

Only one _Sow Discontent_ project may be active at any time.

Your party starts with +1 [[Reputation]] with this new faction. Every time you help achieve one of its primary goals, gain +1 reputation with this faction. When all of its primary goals are met, this new faction replaces the original [[Faction]] and the _Sow Discontent_ project ends. This may happen with or without your assistance, but no reputation is gained unless the party is directly involved.

title: (Level 5) Theoretical Magic
content:
Gain a special long rest downtime move called _Magical Research_. When you complete a long rest and use this move, place a token on this card.

You can spend these tokens to alter an existing spell, either created  via [[(Level 4) Charlatan ★ ↔]] and [[(Level 4) Gifted ★ ↔]] or an existing spell within the Weird domain. You can spend any number of tokens to gain one of the following permanent alterations:

- **1 Token** _Long Range_: Increase the range increment of the spell
- **2 Tokens** _Afflicting_: Choose an already defined condition. This spell afflicts that condition on its target on a success.
- **4 Tokens** _Massive_: Replace damage dice on the spell with d20s.

Alternatively, you can propose an alteration to your GM who will set a cost for the requested alteration.

title: (Level 5) Wrecking Machine
content: When you have marked all but 1 Hit Point, you can use d20s for all damage dice.

title: Burning Creep
content:
*Something here has lit the Creep on fire. The thick black acrid smoke is damaging to the lungs, and prolonged exposure may lead to onset of Creep sickness. The agitated Creep has led to a greater chance of encountering enraged Etherotaxia*
**Tone**: Violent, mournful
**Tier 1**
**Type**: Hazard
**Difficulty**: 10

**Potential Adversaries**:
- [[Madanikuputukas (Swarm)]]
- [[Kuputuka]]
- [[Creep-Bound Etherotaxic Entity 1]]
- [[Creep-Bound Etherotaxic Entity 2]]

**Features**
**Thick Smoke - Passive**
While in Burning Creep, players must make Instinct rolls to travel more than Close Range.
_The thick black smoke obscures everything beyond a distance, making navigation impossible._

**Toxic Smoke - Passive**
While in Burning Creep, players take d6 magical damage before they make any action roll. This cannot be reduced by Armor.
_The toxic fumes burn and scar your tissues and the acidic accretions etch even hard carapaces. Staying too long in this is a dangerous proposition for all but the most hardy creatures._

**Etherotaxic Defender**
Once per scene, you can summon an enraged Creep-Bound Etherotaxic entity. Spend 2 Fear to have an Etherotaxic creature added to the encounter appropriate to the party's tier.
_Disturbing the piles of the matted and burning Creep can cause trapped Etherotaxia to spring free in an angered and panicked state._

title: Creep Infestation
content:
*The gnarled dark undergrowth of Forestdown has spread itself into this area, and it carries with it all the dangers of its progenitor*
**Tone**: Otherworldly, psychedelic, quietly threatening
**Tier 1**
**Type**: Hazard
**Difficulty**: 12

**Potential Adversaries**:
- [[Madanikuputukas (Swarm)]]
- [[Kuputuka]]

**Features**
**Distortion Field - Passive**
While in a Creep Infestation, players must make a Presence Roll (13) or move further into the Creep.
_Rescued children reported that they were walking out of the woods for hours, despite wandering the whole time ever deeper into Forestdown._

**Ethereal Presence - Passive**
While in a Creep Infestation, players are unnaturally calm. For every two actions taken in the Creep, they clear 1 Stress.
_The deeper we found the wanderers, the more at peace they seemed with their surroundings despite the horrible changes inflicted upon them._

**Transformative Stab**
Spend a Fear. A target that has cleared 2 Stress while in a Creep Infestation must make a Presence Roll (15) or take 2d6 magical damage and gain a permanent alteration to their body.
_Only after we'd burned out most of the incursion did we find Commander Orillee. Or, what we assumed was her. It wore her uniform, but the carcass resembled no living thing any of the Hospitallers present had ever seen._

title: Pogg's Court
content:
*The Grandmarshal's audience chamber is a long, cold room at the heart of Eskikale Keep. Grandmarshal Pogg sits at the far end upon a chair that is not quite a throne, attended by two manservants who never leave his side. The Grandmarshal speaks, but his words come as though from a great distance — fragmented, circular, addressing people and events that may or may not exist. The manservants translate, interpret, and occasionally answer for him entirely. An elaborate system of protocol has calcified around these interactions: visitors must address the Grandmarshal through the manservants, must not look directly at the Grandmarshal while he is "composing his thoughts," must present gifts in descending order of weight, and must not speak the word "forest" within the chamber. No one remembers who established these rules. Everyone follows them.*
**Tone**: Surreal, oppressive, darkly absurd
**Tier 2**
**Type**: Social
**Difficulty**: 14

**Potential Adversaries**:
- [[Frightened Villagers]]

**Features**
**The Protocol - Passive**
PCs who violate one of the court's arcane rules (speaking out of turn, addressing the Grandmarshal directly, using forbidden words, presenting gifts incorrectly) must immediately make a Presence Reaction Roll (14). On a failure, the manservants declare the audience suspended and the PCs are escorted out. On a success, the manservants allow a single correction. A second violation in the same audience ends it regardless.
_"His Excellency is composing his thoughts. You will wait. You will not look at him while he composes. This is the third rule."_

**The Manservants - Passive**
The two manservants — quiet, efficient, terrified — control all access to the Grandmarshal. Any attempt to bypass them or speak to Pogg directly requires a Presence Roll (15). On a success, Pogg responds with a moment of startling lucidity before retreating again. On a failure, the manservants intervene physically, and the PC marks a Stress.
_"The Grandmarshal has heard your petition and finds it... He says the lake is singing again. He means he will consider it. Please, just — let us handle this."_

**Pogg's Lucidity - Active**
Spend a Fear. The Grandmarshal has a moment of terrible clarity. He looks directly at a PC and speaks coherently about something he should not know — the state of the Creep, a secret one of the Rearcaptains is keeping, or the true nature of something the party has encountered. The moment passes, and the manservants rush to redirect the conversation, visibly shaken.
_"He looked at me. Right at me. And he said, 'It's already inside the walls.' Then he asked his manservant for more tea."_

**Exposure - Reaction**
When a PC succeeds on a Knowledge or Instinct Roll (13) while in the audience chamber, they notice signs of the Grandmarshal's Creep exposure — the faint dark lines beneath his skin, the way his fingers occasionally move independent of his will, the moments when his eyes focus on something no one else can see. This information is valuable but dangerous; the manservants notice the PC noticing and become Hostile toward them for the remainder of the scene.
_"You see it now. The way his hand twitches. The pattern in the veins at his temple. It's not age. It's not illness. You've seen this before, at the edge of the treeline."_

title: The Ergantine Lake
content:
*The crystal clear waters of Ergantine Lake stretch out before you, deceptively calm. Beneath the surface, massive shapes drift in the deep — creatures transformed by decades of proximity to Forestdown's corruption. Some are benign, even beautiful in their strangeness. Others are not. The lake is the lifeblood of Gatehouse, providing fish, trade routes, and drinking water, but it demands respect. The old Tenders say you can tell how deep the Creep has gotten by how strange the fish are running. Lately, the fish have been running very strange indeed.*
**Tone**: Eerie, wondrous, treacherous
**Tier 1**
**Type**: Exploration
**Difficulty**: 12

**Potential Adversaries**:
- [[Skelkandi]]
- [[Quaddadura]]
- [[Creep-Touched Deaconfish]]
- [[Glowfry School]]

**Features**
**Crystal Depths - Passive**
The water of Ergantine Lake is unnaturally clear. PCs can see deep beneath the surface, granting Advantage on Instinct rolls to spot submerged creatures or objects within Close range. However, this clarity works both ways — submerged creatures can see the PCs just as easily.
_"You can see all the way to the bottom. Every stone, every weed, every shadow. That's the problem. The shadows can see you too."_

**Creep Currents - Passive**
Dark tendrils of Creep drift through the water in unpredictable currents. A PC who enters the water must make an Agility Reaction Roll (11) each time they take an action while submerged or mark a Stress from contact with the corruption. A PC who marks 2 Stress this way gains the _Creep-Touched_ condition.
_"The water itself feels wrong against your skin — not cold exactly, but aware."_

**Something Stirs Below - Active**
Spend a Fear. A massive shape passes beneath the party's position — a shadow that blots out the lakebed for a long, terrible moment before moving on. All PCs must make a Presence Reaction Roll (12) or become Frightened, gaining Disadvantage on their next action roll. If the party is on the water, their vessel rocks violently.
_"For a moment the light changes. Something vast moves beneath you, and then it is gone, and the water is still again, and no one speaks."_

**Lakebed Discovery - Active**
A PC who investigates the lakebed (Knowledge or Instinct Roll, 12) discovers something of interest — a sunken Teetle Cart with its cargo intact, an ancient Sallidean Stone carving half-buried in silt, or the remains of a previous expedition. The discovery provides either useful equipment or a clue about the lake's deeper mysteries.
_"Beneath the sediment, your fingers close around something smooth and carved. It is older than the Keep."_

title: The Market
content:
*A sprawling, semi-permanent market crowded along the outer walls of the Eskikale Keep gatehouse. Traders from Reveille hawk Carnithian goods alongside local vendors peddling lake fish, Teetle jerky, and Paarlid carvings. Beneath the legitimate commerce, a somewhat-tolerated black market operates in plain sight — Lanmaoa extracts, unregistered Sallidean weapons, and stranger things whispered about in the gaps between stalls. The Hygiane Order tolerates it because the market is the only thing keeping Gatehouse fed.*
**Tone**: Bustling, opportunistic, underhanded
**Tier 1**
**Type**: Social
**Difficulty**: 11

**Potential Adversaries**:
- [[Frightened Villagers]]
- [[Lanmaoa]]

**Features**
**Open Trade - Passive**
PCs can purchase common supplies and equipment at standard prices. Unique or rare items require a Presence Roll (11) to locate a willing seller. On a failure, the item is available but at double cost.
_"Everything you need is here, friend. Everything you want costs extra."_

**Black Market - Active**
A PC seeking illicit, rare, or restricted goods must make a Presence Roll (13). On a success, they find what they need but attract the attention of one of the market's power brokers, who will expect a favor in return. On a failure, they are noticed by a member of the Hygiane Order and must explain themselves.
_"You didn't get that from me. In fact, you've never seen me. In fact, I was never here. Are we clear?"_

**Pickpocket's Paradise - Reaction**
When a PC rolls with Fear while in the Market, a thief lifts a small item from them — a coinpurse, a trinket, a letter. The PC may make an Instinct Roll (12) to notice the theft immediately. Otherwise, they discover the loss at a dramatically inconvenient moment.
_"The crowd presses in, and for a moment you feel a hand brush past you. Just the crowd. Probably."_

**Rumor Mill - Active**
Spend a Fear. A merchant or local shares a rumor — partially true, partially embellished. The rumor concerns one of: the Grandmarshal's declining health, the Etherements' experiments, strange behavior in the lake, or the growing boldness of Forestdown's edge. The PC must make a Knowledge Roll (11) to separate truth from embellishment.
_"My cousin's husband's sister works the north tower laundry. She says the Etherements haven't sent out their linens in three weeks. Three weeks! What are they doing in there?"_

title: The Mess Hall
content:
*The central hall of Eskikale Keep where the four orders of the Hygiane Order take their meals together. In theory, it is a place of unity. In practice, the long stone tables are divided by invisible but absolute lines of allegiance. The Hospitallers hold the high table by ancient right, the Medicaments cluster near the kitchens where they can slip away unnoticed, the Etherements sit apart in the darkest corner speaking in hushed tones, and the Tenders — newest arrivals, lowest in station — eat wherever there is room.*
**Tone**: Tense, cliquish, passively hostile
**Tier 1**
**Type**: Social
**Difficulty**: 11

**Potential Adversaries**:
- [[Frightened Villagers]]

**Features**
**Pecking Order - Passive**
Any PC who does not belong to one of the four orders has Disadvantage on Presence rolls made to persuade, command, or impress members of the Hygiane Order while in the Mess Hall. A PC who has been formally inducted into an order loses this Disadvantage when interacting with members of their own order only.
_"You don't sit there. You don't talk to them. You certainly don't ask them for anything. Not until you've earned it."_

**Clique Loyalty - Passive**
Successfully gaining the trust or assistance of a member of one order (Presence Roll 11) causes members of a rival order to become Hostile toward the PCs for the remainder of the scene. The rivalries are: Hospitallers oppose Etherements; Medicaments oppose Tenders.
_"You're eating with the bone-readers now? Well. I suppose we know where your loyalties lie."_

**Old Soldiers' Pride - Active**
A Hospitaller veteran loudly challenges a PC's competence or right to be in the Keep. The targeted PC must make a Presence Roll (12) or mark a Stress. On a success, the PC earns grudging respect from the Hospitallers present.
_"Another batch of green recruits. Tell me, have any of you even seen the treeline?"_

**Loose Tongues - Active**
Spend a Fear. A Medicament, deep in the effects of prolonged Lanmaoa exposure, lets slip a dangerous secret — something about the Etherements' experiments, or the Grandmarshal's condition, or the declining tribute from the Varjalune Republic. The information is valuable but fragmentary, and the Medicament denies everything if confronted later.
_"The mushrooms tell me things, you know. They told me what Oramora keeps in those jars. They told me what it said to him."_

title: The Patrol
content:
*The edge of Forestdown is not a line on a map. It is a feeling — the point where the air changes, where the birdsong stops, where the undergrowth takes on that dark, fibrous quality that the Tenders know means the forest is pushing outward. Your patrol route runs along this edge, a well-salted path that has held the boundary for decades. Today, the salt line is gone. In its place, a wall of new growth has erupted overnight — thick, dark, and spreading visibly as you watch. The Creep is blooming.*
**Tone**: Urgent, overwhelming, unprecedented
**Tier 1**
**Type**: Event
**Difficulty**: 12

**Potential Adversaries**:
- [[Bloom Tendrils]]
- [[Bloom Heart]]
- [[Madanikuputukas (Swarm)]]
- [[Creep-Bound Etherotaxic Entity 1]]

**Features**
**Spreading Corruption - Passive**
The Creep Bloom expands the boundary of Forestdown in real time. This is tracked as a Progress Countdown (8). Each time the GM spends a Fear or a GM turn passes without the PCs making progress toward stopping the Bloom, mark a segment. When the countdown completes, the Bloom has expanded far enough to threaten Gatehouse's outer settlements and the scene shifts from containment to retreat.
_"You can see it moving. The dark tendrils creep across the salted earth as though the salt were nothing. This has never happened before."_

**Toxic Ground - Passive**
The newly Bloomed ground is saturated with concentrated Creep. A PC who moves through Bloomed terrain takes 1d4 magical damage. A PC who ends their turn standing on Bloomed terrain must make an Agility Reaction Roll (11) or mark a Stress. This damage and Stress cannot be avoided by any means other than leaving the Bloomed area.
_"The ground is soft underfoot and it clings to your boots. Where you step, small things wriggle in the disturbed soil."_

**Call for Backup - Active**
A PC can attempt to send a runner to Gatehouse for reinforcements — a Medicament burn team. This requires a Presence Roll (12) to convince a fellow Tender to leave the group, or the PC can go themselves. If successful, reinforcements arrive when the Spreading Corruption countdown reaches 6, turning the tide but not before the damage is done.
_"Someone has to go. Someone has to tell them. But that means leaving the rest of us here with... this."_

**Creep Bloom Surge - Active**
Spend 2 Fear. The Bloom accelerates violently. The ground heaves, new tendrils erupt in a wave, and a burst of spores fills the air. All PCs must make an Agility Reaction Roll (12) or be knocked prone and take 1d8+2 magical damage. Mark two segments on the Spreading Corruption countdown.
_"The earth splits open and the forest roars forward. It is not an attack. It is not malice. It is growth — mindless, unstoppable, hungry growth."_

title: The Proving Ground
content:
*A reinforced chamber deep within the destroyed northern tower of Eskikale Keep, rebuilt by the Etherements for a singular purpose: the containment and study of captured Etherotaxia. Iron-banded doors seal from the outside. Sallidean Stone wards line the walls, their carvings old and of uncertain origin. The Etherements use this space to observe how greater Etherotaxia behave under confinement — how they move, react, and eventually expire. Today, the party has been locked inside with one such specimen, either as a test of their capabilities or because Rearcaptain Oramora wanted to see what would happen.*
**Tone**: Claustrophobic, tense, morally ambiguous
**Tier 1**
**Type**: Event
**Difficulty**: 12

**Potential Adversaries**:
- [[Captured Etherotaxic Entity]]

**Features**
**Sealed Chamber - Passive**
The iron-banded doors are sealed from the outside and cannot be forced open by conventional means. The Sallidean Stone wards prevent the Etherotaxic entity from passing through the walls. Breaking the wards requires a Knowledge or Finesse Roll (14) and renders the containment permanently useless — the Etherements will not be pleased.
_"The door shuts behind you with a sound like a coffin lid. Through the observation slit, you see Oramora's single visible eye watching."_

**Observation - Passive**
The Etherements are watching through observation slits in the walls. Any PC action is being cataloged. If the PCs resolve the encounter socially rather than through combat, the Etherements' reaction is one of disturbed fascination rather than approval — this was not the expected outcome.
_"You feel the weight of eyes on you from every angle. They are not watching to help. They are watching to learn."_

**Escalating Containment - Active**
Spend a Fear. The Etherements activate one of the Sallidean Stone wards from outside, causing it to pulse with energy. All creatures in the chamber take 1d6+1 magical damage. The Etherements use this to "encourage" the entity to be more aggressive if the encounter is proceeding too calmly.
_"The wards flare. The entity screams — not with sound, but with something you feel behind your eyes. Oramora's voice comes through the slit: 'Interesting. Again.'"_

**The Choice - Active**
Spend a Fear. A PC notices a mechanism near the door — a release lever, rusted but functional, that can be reached from inside. Using it opens the chamber. The Etherements will attempt to re-seal the door (Agility Reaction Roll 12 to hold it open), and the consequences of releasing the entity into the Keep will unfold from there.
_"There. Behind the grime on the wall. A handle. They built a way out, once. Before Oramora decided the experiments were more important than safety protocols."_

title: PARTY_OVERVIEW_SIDEBAR_SPEC
content:
# Party Overview Sidebar — Visual Design Specification

> **Status:** Design spec — ready for engineering implementation  
> **Target:** `frontend/src/components/campaign/`  
> **Reviewed against:** existing design system in `tailwind.config.ts`, `globals.css`, `CommandCenterCard.tsx`, `CondensedCharacterCard.tsx`, `SlotBar.tsx`, `StatChip.tsx`

---

## 0. Design System Anchors

Pull directly from the already-established tokens. Do not introduce new color values unless specified below.

| Token | Resolved Value | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `bg-slate-900` | `#0f172a` | Tile background (default) |
| `bg-slate-850` | `#151e2d` | Sidebar container background |
| `border-[#577399]/20` | steel-blue 12% | Default tile border |
| `text-[#f7f7ff]` | near-white | Primary value text |
| `text-parchment-500` | `#d4a94a` | Label text / metadata |
| `text-[#b9baa3]` | warm grey | Secondary body text |
| `text-[#DAA520]` | goldenrod | Hope stat, party Hope |
| `text-emerald-400` | `#34d399` | HP healthy fill |
| `text-amber-400` | `#fbbf24` | HP wounded fill |
| `text-[#fe5f55]` | coral-red | HP critical/down fill, Stress danger |
| `text-violet-400` | `#a78bfa` | Stress fill |
| `bg-[#577399]` | steel-blue | Armor fill |
| `font-serif` | `double-pica`, Georgia | Section headings, character name |
| `font-sans` | `Overpass`, Inter | All body / label text |
| `font-display` | `Heavitas` | Fear counter number only |
| `shadow-card` | `0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.55), 0 0 0 1px rgba(74,10,20,.35)` | Tile resting shadow |

---

## 1. Sidebar Container

### Geometry

```
Desktop (≥1024px):
  Width:        280px fixed
  Height:       100vh (sticky, inset-y-0)
  Position:     fixed right-0 top-0  (z-index: 40)
  Collapsed:    width=0, toggle tab protrudes 32px on the left edge

Mobile (<1024px):
  Width:        100vw
  Height:       auto (slides up from bottom as a drawer)
  Max-height:   70vh
  Position:     fixed bottom-0 left-0  (z-index: 40)
  Collapsed:    height=0 (drawer hidden), floating toggle button visible
```

### Visual Treatment

```css
/* Sidebar shell */
background:   #151e2d;                                   /* slate-850 */
border-left:  1px solid rgba(87, 115, 153, 0.25);        /* steel-blue/25 */
box-shadow:   -4px 0 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6),
              -1px 0 0 rgba(74, 10, 20, 0.20);           /* burgundy inner rim */
backdrop-filter: none;                                   /* solid, not frosted */
```

### Internal Layout

```
[Sidebar Header]  — 56px tall, sticky at top of sidebar
  - "Party" label (font-display "Heavitas", 11px, letter-spacing 0.2em, uppercase, parchment-500)
  - Party member count badge ("4 adventurers" — 10px, steel-blue)
  - Close/collapse button — right-aligned (see §7)

[Fear Callout]    — 72px tall (see §5)

[Divider]         — 1px, rgba(87,115,153,0.15)

[Scrollable tile list]
  - overflow-y: auto
  - scrollbar: 4px wide, thumb #334155 (slate-700), track transparent
  - gap between tiles: 8px (gap-2)
  - padding: 8px 10px (px-2.5 py-2)

[Sidebar Footer]  — 40px, optional "View Full Party" link
```

### Open/Close Transition

```
Property:   transform (translateX on desktop, translateY on mobile)
Duration:   280ms
Easing:     cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94)  (ease-out)
```

## 2. Party Member Tile — Full Anatomy

Each tile is a **vertical card**, `rounded-xl` (12px), with a fixed **internal layout of 4 zones**.

### Overall Tile Dimensions

```
Width:        100% of sidebar content area (≈ 260px on desktop)
Height:       auto (min ~130px, max ~160px depending on HP max count)
Padding:      10px 12px (px-3 py-2.5)
Border-radius: 12px
```

### Zone 1 — Portrait + Name Header (top, 40px tall)

```
Layout: flex row, items-center, gap-2

[Portrait]
  Size:   38px × 38px circle (rounded-full)
  Border: 2px solid rgba(87,115,153,0.40)     default
          2px solid rgba(254,95,85,0.70)       when danger ≥ critical
          2px solid rgba(251,191,36,0.70)      when stress = max (stressed-out)
  Object-fit: cover
  Fallback (no portrait): monogram letter
    Background: rgba(87,115,153,0.20)
    Text:       font-sans font-bold 16px text-[#577399]

[Name block]  flex-1, min-w-0
  [Character name]
    Font:        font-serif (double-pica)
    Size:        13px
    Weight:      600
    Color:       #f7f7ff   (healthy)
                 #fe5f55   (down — add line-through)
    truncate:    yes

  [Class · Level]
    Font:        font-sans
    Size:        10px
    Color:       rgba(185,186,163,0.75)   (.sidebar-text-secondary)
    truncate:    yes

[Danger badge]  shrink-0, appears only when danger ≥ critical
  Icon:    ⚠ (Unicode U+26A0, aria-hidden + sr-only label)
  Size:    14px
  Color:   #fe5f55
  Also show: diagonal-striped overlay on portrait (see §4)
```

### Zone 2 — HP Row (segmented pip bar, most prominent tracker)

```
Layout: flex row, items-center, gap-2, mt-2

[Label]
  "HP"
  Font-sans, 9px, uppercase, tracking-widest
  Color: rgba(185,186,163,0.75)
  Width: 18px fixed, shrink-0

[Pip row]   flex-1
  Each pip:
    Height:   8px
    Width:    flex-1 (distributes evenly)
    Radius:   3px (rounded-sm)
    Gap:      2px
    Filled:   bg = DANGER_HP_FILL[dangerState]
    Empty:    border 1px solid rgba(87,115,153,0.25), bg transparent

  Max pips visible: if hpMax > 12, switch to mini segmented bar (1px pips, gap 1px)
  If hpMax > 20, collapse to single progress bar (see §3-HP)

[Numeric]   shrink-0
  "{remaining}/{max}"   — remaining = max - marked
  Font-sans, 10px, font-semibold, tabular-nums
  Color: matches DANGER_HP_FILL color class (emerald/amber/coral)
```

### Zone 3 — Secondary Trackers Row (Stress + Armor, side by side)

```
Layout: flex row, gap-3, mt-1.5

Each sub-tracker:
  Layout: flex row, items-center, gap-1

  [Label] 9px uppercase, tracking-widest, rgba(185,186,163,0.75), width 28px
  [Mini pips] each pip: 6px × 6px circle (rounded-full), gap 2px
    Stress filled:  bg-violet-500
    Stress empty:   border 1px rgba(167,139,250,0.25)
    Armor filled:   bg-[#577399]
    Armor empty:    border 1px rgba(87,115,153,0.25)
  [Count] 9px, tabular-nums, muted
    Stress count color: text-violet-400  (or text-[#fe5f55] when stress = max)
    Armor count color:  text-[#7a9ab5]   (.text-steel-accessible)
```

### Zone 4 — Hope + Evasion Chip Row (bottom, 24px)

```
Layout: flex row, items-center, gap-2, mt-1.5

[Hope chip]
  Layout: flex row, items-center, gap-1
  Icon:   ✦ (U+2726) — 9px, color #DAA520
  Value:  "{current}/{max}"
  Font:   font-sans, 11px, font-semibold, tabular-nums
  Color:  #DAA520
  Pulse animation: "pulse-glow-goldenrod" (already in keyframes) — applied when hope = max

[Evasion chip]
  Layout: rounded-md badge
  Padding: 1px 6px (px-1.5 py-0.5)
  Border: 1px solid rgba(87,115,153,0.30)
  Background: rgba(87,115,153,0.10)
  Content: "EVA {value}"
  Font:   font-sans, 10px, font-semibold, tabular-nums
  Color:  #f7f7ff
```

## 3. Stat-Specific Visualization

### HP — Segmented Pip Row

| Condition | Fill color | Empty color | Numeric color |
|---|---|---|---|
| Healthy (>50% remaining) | `bg-emerald-500` | `rgba(87,115,153,0.25)` | `text-emerald-400` |
| Wounded (25–50% remaining) | `bg-amber-400` | `rgba(251,191,36,0.20)` | `text-amber-400` |
| Critical (<25% remaining) | `bg-[#fe5f55]` | `rgba(254,95,85,0.20)` | `text-[#fe5f55]` |
| Down (0 remaining) | `bg-[#fe5f55]` | `rgba(254,95,85,0.30)` | `text-[#fe5f55]` |

**Overflow rule:** If `hpMax > 20`, replace pip row with a CSS progress bar:
```
Height: 6px, rounded-full
Background track: rgba(87,115,153,0.15)
Fill: linear-gradient based on danger state
Transition: width 400ms ease-out
```

### Stress — Circle Pips

- Pip diameter: **6px circle** (rounded-full)
- Filled: `bg-violet-500`
- Empty: `border-1px border-violet-500/25 bg-transparent`
- **Danger trigger:** When `stress.marked === stress.max`:
  - All pips: `bg-[#fe5f55]` + `border-[#fe5f55]`
  - Label text: `text-[#fe5f55]`
  - Tile gets stress-maxed compound state (see §4)

### Armor — Circle Pips

- Pip diameter: **6px circle** (rounded-full)
- Filled: `bg-[#577399]`
- Empty: `border-1px border-[#577399]/25`
- No danger state for armor (depleted armor is expected)
- When `armor.max === 0`: hide entire armor row, do not render empty pips

### Hope — Icon + Fraction

- Render as `✦ {current}/{max}` inline, goldenrod (#DAA520)
- When `hope === 0`: color shifts to `rgba(218,165,32,0.35)` (muted)
- When `hope === max`: apply `animate-pulse-glow-goldenrod` (already defined)
- Max is always 6 per SRD; render 6 small pip dots above the number for a quick visual scan:

```
Optional secondary render: 6 mini pips (5px circles), filled goldenrod, empty transparent/border
Position: just above the ✦ numeric, in the Zone 4 row
Space budget: use only when sidebar is expanded to ≥300px width
```

### Evasion — Stat Badge Chip

- Numeric only, no pips
- Render as `[EVA 14]` badge
- Border, background: steel-blue tint (see Zone 4 above)
- No dynamic state changes (Evasion is static)

---

## 4. Danger States

All danger states must communicate through **three simultaneous channels** (color + shape + texture) for accessibility.

### State Table

| State | Trigger | Border | Tile BG tint | Portrait ring | Icon | Text style |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `healthy` | HP > 50% remaining, stress < max | `rgba(87,115,153,0.20)` 1px | none | steel-blue 40% | — | Normal |
| `wounded` | HP 25–50% remaining | `rgba(251,191,36,0.40)` 1px | none | amber 40% | ⚠ sr-only only | Normal |
| `critical` | HP < 25% remaining | `rgba(254,95,85,0.55)` 1px + `animate-coral-pulse` | `rgba(254,95,85,0.04)` | coral-red 70% | ⚠ visible (#fe5f55) | Name stays white |
| `down` | HP = 0 | `rgba(254,95,85,0.70)` 2px | `rgba(254,95,85,0.07)` | coral-red 70%, dashed | ⚠ visible + skull pattern | Name: line-through, `text-[#fe5f55]/70` |
| `stressed-out` | Stress = max (compound with any HP state) | Adds violet inner shadow | light violet wash `rgba(167,139,250,0.04)` | Adds violet outer ring (secondary) | 🌀 sr-only label "Stressed out" | Stress label turns coral |

### `critical` — Pulse Animation

```css
@keyframes party-tile-danger-pulse {
  0%, 100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(254,95,85,0); }
  50%       { box-shadow: 0 0 10px 2px rgba(254,95,85,0.25),
                          0 0  4px 0   rgba(254,95,85,0.15); }
}
/* Duration: 2s, timing: ease-in-out, iteration: infinite */
/* Suppress under prefers-reduced-motion — keep static border change only */
```

### `down` — Portrait Overlay (shape/pattern channel)

```
Render a diagonal-stripes SVG mask over the portrait (CSS background-image).
Pattern: repeating-linear-gradient(
  -45deg,
  rgba(254,95,85,0.35) 0px,
  rgba(254,95,85,0.35) 2px,
  transparent 2px,
  transparent 8px
)
Border: 2px dashed rgba(254,95,85,0.70)
```

### `stressed-out` — Texture Channel

When stress is maxed, add a vertical left-edge accent bar to the tile:
```
Position: absolute left-0 top-0 bottom-0 w-[3px]
Background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(167,139,250,0.7), rgba(167,139,250,0.2))
Border-radius: 12px 0 0 12px
aria-hidden: true
```

## 5. GM Fear Display

The Fear Callout lives **at the top of the sidebar**, just below the header bar, **above the tile list**. It is always visible — it does not scroll with the tile list.

### Visual Identity

The Fear section must look **categorically different** from player tiles — it belongs to the GM/world layer, not the party layer.

```
Layout:       Horizontal callout strip — full sidebar width, 68px tall
Background:   linear-gradient(135deg,
                rgba(74,10,20,0.55) 0%,      /* burgundy-900 dark */
                rgba(21,30,45,0.90) 100%)     /* slate-850 */
Border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(74,10,20,0.50)
Border-top:   none (flush with sidebar header border-bottom)
Padding:      10px 16px (px-4 py-2.5)
```

### Internal Layout

```
[Left section]  flex-col, justify-center
  [Eyebrow label]
    "GM · FEAR"
    Font: font-display "Heavitas", 9px, letter-spacing: 0.22em, uppercase
    Color: rgba(203,45,86,0.70)   (burgundy-600 at 70%)

  [Flavor text]
    "The world watches…" — italic, 9px, rgba(185,186,163,0.45)
    Only shown when fear > 0; hidden at fear = 0

[Right section]  flex-row, items-center, gap-3
  [Fear pips]   — 12 small diamond shapes (◆ U+25C6)
    Each diamond: 10px × 10px, rendered via SVG or CSS transform: rotate(45deg) on square
    Filled:   background #8e1e3e  (burgundy-800), border 1px solid #cb2d56 (burgundy-600)
    Empty:    background transparent, border 1px solid rgba(142,30,62,0.30)
    Gap:      3px
    Arrange in 2 rows of 6 (grid 6×2) or 1 row of 12 depending on sidebar width
    
    When fear ≥ 9:  filled pips switch to bg-[#cb2d56], add `animate-coral-pulse` (re-using existing keyframe)
    When fear = 12: all 12 filled + border becomes solid #e0486e (burgundy-500) pulsing

  [Fear counter]
    "{fear}"
    Font:   font-display "Heavitas", 28px
    Color:  #e0486e  (burgundy-500)  — healthy
            #cb2d56  (burgundy-600)  — fear ≥ 6
            #aa2047  (burgundy-700)  — fear ≥ 9 + animate-pulse-glow
    Line-height: 1
    Min-width: 28px, text-align: right
```

### Fear Accessibility

- The Fear counter must have `aria-label="GM Fear: {n} of 12"`
- The pip grid must have `role="img" aria-label="Fear tracker: {n} of 12 filled"`
- When fear changes, the counter element must have `aria-live="polite"`

---

## 6. Real-Time Update Animations

All animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` — fall back to instant swap.

### Value Change — Number Pop

When any numeric value changes (HP remaining, Stress, Hope, Fear):

```css
@keyframes party-stat-pop {
  0%   { transform: scale(1);    opacity: 1; }
  25%  { transform: scale(1.25); opacity: 1; }
  100% { transform: scale(1);    opacity: 1; }
}
/* Duration: 250ms, timing: cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) (spring bounce) */
/* Apply: animate-party-stat-pop class toggled via JS, removed after 300ms */
```

### HP Decrease — Pip Flash

When an HP pip transitions from filled → empty (damage taken):

```css
@keyframes party-pip-clear {
  0%   { background-color: var(--pip-fill-color); opacity: 1; }
  30%  { background-color: rgba(254,95,85,0.8);  opacity: 1; }
  100% { background-color: transparent;           opacity: 1; }
}
/* Duration: 400ms, ease-out */
/* Only on the specific pip(s) being cleared */
```

### HP Increase (healing) — Pip Fill

When an HP pip transitions from empty → filled:

```css
@keyframes party-pip-fill {
  0%   { background-color: transparent; transform: scale(0.6); }
  60%  { background-color: var(--pip-fill-color); transform: scale(1.15); }
  100% { background-color: var(--pip-fill-color); transform: scale(1); }
}
/* Duration: 350ms, ease-out */
```

### Danger State Transition — Tile Border

When a tile transitions between danger states:
```
Transition: border-color 600ms ease-in-out, box-shadow 600ms ease-in-out
No jump — always cross-fades smoothly.
```

### Fear Counter Change

```css
@keyframes fear-count-flip {
  0%   { transform: translateY(0);    opacity: 1; }
  30%  { transform: translateY(-8px); opacity: 0; }
  31%  { transform: translateY(8px);  opacity: 0; }
  100% { transform: translateY(0);    opacity: 1; }
}
/* Duration: 300ms, ease-in-out */
/* Applied to the fear number span only */
```

### Tile Entry (new member joins)

```css
/* Reuse existing animate-fade-in (already defined in tailwind.config.ts) */
animation: fade-in 0.2s ease-out;
/* Plus: */
@keyframes party-tile-enter {
  from { transform: translateX(20px); opacity: 0; }
  to   { transform: translateX(0);    opacity: 1; }
}
/* Duration: 250ms, ease-out */
```

## 9. Responsive Behavior

### Desktop (≥1024px) — Primary Target

```
Sidebar:        Fixed right column, 280px, full-height
Main content:   Gets padding-right: 280px when sidebar is open
                Transition: padding-right 280ms ease-out (no layout jump)
Tiles:          Single column, 100% sidebar width
Fear callout:   Top of sidebar, full width strip
Toggle:         Left-edge tab (see §7 Desktop)
```

### Tablet (768px–1023px)

```
Sidebar:        Overlay mode (no main content push)
                Slides in from right, 280px wide
                Semi-transparent backdrop: rgba(10,16,13,0.70) behind sidebar
Toggle:         Same left-edge tab as desktop
Tiles:          Single column
```

### Mobile (<768px)

```
Sidebar:        Bottom drawer, slides up
                100vw wide, max-height: 72vh
                Background: #151e2d
                Top rounded: border-radius 16px 16px 0 0
                Drag handle: 36px×4px pill, rgba(87,115,153,0.30), centered at top, 8px margin

Fear callout:   Full-width strip at top of drawer (same design, condensed to 56px)
Tiles:          Horizontal scroll row OR vertical list (toggle via preference)
                Default: vertical list (same as desktop but narrower)
                Tile width: 100% of drawer width
Toggle:         FAB button bottom-right (see §7 Mobile)
Backdrop:       rgba(10,16,13,0.70) on open
```

## 10. Component Sketch — Pseudocode Structure

```tsx
<PartySidebar isOpen={bool} onToggle={fn}>

  {/* Sticky header */}
  <SidebarHeader memberCount={n} onClose={fn} />

  {/* Fear callout — always visible, non-scrolling */}
  <FearCallout fear={currentFear} maxFear={12} />

  {/* Divider */}
  <hr className="border-[#577399]/15" />

  {/* Scrollable tile list */}
  <div className="overflow-y-auto flex-1 space-y-2 px-2.5 py-2">
    {partyMembers.map(member => (
      <PartyMemberTile
        key={member.characterId}
        character={member}         /* live data from useCharacter hook */
        dangerState={computed}
        isStressedOut={computed}
        onAnimateStatChange={fn}   /* triggers pop animation */
      />
    ))}
  </div>

  {/* Optional footer */}
  <SidebarFooter campaignId={id} />

</PartySidebar>

<PartyToggleButton
  isOpen={bool}
  onToggle={fn}
  dangerCount={criticalOrDownCount}
/>
```

## 12. New CSS / Keyframes Required

Add these to `globals.css` alongside existing animations:

```css
/* ─── Party Overview Sidebar ──────────────────────────────────────────────── */

@keyframes party-stat-pop {
  0%   { transform: scale(1); }
  25%  { transform: scale(1.28); }
  100% { transform: scale(1); }
}
.animate-party-stat-pop {
  animation: party-stat-pop 250ms cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1);
}

@keyframes party-pip-clear {
  0%   { background-color: var(--pip-fill); }
  30%  { background-color: rgba(254,95,85,0.8); }
  100% { background-color: transparent; }
}
.animate-party-pip-clear {
  animation: party-pip-clear 400ms ease-out forwards;
}

@keyframes party-pip-fill {
  0%   { transform: scale(0.6); opacity: 0.5; }
  60%  { transform: scale(1.15); opacity: 1; }
  100% { transform: scale(1); opacity: 1; }
}
.animate-party-pip-fill {
  animation: party-pip-fill 350ms ease-out forwards;
}

@keyframes party-tile-danger-pulse {
  0%, 100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(254,95,85,0); }
  50%       { box-shadow: 0 0 10px 2px rgba(254,95,85,0.25),
                          0 0  4px 0   rgba(254,95,85,0.12); }
}
.animate-party-tile-danger {
  animation: party-tile-danger-pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite;
}

@keyframes fear-count-flip {
  0%   { transform: translateY(0);    opacity: 1; }
  30%  { transform: translateY(-8px); opacity: 0; }
  31%  { transform: translateY(8px);  opacity: 0; }
  100% { transform: translateY(0);    opacity: 1; }
}
.animate-fear-flip {
  animation: fear-count-flip 300ms ease-in-out;
}

@keyframes party-tile-enter {
  from { transform: translateX(16px); opacity: 0; }
  to   { transform: translateX(0);    opacity: 1; }
}
.animate-party-tile-enter {
  animation: party-tile-enter 250ms ease-out;
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .animate-party-stat-pop,
  .animate-party-pip-clear,
  .animate-party-pip-fill,
  .animate-party-tile-danger,
  .animate-fear-flip,
  .animate-party-tile-enter {
    animation: none;
  }
}
```

---

## 13. Tailwind Config Additions

Add to `tailwind.config.ts` → `theme.extend`:

```ts
// In keyframes:
"party-stat-pop": {
  "0%":   { transform: "scale(1)" },
  "25%":  { transform: "scale(1.28)" },
  "100%": { transform: "scale(1)" },
},
"party-tile-danger-pulse": {
  "0%, 100%": { boxShadow: "0 0 0 0 rgba(254,95,85,0)" },
  "50%":      { boxShadow: "0 0 10px 2px rgba(254,95,85,0.25), 0 0 4px 0 rgba(254,95,85,0.12)" },
},
"fear-count-flip": {
  "0%":   { transform: "translateY(0)",   opacity: "1" },
  "30%":  { transform: "translateY(-8px)",opacity: "0" },
  "31%":  { transform: "translateY(8px)", opacity: "0" },
  "100%": { transform: "translateY(0)",   opacity: "1" },
},
"party-tile-enter": {
  from: { transform: "translateX(16px)", opacity: "0" },
  to:   { transform: "translateX(0)",    opacity: "1" },
},

// In animation:
"party-stat-pop":         "party-stat-pop 250ms cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)",
"party-tile-danger":      "party-tile-danger-pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite",
"fear-flip":              "fear-count-flip 300ms ease-in-out",
"party-tile-enter":       "party-tile-enter 250ms ease-out",

// In boxShadow:
"glow-fear":  "0 0 14px rgba(142,30,62,0.50), 0 0 4px rgba(142,30,62,0.30)",
"party-tile": "0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.45), 0 0 0 1px rgba(74,10,20,0.20)",
```

---

## 14. Accessibility Checklist

| Requirement | Implementation |
|---|---|
| All animations suppressible | `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` block in §12 |
| Color not sole indicator | Danger states use border style (solid/dashed), icon (⚠), and portrait overlay pattern in addition to color |
| Stress maxed: not color-only | Left-edge accent bar (shape) + icon sr-only label + violet pips |
| Fear counter: screen reader | `aria-live="polite"` + explicit `aria-label` |
| Tile focus ring | `focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#577399]` (2px, #577399, offset 2) |
| Toggle button: keyboard operable | Standard `<button>` with `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls` |
| Portrait alt text | `alt=""` (decorative) — name already in tile |
| Min touch target | All interactive elements ≥ 44px × 44px (WCAG 2.5.5) |
| Contrast — tile labels | `rgba(185,186,163,0.75)` = 4.65:1 on slate-900 → passes AA |
| Contrast — stat values | `#f7f7ff` on slate-900 > 12:1 → passes AAA |
| Contrast — Fear counter | `#e0486e` on `#151e2d` ≈ 4.8:1 → passes AA |
| Danger icon sr text | `<span aria-hidden="true">⚠</span><span className="sr-only">Danger: {state}</span>` |
| Sidebar role | `role="complementary" aria-label="Party overview"` |
| Tile region | `role="article" aria-label="{name} — party vitals"` |

title: Adherent
content: A member of a [[Faction]]. It is possible for an Adherent to be a member of multiple factions at once. In the event that you are dealing with an adherent of multiple factions, add the result of all factions' [[Reputation]] score when calculating [[Attitude]].

title: Attitude
content:
The baseline demeanor of an [[Adherent]] towards the party or a [[Player]].

**+6**: Will sacrifice their wellbeing and life for your goals.
**+5**: Will go out of their way to aid you in any way possible.
**+4**: Willingly offers shelter, equipment and sanctuary.
***
**+3**: Treats the party as an ally, and will defend them fervently.
**+2**: Treats the party in a friendly manner, and will offer nominal aid and assistance.
**+1**: Treats the party as trustworthy, and will listen to arguments and appeals.
***
**0**: Neutral
***
**-1**: Treats the party as untrustworthy, and will refuse basic arguments and appeals.
**-2**: Treats the party as an adversary, and sees their continued existence as a threat.
**-3**: Treats the party as a sworn enemy, and will seek to eliminate the party as a [[Primary Goals|Primary Goal]].
***
**-4**: Will harm others if it harms the party directly.
**-5**: Will harm others if it harms the party indirectly.
**-6**: Will sacrifice their wellbeing and life in order to harm the party.

title: Aura
content: An Aura is a spell that can be turned on by the player. When active, an Aura has specific effects within a given range that will occur at the end of the turn for the player holding the aura. When a player with an active aura is hit, they must succeed at a Spellcast Roll with the Maintenance Difficulty listed on the domain card.

title: Character
content: Any sentient [[Creature]] capable of language and advanced reasoning.

title: Creature
content: Any living being on Tidwell incapable of language and/or advanced reasoning.

title: Curse
content: A drawback on starred domain cards (★) which carries a roleplay or mechanical effect on how your character is played.

title: Faction
content:
A group within Tidwell who share a common goal, belief, or custom which binds them together. Most [[Character|Characters]] and some [[Creature|Creatures]] are aligned with a faction. Factions vary greatly in size, but will always have enough constituents to act as a group and be bound by the faction's [[Primary Goals]]

An individual member of a faction are referred to as [[Adherent]]s.

Some examples of a Faction include the [[Hygiane Order]] and the [[Davites]]. Rarely, a faction may be destroyed via actions or circumstances in the story.

title: Favor
content: If you have a Favor, you can make a reasonable request of the faction or any [[Adherent]] of the faction.
